title: No fake data: first-load + outage show shimmer skeleton and retry, never invented values
description:
Hard rule (Calum): tiles must NEVER show fake/hardcoded/placeholder values - not on first load, not on outage. On both INITIAL load and any downstream API failure a tile shows a shimmering skeleton and keeps retrying until real data arrives. Aggressive refresh is fine (LAN).

API layer - services swallow errors and return hardcoded constants, so tRPC returns 200 with fake data and the client can't detect an outage. Remove these catch-returns; let errors propagate:
- apps/api/src/services/weather-service.ts:139-141 (PLACEHOLDER_NOW), :168-171 (PLACEHOLDER_HOURLY)
- apps/api/src/services/tesla-service.ts:71,84 (TESLA_PLACEHOLDER)
- apps/api/src/services/controls-service.ts:115-130 (FALLBACK)
- apps/api/src/services/climate-service.ts:33,59 (FALLBACK)
- apps/api/src/services/network-service.ts:116-130 (makeFallback)
- apps/api/src/services/events-service.ts:63-66 (PLACEHOLDER_EVENTS)

Frontend layer - tiles do data ?? FALLBACK, rendering fake numbers on first mount AND on error:
- ControlsTile.tsx:87, Next12Hours.tsx:45, EventsTile.tsx:104, TeslaTile.tsx:275, ClimateTile.tsx:36, NetworkTile.tsx:67-74.

Fix plan:
1. Each API service throws on error/unconfigured instead of returning fake data.
2. Configure QueryClient in apps/web/src/lib/trpc.ts: defaultOptions.queries = { retry: true, retryDelay: ~5000, refetchOnWindowFocus: false }. Infinite retry for the wall panel.
3. Every tile: if (!data) return <tile skeleton>  (covers first load AND error). No data ?? FALLBACK.
4. Delete ALL FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER constant blocks (api + web).
5. Use the shared Skeleton primitive for every tile's loading layout.

Reference: evee/apps/web/src/hooks/use-auto-reload.ts, evee/apps/web/src/components/placeholder-tile.tsx. Per-tile follow-ups are tracked separately and depend on this + the primitives epic.
acceptance_criteria: On first load every tile shows an animated shimmer skeleton until real data arrives (no fake values flash in). On any outage (HA/UniFi/weather/DB down) every affected tile reverts to shimmer skeleton and keeps retrying. No FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER constants anywhere (api or web). API services throw on error. Manual test: disconnect HA and reload - zero invented numbers visible.

title: Keep merge queue alive when result signal target is gone
description: The merge queue can poll ticket-verified Beads tickets whose child ticket workflow has already completed or disappeared. After a successful merge, close, and Beads update, signaling that missing child should not fail the long-lived merge queue.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Merge queue ignores missing/already-completed child workflow signal failures after recording a merge result.
- [ ] Other merge processing still records the result and clears the active queue entry.
- [ ] Tests cover a missing child result signal without failing processNextMergeQueueEntry.
- [ ] Project Management tests and typecheck pass.

title: Allow claimed verified tickets in merge queue
description: Merge queue polling only queried open ticket-verified issues, but ticket workflows claim tickets before build/review, leaving reviewed tickets in_progress. This prevented verified workflow-owned tickets like www-qkiv from being repolled after a blocked merge attempt.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Verified merge queue queries include open and in_progress ticket-verified issues.
- [ ] Human-labeled or closed verified tickets remain excluded.
- [ ] Tests cover in_progress verified tickets reaching merge candidates.
- [ ] Project Management tests and typecheck pass.

title: Treat verified builder handoff as agent completion
description: Ticket builder can finish the required work, push the branch, and leave the Builder summary while opencode run remains alive waiting for a final model step. The workflow currently waits only for tmux exit, which wedges completed builder attempts.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Builder wait can complete successfully when the builder handoff is already verified, even if the tmux/opencode process is still alive.
- [ ] The workflow terminates only the exact ticket builder tmux session after verified handoff to avoid leaked processes.
- [ ] Tests cover builder handoff short-circuit before reviewer start.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run test exits 0.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run typecheck exits 0.
close_reason: Shipped in d3e6693c: builder wait now completes after verified handoff and kills the exact builder tmux session.

title: Recreate stale ticket worktrees before agent retries
description: Ticket workflow retries can run from worktrees created before agent/config fixes, so OpenCode keeps loading stale .opencode agent files and exhausted models. The workflow should avoid reusing stale ticket worktrees when starting a fresh workflow attempt.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Ticket workflow code prevents stale existing ticket worktrees from launching agents with old .opencode config.
- [ ] Tests cover recreating or rejecting a stale worktree before builder start.
- [ ] Existing dirty stale worktrees do not cause silent old-model retries.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run test exits 0.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run typecheck exits 0.
close_reason: Shipped in 6aa466e7: ticket worktree creation now removes exact stale worktree and branch before recreating from origin/main.

title: Stop ticket builder using exhausted OpenCode model
description: Ticket workflow builder runs are currently using openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark. That model is returning usage-limit errors in OpenCode logs, while opencode run keeps retrying with empty stdout/stderr, making builder attempts look like silent hangs. Move ticket-builder to an available model and keep workflow metadata/tests aligned so queued tickets can progress.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] .opencode ticket-builder agent no longer uses openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark.
- [ ] Project-management TICKET_BUILDER_MODEL matches the ticket-builder agent model.
- [ ] Tests asserting ticket builder model/command are updated.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run test exits 0.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run typecheck exits 0.
- [ ] A real queued ticket builder run starts producing output or exits cleanly instead of silently retrying usage-limit errors.
close_reason: Shipped in df731ab9: ticket-builder now uses openai/gpt-5.5-fast and config/tests were updated.

title: Move builder ticket-review transition into workflow
description: Ticket builder currently owns the Beads lifecycle transition from ticket-ready to ticket-review via prompt instructions, while the workflow also has transition activities. Move this state-machine transition into the Temporal workflow so builder agents only implement, commit, push, and leave a structured handoff. The workflow should mechanically verify the builder handoff and then move the ticket to review, reducing contradictory states like builder-passed plus ticket-human.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Ticket-builder prompt no longer instructs the agent to move Beads lifecycle labels.
- [ ] Builder prompt explicitly says the agent must not move ticket-ready/ticket-review/ticket-human lifecycle labels.
- [ ] Temporal workflow moves a successful, verified builder handoff to ticket-review using workflow code, not prompt compliance.
- [ ] Builder success validation checks the required handoff evidence before transitioning to review.
- [ ] Tests cover that successful builder handoff triggers ticket-review transition and that the builder prompt does not contain the lifecycle update command.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run test exits 0.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run typecheck exits 0.
close_reason: Shipped to main in 76523d59. Builder prompt no longer moves lifecycle labels; workflow verifies builder handoff and moves successful builds to ticket-review. Verified with products/project-management tests, typecheck, changed-file biome, and pre-push hooks.

title: Remove ticket agent step caps and emit OpenCode JSON logs
close_reason: Removed explicit ticket agent step caps and switched automated OpenCode runs to JSON output

title: Handle origin/main advancement in merge queue
description: Merge queue push can race with CI badge commits or other writers advancing origin/main after the initial pull. Add bounded sync/replay/re-gate/retry behavior so safe remote advancement is handled without force-push.

title: Fix merge queue completed dedupe
close_reason: Fixed merge queue dedupe by request id and renamed workflow id to merge_queue

title: Route merge gate failures back to builder
description: Why this exists: ticket workflow merge queue failures currently retry inside the merge queue and escalate to human instead of returning deterministic failures to the builder. What needs to be done: centralize validation gates, prepare ticket worktrees before builder runs, validate builder output before review, route merge/final gate failures back through the builder loop, and keep reviewer focused on acceptance review.
close_reason: Implemented shared gate, worktree prep, post-builder validation, and merge queue retry routing in cbec0877.

title: Add Temporal recovery workflow for stuck ticket runs
description: Temporal ticket runs can be lost or drift after local Temporal restarts, DB corruption, or interrupted automation while Beads tickets remain in workflow-owned labels/phases. Add a project-management Temporal recovery workflow that is bootstrapped beside the existing ticket and merge queue workflows, polls for stranded ticket runs, clears exact ticket-scoped resources, and moves the ticket back to ticket-ready so the normal ticket_queue workflow can restart it.
design: Model this after products/project-management/temporal/workflows.ts ticketQueueWorkflow: an always-on Temporal workflow started from queue-bootstrap.ts with USE_EXISTING conflict policy, stable workflow id, activity-based I/O, poll/sleep loop, and bounded batch size. Proposed workflow id: stuck_ticket_recovery. It must not live in Control Center worker and must not be a Kubernetes CronJob. Recovery should not directly start builder/reviewer; it resets stuck tickets and lets ticket_queue own fresh ticket_${ticketId} child startup. Reuse products/project-management/ticket-artifact-cleanup.ts rather than duplicating cleanup logic. Default to keeping evidence logs unless the cleanup planner already has an explicit safe remove-evidence path.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] queue-bootstrap.ts starts a persistent Temporal recovery workflow with workflow id stuck_ticket_recovery and USE_EXISTING conflict policy alongside ticket_queue and ticket_merge_queue.
- [ ] Recovery workflow mirrors ticketQueueWorkflow polling style: activity-based I/O, configurable poll interval, max tickets per poll, sleep between polls, and no direct shell/git/tmux I/O inside workflow code.
- [ ] Recovery detects tickets in workflow-owned labels/phases whose expected ticket workflow id ticket_${ticketId} is absent, terminated, completed unexpectedly, or otherwise not running.
- [ ] Recovery skips tickets with live ticket_${ticketId} executions.
- [ ] Recovery reuses ticket-artifact-cleanup.ts to plan and execute exact ticket-scoped cleanup for tmux sessions, Temporal/manual worktrees, local branches, and pushed remote branches where safe.
- [ ] Recovery never deletes unrelated .beads state, Temporal DB volumes, non-matching tmux sessions, non-matching worktrees, non-matching branches, or evidence logs by default.
- [ ] Recovery moves recovered tickets back to ticket-ready, removes workflow-stage labels, marks retry/recovery metadata, and records cleanup results so ticket_queue can pick them up normally.
- [ ] Tests cover polling batch behavior, live workflow skip, dead/missing workflow recovery, missing artifact tolerance, cleanup failure handling, Beads metadata updates, and bootstrap start behavior.
- [ ] Gates: bun run test, bun run typecheck, and bunx biome check . green.
- [ ] No fake data guard stays green.
- [ ] Committed as feat(workflows/<ticket-id>): add stuck ticket recovery workflow; merged to main; bd closed.

## Reviewer findings

Changes needed.

Acceptance evidence:
- Verified `queue-bootstrap.ts` starts `ticket_queue`, `ticket_merge_queue`, and `stuck_ticket_recovery` with `WorkflowIdConflictPolicy.USE_EXISTING` and stable workflow id `stuck_ticket_recovery`.

Findings:
1. Exact gate AC is not satisfied. `bunx biome check .` in this worktree processed 0 files and failed with “No files were processed in the specified paths.” The ticket AC requires `bunx biome check .` green.
2. Cleanup failure handling is unsafe. `recoverStuckTicket()` executes cleanup actions, but then always updates Beads to add `ticket-ready`/`ticket-retry` and remove workflow labels even when one or more cleanup actions failed. That can let `ticket_queue` pick up a ticket while a stale worktree, branch, remote branch, or tmux session still exists. This does not satisfy safe recovery/cleanup failure handling.
3. Branch cleanup is not constrained to ticket-matching branches when `ticket_branch` metadata is present. `isTicketWorkflowBranch()` returns true for any `expectedBranchName` except `main`/`master`, even if it does not start with `${ticketId}-`, so corrupted/stale metadata can delete a non-matching local or remote branch. This violates the AC that recovery never deletes non-matching branches.

title: Implement serialized merge queue workflow
description: Replace per-ticket inline merge/finalize behavior with a real long-lived Temporal workflow named ticket_merge_queue. ticketWorkflow should signal verified tickets into that merge queue and wait for a ticketMergeResult signal. The merge queue owns all serialized main mutations, final gates, main push, Beads cleanup, bd close, and bd dolt push.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Red test first: ticketWorkflow reviewer-verified path signals ticket_merge_queue and does not call merge activities inline.
- [ ] Red test first: merge queue processes two verified requests strictly serially and returns one result per ticket workflow.
- [ ] Red test first: dirty local main becomes human-blocked before fetch, merge, gates, push, or close.
- [ ] Red test first: remote moved before push retries from update-main without force-pushing.
- [ ] Red test first: worker startup enqueues existing open ticket-verified tickets and ignores closed shipped or ticket-human tickets.
- [ ] Define typed MergeQueueWorkflowInput, MergeQueueRequest, MergeQueueResult discriminated union, MergeQueueStep registry, MergeQueueEntry/Snapshot, enqueue/cancel/result signals, and snapshot query.
- [ ] Worker startup idempotently ensures workflow id ticket_merge_queue with WorkflowIdConflictPolicy.USE_EXISTING.
- [ ] ticketWorkflow enqueues MergeQueueRequest after reviewer verification and waits for ticketMergeResult before completing.
- [ ] ticketWorkflow handles merged, retryable-failure, and human-blocked results explicitly with exhaustive switches.
- [ ] Merge queue deduplicates by ticketId/requestId when queued, active, or completed.
- [ ] Merge queue owns assert-clean-main, update-main, merge-ticket-branch, final-gates, push-main, shipped metadata/label cleanup, bd close, bd dolt push, and result signaling.
- [ ] Merge queue never force-pushes main and never stashes/resets anonymous local changes.
- [ ] Merge queue retries bounded merge-safe failures with default maxMergeAttempts=3, then escalates to ticket-human with a Beads comment/reason.
- [ ] Main-pushed but Beads-finalization failure is handled as human-blocked/recoverable finalization failure without rerunning git merge.
- [ ] Existing runSerializedMergeWorkflow tests are preserved or migrated to the queue-owned request processor.
- [ ] Unit tests cover FIFO enqueue, duplicate request suppression, cancel queued request, snapshot query, continue-as-new/history compaction trigger, startup ensure, existing verified reconciliation, ticket result signaling, dirty main, local ahead/diverged main, remote moved before push, merge conflict, final-gate failure, Beads close failure, and Beads push failure.
- [ ] Gates: bun run --filter project-management test + typecheck + relevant biome check green.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
## Reviewer findings

Changes needed.

Evidence reviewed:
- `bd show www-kjbr` acceptance criteria and existing builder comment.
- Builder commit delta only: `git show --stat --oneline HEAD` shows commit `926fda7e feat(project-management/www-kjbr): add serialized merge queue`, 7 changed files. `git diff HEAD^..HEAD` reviewed.
- Read changed implementation and tests in `products/project-management/beads-adapter.ts`, `temporal/command-activities.ts`, `temporal/workflows.ts`, `temporal/queue-bootstrap.ts`, and related tests.
- Ran gates: `bun run --filter project-management test` passed, 14 files, 92 tests. `bun run --filter project-management typecheck` passed. `bunx biome check <7 changed files>` passed. `scripts/check-fake-data.sh` passed.

Findings:
1. Merge-safe failures are not actually retried up to `maxMergeAttempts`. In `processMergeQueueRequest`, a merge failure or final-gate failure returns `retryable-failure` immediately on the first attempt when `attempt < maxAttempts` instead of continuing/retrying inside the merge queue. The queue then records that result as completed and signals the ticket workflow. This violates the AC requiring bounded retries with default `maxMergeAttempts=3`, then escalation to `ticket-human` with a Beads comment/reason.
2. Startup reconciliation does not work when `ticket_merge_queue` already exists. `ensureTicketQueueWorkflowWithClient` only starts `mergeQueueWorkflow` with `WorkflowIdConflictPolicy.USE_EXISTING`. Existing verified tickets are read only inside `mergeQueueWorkflow` at workflow execution start. If the workflow is already running, worker startup does not query/signal existing open `ticket-verified` tickets, so it does not satisfy the AC for worker startup discovery/reconciliation without duplicate merges.
3. Required merge queue behavior is under-tested. There is no test exercising the actual Temporal `mergeQueueWorkflow` signal/query loop, FIFO processing through `enqueueMerge`, duplicate suppression while queued/active/completed, `cancelMerge`, `mergeQueueSnapshot`, continue-as-new compaction, result signaling to a waiting ticket workflow, or existing verified-ticket reconciliation on an already-running queue. Current tests mostly cover `processMergeQueueRequest` as a helper and queue bootstrap start options, so multiple ticket AC items remain unverified.
4. Human escalation coverage is incomplete for queue-owned failures. Tests cover dirty-main and close-ticket human-block result shape, but not escalation activity/comment invocation after bounded merge/final-gate retries, local ahead/diverged main, merge conflict retry/escalation, final-gate retry/escalation, Beads close failure, or Beads push failure as required by AC.

Acceptance evidence:
- Satisfied: typed merge queue request/result/signal/query types exist; worker starts `ticket_merge_queue` with USE_EXISTING; ticket runner can enqueue a verified ticket and wait for a merge result; queue processor owns the happy-path main merge/finalization activities; gates and fake-data guard are green.
- Not satisfied: bounded retry/escalation semantics, worker startup reconciliation for existing running queue, full queue Temporal semantics, and required test coverage.

Update the guard to validate the reusable-action shape so it catches real regressions without failing on the refactor.

title: Add Text Your Ex iPhone dev loop and stale-session cleanup
description:
Why this exists: Text Your Ex local native development needs a deterministic physical iPhone run path from Tilt, and local auth should not silently seed demo users or make stale deleted sessions look like first-time signup.

What to do:
- Add a versioned iPhone build/install/launch script and a manual Tilt button.
- Keep device ids, local server URL, derived data, destination id, and Apple team overridable by env.
- Stop normal local API boot from auto-seeding demo users.
- Keep explicit e2e/dev reset seeding available.
- Make /auth/dev fail clearly when seeded demo data is absent instead of recreating it.
- Show a clearer stale-session/continue-with-Apple state when a stored token no longer maps to a user.

Acceptance criteria:
- [x] Local users table can remain empty after normal app boot.
- [x] Physical iPhone script builds, installs, and launches Text Your Ex.
- [x] Tilt exposes a manual Run on iPhone button.
- [x] Text Your Ex typecheck passes.
- [x] Unrelated repo changes are left unstaged.
close_reason: Implemented in cb33edb0: Text Your Ex iPhone Tilt/script flow, no implicit dev seeding, and stale-session onboarding copy. Typecheck green; pushed to main.

title: Debug Apple Sign In sheet signup-not-completed

- [ ] Result is classified as native request-shape bug, Apple Developer/App ID configuration bug, or device/account-side Apple auth bug.
- [ ] If code changes are needed, simulator build exits 0.

notes: Tried to replace CI/TestFlight loop with direct local iPhone live-reload install. Local Tilt stack healthy; exposed Vite on 0.0.0.0 and verified http://192.168.0.250:5173 returns 200. Patched generated ios/App/App/capacitor.config.json locally to use server.url http://192.168.0.250:5173. Direct iPhone xcodebuild with DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=X9E4HG27NK fails because that team is not configured in local Xcode accounts. Direct build with local team WRKMH85W4D fails because the team has a PLA update requirement and its wildcard provisioning profile lacks Sign in with Apple entitlement. Conclusion: direct local-device SIWA loop requires configuring the App Store Connect team in local Xcode or generating/installing a development profile for co.worldwidewebb.textyourex with SIWA capability; otherwise TestFlight remains the only valid real-device SIWA proof path.

title: Wire TicketWorkflow to queue and agent activities
description: TicketWorkflow currently only applies provided events and returns state. The end-to-end proof needs a real runner path that claims ticket-ready work, creates a worktree, launches builder/reviewer activities, waits for tmux/OpenCode completion or bounded proof signals, writes Beads metadata/comments/labels, and hands verified tickets to MergeWorkflow. Without this, www-3agy.16 cannot honestly prove the full Temporal-backed ticket workflow.

- [ ] TicketWorkflow or a workflow-runner claims one ticket-ready proof ticket through Beads adapter/activity boundaries

- [ ] Runner starts builder and reviewer tmux/OpenCode Activities exactly once for proof mode
- [ ] Runner records metadata/comments for branch, worktree, tmux sessions, OpenCode sessions, logs, and result
- [ ] Runner transitions ticket labels ticket-ready -> ticket-review -> ticket-verified before merge
- [ ] Runner invokes serialized MergeWorkflow for verified proof ticket
- [ ] Tests cover the runner with fake Activities and no real OpenCode calls
close_reason: Wired TicketWorkflow runner through activity boundaries: claims proof ticket, creates worktree, starts/waits builder and reviewer tmux/OpenCode activities, records metadata/comments, transitions labels to review/verified, and invokes serialized merge. Verified with fake-activity runner tests plus project-management tests/typecheck/config/focused Biome.

- [ ] One tiny proof ticket is created with /ticket
- [ ] Project Management UI is used to observe/control the flow

- [ ] Builder runs in tmux/OpenCode and produces a pushed branch

- [ ] Merge workflow merges/pushes to main

title: Implement serialized MergeWorkflow
description: Implement the serialized workflow that moves verified ticket branches back to main. Merge is deterministic first: update main, cherry-pick or merge, run final gates, push main, then close the Beads ticket. Agent fallback is separate.

title: Implement worktree and tmux Activities

description: Build the Beads adapter that treats Beads as the durable task queue. Labels drive workflow queues; metadata records machine state; comments carry builder summaries, reviewer findings, and escalation history. .tickets, if used, is only runtime/log cache and not authoritative.

title: Guard prod deploys against missing GHCR pull secrets
description: Today www-0y64.4 deploy failed because control-center/ghcr-pull was missing from the live cluster while Pulumi still had it in state. Kubelet pulled private GHCR images anonymously and hit ImagePullBackOff/unauthorized until a targeted Pulumi refresh/up recreated the Secret. Add a deploy/prod verification guard that asserts ghcr-pull exists in every namespace with GHCR workloads before or during deploy, and document the recovery path.

title: Build Temporal-backed ticket workflow in project-management
description: Milestone epic for the new Beads-backed tickets workflow. This moves ticket creation, execution, review, merge, observability, and controls into products/project-management with Temporal durable orchestration.
design:
Goal: build the new tickets workflow as part of products/project-management, with Temporal as the durable orchestrator from v1. Beads remains the ticket source of truth. Project Management becomes the workflow cockpit, replacing the separate TUI idea. OpenCode remains the coding/review engine initially, wrapped by tmux for live inspectability. Temporal owns workflow state, attempts, timers, retries, queries, and signals.

Core labels: ticket-ready is the builder queue, ticket-review is the reviewer queue, ticket-verified means reviewed and ready for serialized merge, ticket-retry marks reviewer-requested builder changes, ticket-human is the escape hatch and must stay unclosed so downstream dependencies remain blocked.

Workflow: /ticket creates immediate Beads tickets via writing-tickets. Runnable tickets default to ticket-ready, manual tickets use --manual and do not get ticket-ready. TicketWorkflow claims ticket-ready, prepares a worktree under .worktrees/tickets/<ticket-id>-<slug>, starts builder in tmux/OpenCode, verifies deterministic Beads handoff, starts reviewer, verifies deterministic Beads handoff, then MergeWorkflow serializes merge/cherry-pick to main, runs final gates, pushes main, and only then closes the ticket.

Revised agent handoff model: agents do not pass verdict JSON through stdout/stderr. Beads is the durable handoff boundary. Builder and reviewer mutate the ticket directly with comments and labels. Temporal supervises by checking Beads state after each agent run.

Builder loop: Temporal starts one builder session for the ticket. Builder edits in the ticket worktree, commits/pushes, leaves a Beads handoff comment, and moves the ticket to ticket-review. If reviewer requests changes by moving the ticket to ticket-retry, Temporal resumes the same builder session first, preserving context, and prompts it to read the latest reviewer comment and continue in the same worktree. Fresh builder sessions are fallback only when the original session cannot be resumed.

Reviewer loop: Temporal starts one reviewer session for the ticket. Reviewer reviews the builder branch/worktree, leaves a Beads review comment, and moves the ticket to exactly one of ticket-verified, ticket-retry, or ticket-human. If the reviewer forgets the handoff, Temporal resumes the same reviewer session first and prompts it to complete the missing Beads transition/comment. Fresh reviewer sessions are fallback only when the original session cannot be resumed.

Deterministic checks: after builder exits, Temporal verifies the ticket has a fresh builder comment and ticket-review. After reviewer exits, Temporal verifies the ticket no longer has ticket-review, has exactly one of ticket-verified/ticket-retry/ticket-human, and has a fresh reviewer comment. If handoff is missing, Temporal resumes the same agent session. After max repair attempts, Temporal marks ticket-human and stops.

Merge: ticket-verified enters MergeWorkflow. MergeWorkflow serializes merge to main, runs final gates, pushes main, and closes the ticket. Merge/gate failures can start/resume a merge-fix agent, then rerun deterministic gates. Exhaustion marks ticket-human, never closed.

Temporal-ready boundaries: TicketStore handles Beads reads/writes/comments/metadata; WorktreeManager handles git worktrees/branches; AgentRunner handles OpenCode/tmux/log capture/resume; TicketWorkflowEngine is Temporal workflow code; MetricsStore aggregates durations/cost/quota; TicketControl maps UI controls to Temporal signals. Non-deterministic operations are Activities, not Workflow code.

State diagram:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Ready: ticket-ready
  Ready --> BuilderRunning: start builder session
  BuilderRunning --> Review: builder comment + ticket-review
  Review --> ReviewerRunning: start reviewer session
  ReviewerRunning --> Verified: reviewer comment + ticket-verified
  ReviewerRunning --> Retry: reviewer comment + ticket-retry
  ReviewerRunning --> Human: reviewer comment + ticket-human
  Retry --> BuilderRunning: resume same builder session
  Verified --> MergeQueue: deterministic gates pass
  MergeQueue --> Main: merge + push + close ticket
  BuilderRunning --> BuilderRepair: missing builder handoff
  BuilderRepair --> BuilderRunning: resume same builder session
  ReviewerRunning --> ReviewerRepair: missing reviewer handoff
  ReviewerRepair --> ReviewerRunning: resume same reviewer session
  BuilderRepair --> Human: repair attempts exhausted
  ReviewerRepair --> Human: repair attempts exhausted
  MergeQueue --> Human: merge/gates exhausted
  Human --> [*]
  Main --> [*]
```

Sequence diagram:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant T as Temporal
  participant B as Builder session
  participant R as Reviewer session
  participant BD as Beads
  participant G as Git

  T->>BD: claim ticket-ready
  T->>G: create worktree/branch
  T->>B: start builder
  B->>G: edit + commit + push
  B->>BD: builder comment + move to ticket-review
  T->>BD: verify builder handoff

  T->>R: start reviewer
  R->>BD: reviewer comment + move to verified/retry/human
  T->>BD: verify reviewer handoff

  alt ticket-retry
    T->>B: resume same builder session
    B->>G: fix + commit + push
    B->>BD: builder comment + move to ticket-review
    T->>R: resume same reviewer session
  else ticket-verified
    T->>G: merge + final gates + push main
    T->>BD: close ticket
  else ticket-human
    T->>BD: stop for human
  end

  opt missing handoff
    T->>B: resume same builder, complete Beads handoff
    T->>R: resume same reviewer, complete Beads handoff
  end
```

Safety: builder never closes tickets; reviewer verifies but does not merge; merger owns close after main push. Defaults: builder max attempts 2, reviewer max attempts 2, same-session handoff repair max attempts 1 before human. Step exhaustion and timeouts are distinct outcomes. Max attempts moves to ticket-human, status blocked, never closed. One real OpenCode proof only, in proof mode, with bounded steps/cheap model to protect credits; retry/failure paths use fake activities/tests.

Runtime: products/project-management hosts worker/server/UI. Tilt or a local dev command starts Temporal dev server and worker. Project Management Kanban shows workflow queues and controls. No separate TUI in v1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All child tickets are closed or explicitly deferred
- [ ] /ticket creates immediate Beads tickets using writing-tickets
- [ ] Project Management UI shows and controls ticket workflow queues
- [ ] Temporal worker runs builder, reviewer, and merge workflows locally
- [ ] One end-to-end proof ticket goes from ticket-ready to closed through Project Management UI
- [ ] TDD evidence exists for workflow state transitions, Beads queue behavior, deterministic activities, and UI controls
- [ ] Credit-safe proof uses only one real OpenCode builder/reviewer path; failure/retry paths are tested with fakes

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
Implementation prompt for a new chat:

We are in /Users/calum/code/github.com/0x63616c/world-wide-webb.

Goal: implement the full `www-3agy - Build Temporal-backed ticket workflow in project-management` epic end-to-end, as fast as possible, using subagents for parallel work where safe.

Start by reading:
- CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md
- AGENTS.md
- CLAUDE.md
- docs/ticket-standards.md
- products/project-management/README.md
- Beads epic `bd show www-3agy --json`
- Children `bd show www-3agy --children --json`

Rules:
- Use Beads as the source of truth.
- Treat each Beads child ticket as its own subagent work package.
- Launch subagents only for tickets whose Beads dependencies are satisfied.
- Do not run dependent tickets in parallel.
- Each subagent must claim exactly one ticket, work only that ticket, and return implementation summary + verification + changed files.
- The main coordinator must not blindly trust subagent “done” reports. It must inspect diffs, run gates, verify AC, then close tickets and commit/push.
- Use `bd update <id> --claim` before working each child ticket.
- Start with `www-3agy.1 - Add writing-tickets skill and /ticket command`.
- Build with TDD wherever practical: write failing tests/shape checks first, then implementation.
- Use `bun`/`bunx`, never npm/npx.
- Do not run real OpenCode agent proof repeatedly. Only one real OpenCode end-to-end proof is allowed, at the final verification ticket, and it must be credit-safe.
- Use fake/stub Activities for retry/failure/human paths.
- Do not touch unrelated dirty files.
- Do not open PRs. Ship through main when the workflow reaches merge steps, respecting repo rules.
- Commit small, focused commits per logical child ticket with `type(area/www-3agy.N): desc`.

Architecture decisions already made:
- Build this into `products/project-management`.
- Use Temporal from v1 as durable orchestrator.
- Project Management UI is the workflow cockpit, no separate TUI.
- Beads is the durable ticket queue.
- Queue labels:
  - `ticket-ready`
  - `ticket-review`
  - `ticket-verified`
  - `ticket-retry`
  - `ticket-human`
- `ticket-human` tickets must stay unclosed so downstream Beads dependencies remain blocked.
- `.tickets/` may exist only for logs/prompts/runtime cache, not authoritative workflow state.
- Worktrees go under `.worktrees/tickets/<ticket-id>-<slug>`.
- tmux sessions:
  - `ticket_<id>_build_<attempt>`
  - `ticket_<id>_review_<attempt>`
  - `ticket_<id>_mergefix_<attempt>`
- Builder never closes tickets.
- Reviewer verifies but does not merge.
- MergeWorkflow serializes merge to main, runs final gates, pushes main, then closes ticket.
- Default attempts:
  - builder max attempts 2
  - reviewer max attempts 2
- Step exhaustion and timeout are distinct outcomes.
- On max attempts: remove queue labels, add `ticket-human`, status blocked, comment failure history, do not close.

Use subagents by ticket, in dependency-safe batches:

Batch 1:
- `www-3agy.1`

Batch 2:
- `www-3agy.2`
- `www-3agy.3`

Batch 3:
- `www-3agy.4`
- `www-3agy.5`

Batch 4:
- `www-3agy.6`

Batch 5:
- `www-3agy.7`
- `www-3agy.8`

Batch 6:
- `www-3agy.9`

Batch 7:
- `www-3agy.10`
- `www-3agy.11`

Batch 8:
- `www-3agy.12`
- `www-3agy.13`

Batch 9:
- `www-3agy.14`
- `www-3agy.15`

Batch 10:
- `www-3agy.16`

Acceptance requirements:
- `/ticket` creates immediate tickets using `writing-tickets`.
- `writing-tickets` is mandatory/canonical and defaults to `ticket-ready`; `--manual` skips runnable labels.
- Temporal worker runs locally under project-management.
- Workflow state transitions are tested with fakes.
- Non-deterministic operations are Activities.
- UI shows workflow queues, Temporal phases, tmux attach commands, OpenCode sessions, logs/prompts, metrics/quota.
- UI controls signal Temporal workflows.
- One tiny E2E proof ticket goes `/ticket` -> `ticket-ready` -> builder -> `ticket-review` -> reviewer -> `ticket-verified` -> MergeWorkflow -> main push -> Beads closed.
- Only one real OpenCode proof run. Everything else uses fake/stub tests.

Before claiming done:
- Run relevant tests for project-management.
- Run `bun run typecheck` or justified focused typecheck if project-management is isolated.
- Run focused Biome checks on changed paths.
- Verify Beads child AC.
- Close completed child tickets.
- Sync Beads with `bd dolt push`.
- Commit and push all code changes.

title: Prevent prod Pulumi up without image digest pins
description: Why this issue exists: a local prod Pulumi update with wwwinfra:imageDigests empty rendered private GHCR :main tags, causing replacement pods to hit ImagePullBackOff until digest pins were restored manually. What needs to be done: add a prod-safety guard so prod deploys refuse to render mutable/private :main app images when digest pins are absent, while preserving local/dev workflows.

- [ ] No fake data, check-fake-data guard green

title: Captive portal email-OTP unworkable inside Apple CNA (can't reach Mail pre-auth)
description: Real-device finding (www-q002.17, 2026-06-16). Email-code verification is fundamentally broken inside Apple's Captive Network Assistant: the CNA sheet is a sandboxed mini-browser, reading the emailed code requires opening Mail, but Mail has no internet pre-auth and leaving the sheet kills the flow. On the iPad wall panel (no cellular) it is fully dead. Email-OTP is the wrong primitive for a CNA. Recommended fix: drop OTP (email + consent -> immediate authorize). SMS-OTP also viable for iPhone (cellular delivery + in-sheet autofill) but not the iPad panel. Blocks q002.17 production cutover.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Auth mechanism chosen + documented in docs/captive-portal/ (decision: drop-OTP vs SMS vs other)
- [ ] Flow is single-step (no multi-screen bail point) so abandonment is not possible mid-flow
- [ ] Real iPhone completes guest authorization WITHOUT leaving the CNA sheet
- [ ] iPad wall panel (no cellular) completes guest authorization end-to-end
- [ ] portal_authorization row written + UniFi authorize-guest succeeds (30-day lease)
- [ ] No step requires opening Mail/another app to proceed
- [ ] Pre-auth: gateway ALWAYS redirects the CNA probe (never serves Apple Success) so every fresh association re-pops the sheet
- [ ] Re-trigger guidance documented (forget+rejoin / wifi toggle / open http) for the post-cancel iOS-suppression case

title: Wire infra/src k8s cluster program to per-product namespaces from @repo/platform
design:
Surfaced by www-r3it re-audit. M1 typed identity (packages/platform: defineProduct().namespace = slug) shipped and is consumed by the CF lane, but infra/src/ (k8s cluster program) still hardcodes APP_NAMESPACE="control-center" (cluster.ts:11) and threads it through program.ts. NORTH_STAR requires each product its own namespace. This is the infra-side wiring of the existing typed primitive, plus the adjacent naming cleanup needed so product identity is consistent across k8s, Pulumi, CI image builds, and digest config.

Non-technical decision from Calum, 2026-06-17:
- Scope is not just the minimal namespace split. Include the product image naming cleanup now.
- Prefer full product slugs for infra/global names. Do not preserve cc/cp/tye shorthand for GHCR/Pulumi/config just because it exists today.
- DNS hostnames keep short DNS codes (app--cc, app--cp, app--tye, app--amp) because those are intentional user-facing route labels, not infra object names.

Naming rule:
- If the namespace is visible, use the short local component name.
- If the namespace is not visible, use the full product slug.
- User-facing DNS remains the intentional dnsCode.

Examples:
- Product slug: control-center, captive-portal, text-your-ex, amp.
- Namespace: control-center, captive-portal, text-your-ex, amp.
- K8s Deployment/Service inside namespace: api, web, worker, media-worker, frontend, app. Avoid redundant names like control-center-api inside namespace control-center.
- Pod names inherit local deployment names, e.g. api-... in namespace control-center and api-... in namespace text-your-ex.
- Pulumi logical names are stack-wide and should include product slug: control-center-api, control-center-web, text-your-ex-api, amp-app.
- GHCR repos should use full product slug: ghcr.io/0x63616c/www-control-center-api, www-control-center-web, www-text-your-ex-api, www-captive-portal-app-or-portal, www-amp-app.
- Pulumi digest config keys should align with product-component identity, e.g. wwwinfra:imageDigests.control-center-api, wwwinfra:imageDigests.control-center-web, wwwinfra:imageDigests.text-your-ex-api, wwwinfra:imageDigests.amp-app. Component-only keys like api will collide once multiple products have an api.
- Secret names should derive from product slug/prefix. Prefer full slug names like control-center-secrets-api and text-your-ex-secrets-api if pulumi preview is safe; otherwise document any compatibility exception explicitly.
- PVC names can stay short when namespace-local, e.g. maps, drizzle-data. Use product slug in Pulumi logical names if needed.
- CNPG cluster/RW service names derive from product database identity, e.g. control-center-rw, text-your-ex-rw.

Hardcoded audit, 2026-06-17. BEFORE starting, re-run this grep and check for new occurrences:

  grep -rn "cc-secrets\|APP_NAMESPACE\|control-center\|www-cc-\|www-cp-\|www-tye-" infra/src/ .github/workflows packages/platform --include="*.ts" --include="*.yml" --include="*.yaml"

Known namespace/product identity places to fix:
- cluster.ts: APP_NAMESPACE="control-center" root constant drives everything.
- program.ts: passes one shared namespace into ESO/CNPG/certmanager/services/crons.
- eso.ts: cc-secrets-${service} k8s Secret names.
- component.ts: cc-secrets-${p.name} volume mount default.
- services.ts: POSTGRES_HOST="control-center-rw" must derive from product database/cluster name.
- cnpg.ts: hardcoded case "control-center" product match.
- services.ts/crons.ts: GHCR images currently use default www-cc-* plus bespoke shorthand map for www-cp-*, www-tye-*, www-amp-*.
- CI workflow: image build/push names and digest lookup keys must match the new product-component naming.
- tests: image digest expectations currently assert www-cc-*.

Operational constraint:
- Control Center k8s namespace must stay control-center, and pulumi preview must show no destructive Control Center namespace churn.
- Image repository renames are deliberate churn and must be updated atomically across CI, Pulumi image references, digest config keys, and tests.
- Do not change Cloudflare/DNS host naming as part of this ticket.

Cosmetic/probably fine unless touched for nearby cleanup:
- component.ts control-center:infra:* Pulumi resource type strings have no k8s effect.
- cluster.ts DEFAULT_CONTEXT="cc-homelab" is a local kubeconfig context name, not a product namespace.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Re-run the hardcoded identity grep in the design before starting; classify every match and clean up anything newly weird.
- [ ] infra/src/cluster.ts no longer hardcodes one shared APP_NAMESPACE; namespace derives from defineProduct(slug).namespace.
- [ ] infra/program.ts creates one Kubernetes Namespace per deployed product.
- [ ] ESO/native secrets, CNPG, cert-manager, services, and crons receive product-derived namespace instead of a shared constant.
- [ ] Kubernetes Deployment/Service names stay local within their namespace where appropriate, e.g. control-center/api, text-your-ex/api.
- [ ] Pulumi logical names include product slug where resources are stack-global, e.g. control-center-api, text-your-ex-api.
- [ ] GHCR image repositories use full product slugs, e.g. www-control-center-api, www-text-your-ex-api, not www-cc-* / www-tye-* shorthand.
- [ ] CI build/push jobs and Pulumi digest lookup keys are updated atomically to the new product-component image names.
- [ ] Pulumi image digest config keys are product-component scoped, e.g. imageDigests.control-center-api, not component-only api.
- [ ] Secret names derive from product slug/prefix; prefer full slug names like control-center-secrets-api if pulumi preview is safe, otherwise document any compatibility exception.
- [ ] component.ts secret volume mount default uses product-derived secret name, not cc-secrets-*.
- [ ] POSTGRES_HOST derives from product database/CNPG cluster identity, not hardcoded control-center-rw.
- [ ] cnpg.ts product match uses defineProduct slug, not hardcoded string.
- [ ] Cloudflare/DNS hostnames keep existing dnsCode-based names and are not renamed in this ticket.
- [ ] Tests assert namespace === product.namespace and cover product-derived image repository/digest key naming.
- [ ] pulumi preview shows no destructive namespace churn for Control Center.
- [ ] Relevant gates pass.
notes: Completed by epic www-ghze. Final preview evidence: https://app.pulumi.com/worldwidewebb/world-wide-webb/prod/previews/61d3e40c-733f-489b-b527-9bc14148a61b. Product namespaces: control-center, captive-portal, text-your-ex, amp; shared edge runtime in platform. K8s metadata names are local inside namespaces, e.g. text-your-ex/api and text-your-ex/frontend; tye-* appears only in Pulumi legacy aliases/original deletes during migration. GHCR repos and digest keys use full product slugs. Preview shows no Control Center CNPG/live Postgres replacement and no Control Center backup/media PVC churn. Expected replacements: Captive Portal/TYE CNPG clusters and related resources move to product namespaces, AMP app moves to amp, cloudflared moves to platform. Full gates passed: bun run test, bun run typecheck, worktree-safe Biome, scripts/check-fake-data.sh.
close_reason: Completed by www-ghze namespace/image naming migration with final preview and gates recorded.

title: [bug] api--tye.worldwidewebb.co 521: TYE API has no public tunnel route declared
description:
api--tye.worldwidewebb.co returns 521 (Cloudflare: origin unreachable). Root cause: textYourExProductManifest() declares the TYE API as internalService({port:8787}) - cluster-only, no public exposure. infra/cloudflare/src/routes.ts productRoutes() only wires tye.app.exposure (the frontend); no api--tye tunnel ingress rule or CNAME is declared.

Fix: add a publicApi (or equivalent) exposure to the TYE manifest, wire it into routes.ts productRoutes(), apply via pulumi up in infra/cloudflare.

The k8s side is fine: tye-api pod is 1/1, service tye-api ClusterIP:8787 exists. Only the CF tunnel ingress + CNAME are missing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] textYourExProductManifest() declares api exposure pointing at tye-api:8787 (or equivalent public exposure helper)
- [ ] infra/cloudflare/src/routes.ts productRoutes() includes the TYE API route (api--tye.worldwidewebb.co -> http://tye-api:8787)
- [ ] pulumi up applies the new ingress rule + CNAME, no destructive diff
- [ ] curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code} ssl=%{ssl_verify_result}' https://api--tye.worldwidewebb.co/api/health returns non-521, ssl=0
- [ ] CORS unblocked: curl with -H 'Origin: https://app--tye.worldwidewebb.co' returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin header
close_reason: TYE API is intentionally internal-only (internalService). Public api--tye route was not planned. Superseded by www-tye-arch investigation.

title: [bug] TYE prod CORS block: api--tye rejects app--tye origin (stale image pre-flatten)
description:
CORS error in prod: 'https://api--tye.worldwidewebb.co/api/auth/demo' blocked from origin 'https://app--tye.worldwidewebb.co' - no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.

Root cause: server.ts ALLOWED_ORIGINS already contains 'https://app--tye.worldwidewebb.co' (correct, www-kbiy domain flatten), but the RUNNING prod image was built before the flatten landed. The old image has the old origin (app.tye.worldwidewebb.co) so it rejects the new host.

Fix: force a rebuild + redeploy of the www-tye-api (currently control-center-... wait, tye-api) image. Either push a trivial change to products/text-your-ex/apps/api/ to trigger the CI path filter, or run 'gh workflow run ci.yml --ref main' with force_all=true.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] curl https://api--tye.worldwidewebb.co/api/auth/demo with Origin: https://app--tye.worldwidewebb.co returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://app--tye.worldwidewebb.co (or the endpoint responds normally)
- [ ] app--tye.worldwidewebb.co loads in a browser without CORS errors in the console
- [ ] The fix is a redeploy of the current code (NOT a code change - server.ts is already correct)
close_reason: CORS error was symptom of TYE API being internal-only by design. Closing - no bug here.

title: LANDMINE: cuuw wildcard default-deny Access would block public app--tye + dashboard
description: Found in www-kbiy preview. desiredAccessApps() (infra/cloudflare/src/access.ts:83) adds a wildcardBlockFloor *.worldwidewebb.co -> deny everyone (precedence 99). Cloudflare Access applies the wildcard app to ANY hostname lacking a more-specific Access app. app--tye is PUBLIC (publicWeb, intentionally no Access app), as are dashboard/hooks-test/portainer. So applying the gate as-is DENIES public app--tye and dashboard. The cuuw design lists explicit allows for cc/amp/storybook/drizzle/hooks but NOT a public-host bypass. Need an explicit allow (or public bypass) for every public host before the wildcard-deny floor is applied.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] every public host (app--tye, and any other publicWeb) has an explicit Allow Access app above the wildcard deny, OR the wildcard floor is scoped to exclude public hosts
- [ ] a test asserts public hosts are not caught by the default-deny
- [ ] verified: public app--tye returns 200 (not an Access challenge/deny) after the gate is applied
notes: MITIGATED (not fully resolved): the wildcard default-deny floor is now flag-gated OFF by default (applyAccessGate, www-b6ad commit), so it currently blocks nothing. The real fix - an explicit allow/bypass for every public host (app--tye) ABOVE the floor - is still REQUIRED before the gate is ever enabled, and belongs to www-cuuw. Keep open until that bypass exists and the gate can be safely turned on.

title: GITOPS GAP: infra/cloudflare Pulumi project not deployed by CI
description: The cloudflare tunnel/DNS/Access config lives in its own Pulumi project (control-center-cloudflare, infra/cloudflare) which CI does NOT deploy (the deploy job only runs pulumi up on the main workload infra). Same gitops gap as infra/unifi. Consequence (found in www-kbiy): route/access code changes land on main + pass gates but never reach the live tunnel, so cloudflared keeps serving stale ingress. The stack is also still marked ADOPT-ONLY/do-not-apply in program.ts from its import milestone. Wire it into CI (or a gated deploy lane) so route changes converge to prod like workloads do.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI (or a gated job) runs pulumi up on control-center-cloudflare on relevant changes
- [ ] program.ts adopt-only/do-not-apply guard comments resolved (it's a real deploy path now that bosun is gone)
- [ ] a route/host change on main converges to the live tunnel without manual pulumi up
notes: UNBLOCKED re: access gate - the cloudflare stack is now a live deploy target with the access gate flag-gated OFF by default (www-b6ad), so CI can pulumi-up it safely (no floor, no dashboard block). NEW prerequisite for a clean CI converge: the service-token Access policy bug () must be fixed first, else 'pulumi up' exits non-zero (2 errored on the kiosk/hooks policies). Routing + product Access are already applied by hand today.

title: Flatten product hostnames app.X→app--X + delete ACM (free Universal SSL, no $10/mo)
description: Calum's decision 2026-06-15: avoid the $10/mo Cloudflare ACM. Nested 2-label hosts (app.cc.worldwidewebb.co) aren't covered by the free *.worldwidewebb.co Universal SSL (wildcard = one label only), causing sslv3 handshake failures. Fix: flatten to a SINGLE label app--cc.worldwidewebb.co (double-dash per Calum), which Universal SSL covers for free, keeping CF proxy + Access + tunnel intact. Then delete the inert ACM CertificatePack code + applyNestedTls flag entirely. Keystone change: packages/platform/src/index.ts:202 host template '.' -> '--'.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] packages/platform/src/index.ts:202 builds app--<code>.<domain> (host + '--' + dnsCode), bare-host template (:206) unchanged
- [ ] All derived + hardcoded literals updated: capacitor.config.ts:16, text-your-ex/apps/api/src/server.ts:9 CORS, amp/src/App.tsx:8, all infra/cloudflare/test/*.ts assertions, capacitor.config.test.ts:24
- [ ] ACM fully deleted: infra/cloudflare/src/tls.ts removed, program.ts applyNestedTls import+gate+output removed, test/tls.test.ts removed; grep 'applyNestedTls\|CertificatePack' in infra/cloudflare returns empty
- [ ] Docs updated to match reality: docs/platform/NETWORKING.html + README.html 'nested hostnames intentional / *.worldwidewebb.co does not cover app.cc' contract corrected to flattened single-label; go-live-runbook.md ACM keystone section replaced
- [ ] Gates green: bun run typecheck, bun run test (0 failed 0 skipped), bunx biome check, bunx knip all exit 0, output shown, none weakened
- [ ] Shipped: type(networking/www-xxx) commit, merged to main no PR, pushed, CI+deploy green
- [ ] VERIFIED LIVE PROD: curl https://app--tye/app--cc/app--amp.worldwidewebb.co returns ssl_verify=0 (handshake OK) and a real HTTP status (200 public / 302 Access), evidence shown in transcript
- [ ] No regressions: dashboard.worldwidewebb.co still 200, main clean
notes: Code complete + all gates green (typecheck/test 1919pass/biome/knip) on main (merge 5996323f4, push 269f3852f). CI run 27557530062 building+deploying. Pending: live curl verify of app--tye/app--cc/app--amp once cloudflare stack rolls.
close_reason: Flatten shipped + verified LIVE in prod: app--tye 200, app--cc/app--amp 302 (Access-protected), all ssl_verify=0, dashboard 200 (no regression). Browser screenshot confirms real TYE app renders at app--tye.worldwidewebb.co. ACM deleted, $0/mo. Cloudflare stack applied (routing + product Access). Gates green.

title: Rename beads prefix CC -> www + scrub git history (msgs, file contents, filenames)
beads project-management web UI (list/kanban/issue views, Vercel theme)
