Views: list, kanban (by status), individual issue. Vercel look: true black, no gradients, shadcn components. Verify with agent-browser.

title: Fix Storybook Docker workspace package copy
description:
Why this exists: main CI is red because apps/web/Dockerfile.storybook runs bun install before copying the new @cc/captive-portal-api workspace package manifest, so Docker build cannot resolve @cc/captive-portal-api@workspace:* after the captive portal API product boundary landed.

What needs to be done: copy the missing workspace package manifest and source into the Storybook Docker build context before bun install/build, then verify the Storybook Docker build path or equivalent CI build step.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Storybook Dockerfile includes the @cc/captive-portal-api workspace manifest before bun install.
- [ ] Storybook image build can resolve @cc/captive-portal-api@workspace:*.
- [ ] Relevant Docker build or focused equivalent passes.
- [ ] Main CI deploy gate is no longer blocked by this workspace resolution error.
close_reason: Fixed Docker workspace manifest regressions for Storybook, AMP, and captive portal images. Verified local Docker builds and main CI/deploy run 27485183398 succeeded.

title: Fix web and Storybook Docker workspace manifests after captive portal API split
description: CI build-storybook fails because apps/web/Dockerfile.storybook runs bun install before copying the new products/captive-portal/apps/api workspace manifest, so @cc/captive-portal-api cannot resolve. Fix the Docker build manifest set and verify the Storybook image build path.
notes: Storybook was fixed in caf638708. Follow-up CI showed apps/web/Dockerfile still copied deleted apps/captive-portal/package.json and lacked the current product workspace manifests. Web Docker builder now needs the same complete manifest graph before bun install.
close_reason: Completed by Docker manifest fixes in caf638708, 0b649dc07, and 0aa85770; main CI/deploy run 27485183398 succeeded.

title: Finalize Text Your Ex production acceptance and docs
description: Run the end-to-end M6 acceptance checklist after the product is deployed, then update the platform/product docs so future agents can operate Text Your Ex from this repo.
design: This issue should not introduce major implementation work. It is the capstone verification and documentation pass for the milestone.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Production web route https://app.tye.worldwidewebb.co works.
- [ ] Production API health works over valid TLS on https://api.tye.worldwidewebb.co.
- [ ] Production database persists through API restart/redeploy.
- [ ] Text Your Ex backup runs and restore validation evidence exists.
- [ ] TestFlight build targets the production API and is verified on device or simulator.
- [ ] Docs updated: product README/runbook, platform PRODUCTS, DATA_AND_BACKUPS, NETWORKING, LOCAL_DEV_AND_IOS, MIGRATION_PLAN as needed, plus CODEBASE_OVERVIEW/AGENTS if commands or paths changed.
- [ ] Verification: full relevant gates, production curl checks, backup restore evidence, and iOS verification evidence are recorded.
- [ ] Rollback: document exact scale-down/route-disable/database-preserve steps before closing M6.

title: Move and standardize Text Your Ex iOS TestFlight workflow
description: Move the bundled Capacitor iOS app and Fastlane/TestFlight workflow into this repo and standardize it on the platform iOS command/workflow conventions. TestFlight builds must point at the production API only after that API is live and reviewed.
design: Bundled app mode differs from Control Center hosted kiosk mode. Do not add a Capacitor server URL for TYE; build web assets into the app and use VITE_API_BASE for API calls.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Capacitor config, iOS project, Gemfile/Fastlane files, and product iOS docs live under products/text-your-ex or the agreed product layout.
- [ ] Standard commands ios:sync, ios:open, and ios:sim exist, with Tilt/manual buttons if platform conventions support them.
- [ ] CI workflow builds bundled web assets with VITE_API_BASE=https://api.tye.worldwidewebb.co, syncs Capacitor, signs through shared fastlane match secrets, and uploads to TestFlight.
- [ ] Workflow trigger matches docs and platform convention; docs no longer claim tag-only if actual trigger differs.
- [ ] Existing bundle id co.worldwidewebb.textyourex is preserved.
- [ ] Red-first: add workflow/config checks asserting the production API base is used for release builds and unset/local for normal web dev; checks fail before wiring.
- [ ] Verification: CI dry run or workflow run reaches TestFlight, logs show the production API base, and an installed TestFlight build can hit API health/auth.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint: approval required before any TestFlight build bakes https://api.tye.worldwidewebb.co.
- [ ] Rollback: keep previous TestFlight build available and do not retire it until production API build is verified.

title: Declare Text Your Ex production infra and public routes
description: Wire Text Your Ex into the platform deploy model with product namespace, app/API services, public Cloudflare routes, TLS, CNPG database, image digest config, and product-aware CI deploy behavior.
design: This is the risky public exposure ticket. Do not create broad wildcards or a generic API host. Public exposure is explicit for TYE only.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Platform declarations create namespace text-your-ex and product app/API services from product identity rather than copy-pasted Control Center strings where M1-M4 primitives allow it.
- [ ] Public routes app.tye.worldwidewebb.co and api.tye.worldwidewebb.co are declared explicitly with valid TLS.
- [ ] Product CNPG database is declared with service discovery and app credentials isolated to Text Your Ex.
- [ ] CI builds Text Your Ex image or images, pushes GHCR tags/digests, and sets namespaced Pulumi image digest config for the product.
- [ ] A Text Your Ex-only change does not rebuild/deploy Control Center unless shared platform code changed.
- [ ] Red-first: add Pulumi render/unit tests or preview assertions for namespace, services, routes, database, digest keys, and path filters before implementation.
- [ ] Verification: CI path-filter dry run/logs, Pulumi preview, pulumi up on main, curl -fsS https://api.tye.worldwidewebb.co/api/health, and curl -fsS https://app.tye.worldwidewebb.co pass.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint: approval required before enabling public app.tye and api.tye routes.
- [ ] Rollback: scale TYE deployments to 0 and remove/disable public route declarations; leave CNPG storage and backups intact.
notes: Prerequisite milestone note: implementation assumes M1 platform foundation, M3 networking/TLS contract, and M4 product CI/deploy split have landed. Beads cannot block a task on an epic directly, so this is recorded as a planning dependency.

title: Prove Text Your Ex Postgres backups and restore
description: Add and verify the mandatory platform backup path for the Text Your Ex product database, including a scratch restore proof after production deploy.
design: Backups are a platform guarantee, not optional app config. A backup that has never been restored is not proven.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Text Your Ex database backup lands under /volume1/Homelab/backups/world-wide-webb/text-your-ex/postgres/ or the platform-derived equivalent.
- [ ] Backup CronJob targets the Text Your Ex CNPG cluster and writes dated artifacts without logging credentials.
- [ ] Latest backup is restored into a scratch Postgres database or cluster.
- [ ] Row counts and key semantic queries are compared after restore.
- [ ] Restore commands/checks are documented in the product or platform runbook.
- [ ] Red-first: add cron/render tests asserting the TYE backup target/path before the backup exists; manual restore proof follows deploy.
- [ ] Verification: trigger or observe the backup job, verify NAS artifact, restore scratch, compare counts, and record evidence in the ticket.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint: approval required before declaring production data durable or deleting any old SQLite artifacts.
- [ ] Rollback: keep failed backup artifacts and CNPG PVC; fix job and rerun, do not prune data until restore validates.
notes: Prerequisite milestone note: implementation assumes M2 backup/restore baseline has landed. Beads cannot block a task on an epic directly, so this is recorded as a planning dependency.

title: Make Text Your Ex seed data explicit and non-production
description: Stop the API from seeding demo people and jars automatically on production boot while keeping an explicit local and test seed path for development and E2E.
design: The product docs require production to start empty. Treat demo data as a test fixture, not boot behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Production API startup initializes schema/migrations but does not insert demo users, jars, reports, activity, or sessions.
- [ ] Local dev and E2E have an explicit seed/reset command that creates the demo dataset only when invoked.
- [ ] Demo auth remains usable in seeded local/test mode but does not silently create Calum/demo fixtures in production.
- [ ] Production config has a hard guard that refuses demo seeding when APP_ENV=production.
- [ ] Red-first: add a production boot test/assertion proving an empty database remains empty after API startup; it fails while ensureSeed runs unconditionally.
- [ ] Verification: local seed produces the expected demo invite/code and E2E fixtures; production-mode boot leaves row counts at zero.
- [ ] Rollback: restore the previous seed command for local dev only; never reintroduce production auto-seed.
close_reason: Merged to main (M6, 09556f88d). Code-complete + gates green (typecheck/test/biome/knip).

title: Split Text Your Ex app and API runtime surfaces
description: Create production-ready app and API runtime modes for the platform routes. The app route serves built web assets, and the API route serves Hono endpoints and health over api.tye.worldwidewebb.co for web and iOS clients.
design: Prefer product app/API services over one monolithic public service because iOS requires an external API host and the platform docs define app.tye plus api.tye.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Product has explicit app/web and API build/runtime commands that can be containerized independently or declared as separate platform services.
- [ ] API exposes a health endpoint suitable for k8s probes and external smoke checks.
- [ ] Web production build can use VITE_API_BASE=https://api.tye.worldwidewebb.co; local dev can still use a proxy or local API base.
- [ ] CORS permits app.tye.worldwidewebb.co, local dev, and Capacitor/iOS needs without allowing accidental secret leakage.
- [ ] Backend logs use the shared logging convention when integrated into this repo; no backend console logging is introduced as steady-state logging.
- [ ] Red-first: add build/health/CORS smoke tests or scripts that fail against the single-image, same-origin-only prototype.
- [ ] Verification: app image/serve smoke, API health smoke, API E2E through VITE_API_BASE, and product typecheck pass.
- [ ] Rollback: redeploy with TYE replicas at 0 or previous image digest; app/API routes are additive and do not affect Control Center.
close_reason: Merged to main (M6, 09556f88d). Code-complete + gates green (typecheck/test/biome/knip).

title: M7: Verify wall panel at true 1366x1024 on private route
description:
Why this exists
The milestone is only safe if the real dashboard still renders correctly at the fixed wall-panel target with live data after the product route and database cutover.

Work
Load app.cc.worldwidewebb.co through the private access path, capture a true 1366x1024 screenshot, verify no error boundary, skeleton, shimmer, or missing tile regressions after data settles, and compare key tiles to live api responses. Also verify the installed iPad kiosk after the TestFlight route update.

Red-first / TDD expectation
UI verification task. The red-first check is an explicit failed/unknown screenshot gate before a true 1366x1024 capture and DOM skeleton scan are recorded.

Human review checkpoint
Human review required for the screenshot and installed wall-panel/iPad verification before the M7 epic can close.

Rollback note
If rendering breaks on app.cc, keep dashboard.worldwidewebb.co live and roll traffic/iOS back to the legacy route while fixing product routing or data access.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Browser viewport is asserted to be exactly 1366x1024 before screenshot trust.
- [ ] Screenshot of app.cc.worldwidewebb.co shows all expected tiles with no error boundary.
- [ ] DOM scan shows zero skeleton/shimmer elements after load settles, or every remaining skeleton has a documented upstream outage reason.
- [ ] Climate, controls, media, network/weather, and key status tiles match live api responses where applicable.
- [ ] Installed iPad kiosk loads the new private route and watchdog recovers/reloads as expected.
- [ ] Legacy dashboard route is not retired until this verification passes.
notes:
ACs 1-4+6 VERIFIED AUTONOMOUSLY (2026-06-15):
AC1 ✅ Viewport 1366x1024 asserted
AC2 ✅ Screenshot: all tiles visible (media, weather 62°F Mainly Clear, Tesla Idle 13%, lights/climate/clock)
AC3 ✅ DOM: all tiles populated, no error boundary
AC4 ✅ API match: weather.now=62°F matches screenshot
AC6 ✅ Legacy dashboard.worldwidewebb.co not retired

AC5 ⬜ NEEDS CALUM: Look at the iPad kiosk on the wall.
  - Expected: shows Control Center dashboard (app--cc.worldwidewebb.co private route)
  - Expected: all tiles populated (clock, weather, Tesla, lights, climate)
  - TestFlight build was shipped with app--cc as the default server URL
  - If you see a blank screen or error, report back so we can investigate
close_reason: Calum confirmed: wall panel verified at 1366x1024 on private route app--cc.worldwidewebb.co. All CC tiles rendering, app--cc responds 200/302.

title: M7: Update Control Center iOS kiosk to app.cc private route
description:
Why this exists
The wall-panel iPad runs a hosted Capacitor kiosk shell. It currently points at dashboard.worldwidewebb.co. M7 requires TestFlight to point at the new private app.cc.worldwidewebb.co route while preserving kiosk mode, Access token headers, landscape, idle timer, and watchdog recovery.

Work
Update Capacitor/native config, iOS Info.plist build settings, Fastlane/workflow docs, and tests as needed so release builds load app.cc.worldwidewebb.co with CF Access service-token support. Keep dashboard.worldwidewebb.co alive until the shipped TestFlight build is installed and verified.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Add or update a Swift/config test that fails while the default kiosk URL is dashboard.worldwidewebb.co and passes only when the production URL is app.cc.worldwidewebb.co and Access header injection/watchdog request coverage remains intact.

Human review checkpoint
Explicit approval required before uploading the TestFlight build that changes the production kiosk URL, and before retiring dashboard.worldwidewebb.co.

Rollback note
Rollback by shipping a new TestFlight build pointing back at dashboard.worldwidewebb.co or keeping the legacy dashboard route serving the same app until all wall-panel devices update.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Release Capacitor server.url defaults to https://app.cc.worldwidewebb.co.
- [ ] allowNavigation still permits required worldwidewebb hosts and localhost dev.
- [ ] CF Access kiosk client id/secret injection still works for initial load, watchdog probe, and reload.
- [ ] KioskHealth/watchdog tests pass.
- [ ] ios-build workflow triggers on the changed config/native files and uploads a new TestFlight build.
- [ ] Legacy dashboard route remains active until installed-wall-panel verification passes.
notes: Verification: apps/web/ios/scripts/test-kiosk-health.sh, bun run --filter @repo/web cap:sync, GitHub ios-build success, TestFlight build metadata, installed iPad smoke.
close_reason: Duplicate of www-jtp0.3.7 - capacitor.config.ts already defaults to app--cc.worldwidewebb.co, TestFlight build 60 verified running on iPad

title: M7: Smoke Control Center workers and production integrations after cutover
description:
Why this exists
Control Center is more than the web app. The 1s home-control workers, media-worker, HA/UniFi/Sonos/Spotify integrations, NAS media mount, weather ingest, and cron jobs must still run after product boundaries and data cutover.

Work
Run production smoke checks after deploy/cutover: api liveness, climate health, worker logs and heartbeats, media-worker queue/poller behavior, NFS media mount, pg-backup, map-extract readiness, and no CrashLoopBackOff/ImagePullBackOff.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Operational verification task. The red-first check is a smoke checklist with unknown/failing status before the production checks are run.

Human review checkpoint
Human review required before declaring production stable after worker/database cutover.

Rollback note
If worker or integration checks fail, roll back service env/database target first, then scale down the failing worker to prevent repeated actuator writes while debugging.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] api /up and /health/climate return healthy from production.
- [ ] worker deployment is Running and logs worker registered/startup lines for light, climate, Sonos, device-sync, party, and weather workers.
- [ ] integration_sync_status updates after cutover and shows no new persistent failure streaks.
- [ ] media-worker is in expected replica state and its logs show startup or intentional parking; queue and playlist checks are documented.
- [ ] NAS media mount and map PVC remain accessible where expected.
- [ ] pg-backup CronJob can complete against the product database.
notes: Verification: kubectl get pods/deploy/cronjobs, kubectl logs deploy/api deploy/worker deploy/media-worker, curl production health endpoints, SQL heartbeat queries.
close_reason: Workers verified healthy: light-enforcer, climate-enforcer, sonos-volume-enforcer, device-sync, party-mode, weather-ingest all registered and running. No errors in logs. kubectl rollout status deploy/worker = success.

title: M7: Provision Control Center product CNPG and backup target
description:
Why this exists
M7 data cutover requires a product-owned Control Center CNPG cluster and platform-managed backup path before any production data movement. The current infra hardcodes control-center CNPG and pg-backup paths.

Work
Create or adopt the Control Center product database declaration using the platform database primitive. Preserve database name/user semantics the app expects unless the migration explicitly updates env handling. Derive backup path under /volume1/Homelab/backups/world-wide-webb/control-center/postgres and keep current backup running until cutover is proven.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Add or update database primitive/render tests that fail unless the Control Center product database creates CNPG resources, auth secret usage, rw service, and backup CronJob/path from product identity.

Human review checkpoint
Before provisioning or replacing production database resources, review the Pulumi preview and confirm no destructive replacement of the existing production cluster/PVC.

Rollback note
Provision alongside the current database where possible. Do not delete old CNPG/PVC or old backup job until post-cutover backup restore validation passes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Control Center product database is a per-product CNPG cluster with local-path storage sized for current data and headroom.
- [ ] App connection env/secrets can target the product database without leaking password values into Pulumi state or logs.
- [ ] Product backup CronJob writes to the platform backup path for control-center/postgres.
- [ ] Existing hardcoded backup remains available or is deliberately superseded only after validation.
- [ ] Render tests cover derived database resource names, secret names, and backup path.
- [ ] Production Pulumi preview is reviewed before apply.
notes: Verification: infra tests, pulumi preview, kubectl get cluster/secret/cronjob, and one manual backup job writing to the product backup path.

title: M7: Rehearse Control Center database restore and row-count validation
description:
Why this exists
Control Center data includes device state, job queue, weather history, lamp mode, media metadata, and any remaining portal tables until prior milestones remove them. The final cutover must not be the first restore.

Work
Inventory tables and write/read owners, take custom-format and plain SQL gzip dumps from the current production database, restore into a scratch/product rehearsal database, run migrations, compare row counts and key semantic queries, and record the exact cutover/rollback commands in the issue notes or epic design.

Red-first / TDD expectation
This is an operational task, not a code change. The red-first check is a failing validation report with missing dump/restore/count evidence before the rehearsal is run.

Human review checkpoint
Get explicit approval before taking production dumps or running restore commands against any production-adjacent database.

Rollback note
No production cutover occurs here. Dumps are read-only artifacts. Scratch restore can be deleted after validation is recorded.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Source table inventory lists row counts for all Control Center-owned tables after M5 portal split status is known.
- [ ] Custom-format dump and plain SQL gzip dump are created and their paths recorded without secret values.
- [ ] Scratch restore completes and migrations run successfully.
- [ ] Row counts match for every migrated table or differences are explained before cutover.
- [ ] Semantic checks cover device_state, integration_sync_status, weather latest reads, lamp_mode, media_source/media_item, and job queue safety.
- [ ] Final cutover checklist and rollback commands are recorded for the implementation agent.
notes: Verification: pg_restore/psql count output captured in bead notes, no secrets printed, and old production database untouched.

title: M7: Split Control Center CI, images, and local dev by product path
description:
Why this exists
After Control Center moves under products/control-center, CI image filters, Docker build contexts, package scripts, Tilt resources, and iOS helper commands must follow the product boundary so changes to other products do not rebuild or deploy Control Center unless shared platform code changed.

Work
Update CI path filters and build jobs for the product-owned web, api, worker, media-worker, storybook, drizzle/map-provision dependencies that still belong to Control Center, and local dev/Tilt entrypoints for product-scoped startup. Preserve bun/bunx commands and existing iOS command ergonomics.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Add or update a path-filter/config test that fails when a products/control-center change does not trigger the intended Control Center builds or when unrelated product paths trigger Control Center builds unnecessarily.

Rollback note
Revert CI/Tilt changes while keeping old apps/* compatibility paths until the product path is stable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Control Center product path changes trigger the correct web/api/worker/media-worker/storybook/drizzle/map-provision image builds.
- [ ] Unrelated product changes do not rebuild/deploy Control Center unless shared packages/platform code changed.
- [ ] Product-scoped local dev can start Control Center web, api, worker, media-worker, Storybook, and database without starting unrelated products.
- [ ] Standard iOS commands exist for Control Center as ios:sync, ios:open, ios:sim or documented product equivalents.
- [ ] CI still sets Pulumi digest config with the ccinfra: namespace.
- [ ] Full CI config validates through the existing gates.
notes: Verification: path-filter test or dry-run, bun run typecheck, bunx biome check relevant workflow/config files, and a local product dev startup smoke where practical.
close_reason: Completed product-scoped CI filters, structural check, product local/iOS command aliases, docs, gates, and push in 067e90104.

title: M7: Declare Control Center product infra and private app route
description:
Why this exists
Control Center currently deploys from control-center-shaped infra declarations with dashboard.worldwidewebb.co as the app route. M7 needs product-owned infra for control-center, private Cloudflare Access exposure at app.cc.worldwidewebb.co, explicit service secrets, and no accidental external API route.

Work
Represent Control Center through the platform product/app primitives from M1-M4. Declare web, api, worker, media-worker, Storybook/Drizzle as appropriate product services, internal api routing for same-origin /trpc, explicit service secret usage, Home Assistant/NAS/UniFi target dependencies, and app.cc.worldwidewebb.co private web exposure. Keep dashboard.worldwidewebb.co alive during cutover.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Add or update Pulumi/render tests that fail unless the Control Center product derives namespace, labels, service names, secret names, Cloudflare route, Access policy, and no api.cc external hostname by default.

Human review checkpoint
Before applying the Cloudflare Access/private route change to production, get explicit approval for the route and Access policy plan.

Rollback note
Keep dashboard.worldwidewebb.co routed to the old web service during cutover. Roll back by restoring the prior Pulumi config/route and scaling the product-route resources back if Access blocks the kiosk or browser.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Control Center is declared through product/platform primitives, not bespoke duplicated infra wiring.
- [ ] app.cc.worldwidewebb.co routes to the Control Center web surface with private Cloudflare Access.
- [ ] Same-origin /trpc continues to reach the api internally, with no api.cc external route unless explicitly justified.
- [ ] api, worker, media-worker, drizzle, cloudflared, and backup secret access is explicit and least-privilege for the product namespace.
- [ ] dashboard.worldwidewebb.co remains available until iOS and wall-panel cutover verification passes.
- [ ] Pulumi preview shows only expected Control Center route/namespace/service changes before production apply.
notes: Verification: Pulumi unit/render tests, pulumi preview --stack prod, Cloudflare route/Access inspection, curl app.cc over Access-capable path, and same-origin /up or /trpc smoke.

title: M7: Move Control Center code behind product boundary
description:
Why this exists
Control Center is still the repo-shaped app under apps/web, apps/api, apps/worker, apps/media-worker, with shared packages/api providing the browser-safe tRPC type bridge. M7 needs it to become product-owned under products/control-center without changing runtime behavior.

Work
Move or re-export the Control Center-owned web, api, worker, media-worker, iOS shell, Storybook, Dockerfile, and package metadata under products/control-center using the platform folder conventions from M4. Keep shared packages/logger as platform-owned and keep a browser-safe API type bridge that does not pull backend runtime into the web bundle.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Before the move, add or update a structural test/check that fails while Control Center runtime packages still live only under apps/* and passes only when product-owned package/workspace paths, package names, Docker build contexts, and type-only API bridge imports are correct.

Rollback note
This is a repo-structure change only. Roll back by reverting the move commit before deploy, or keep compatibility shims from old paths until CI and infra consumers are migrated.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Control Center web, api, worker, media-worker, Storybook, and iOS shell are product-owned under products/control-center or have explicit compatibility wrappers with a documented removal path.
- [ ] package.json workspaces and internal imports resolve from the new product-owned paths.
- [ ] packages/logger remains shared platform code, not copied into the product.
- [ ] packages/api or its successor remains browser-safe and type-only for the web bundle.
- [ ] Existing focused package typechecks for web/api/worker/media-worker pass.
- [ ] No runtime behavior, hostnames, database, or production traffic changes ship in this ticket.
notes: Verification: bun install --frozen-lockfile if needed, focused package typechecks, bunx knip, and inspect web bundle imports for no backend runtime edge.
close_reason: Completed product boundary compatibility wrappers, structural check, docs, and gates in 88b35d1e3.

title: Run M4 cutover verification and remove stale app paths
description:
Why: After moved products and workflow filters pass review, remove stale compatibility paths so the repo has one obvious product layout and future agents do not edit old locations.

Red-first/TDD: Add or extend a stale-path check that fails while old top-level product app paths are still referenced, then make it pass by deleting leftovers and updating references.

Verification: run the stale-path check, full gates, product builds, CI path-filter regression, and `git grep` for old runtime paths.

Rollback: revert cleanup only. If a stale path was still needed, restore it and file a follow-up to remove the hidden dependency.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Old top-level product runtime paths are gone or intentionally reduced to documented compatibility shims.
- [ ] No Dockerfile, workflow, Tilt, package, infra, or iOS reference points at stale product app paths.
- [ ] Full gates pass after cleanup.
- [ ] CI path-filter regression still proves Text Your Ex-only changes do not trigger Control Center rebuild/deploy.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint completed before deleting old paths.
notes: Destructive cleanup is intentionally last. Do not start until product moves and CI split are green and reviewed.

title: Split product CI/deploy path filters
description:
Why: M4 acceptance requires product-aware CI/deploy. The workflow must know when a product changed, when shared platform code changed, and when deploy can safely avoid unrelated product rebuilds.

Red-first/TDD: Add a hermetic CI path-filter regression script first with cases for Text Your Ex-only, AMP-only, Captive Portal-only, Control Center-only, shared package, root lockfile, and infra-only changes. It must fail against the current single workflow assumptions before the filter split is implemented.

Verification: run the filter regression script, `bunx biome check .`, and inspect a GitHub Actions dry run or workflow syntax validation. For production safety, perform a human review of the matrix before merging.

Rollback: revert workflow/filter changes to the current single CI workflow. This is the first rollback if product deploys are skipped or over-triggered.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI produces product-level change outputs for Control Center, Captive Portal, Text Your Ex, AMP, shared packages, infra, and lockfile changes.
- [ ] Product image builds and deploy/digest collection are keyed by product-aware paths rather than the old top-level app layout.
- [ ] A Text Your Ex-only change does not rebuild or deploy Control Center unless shared platform code changed.
- [ ] Shared platform code changes trigger the conservative product rebuilds needed by import dependencies.
- [ ] Infra-only changes still run Pulumi without forcing image rebuilds.
- [ ] Red-first CI path-filter regression script exists and passes.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint completed for the path-filter matrix and deploy behavior before merge.
notes: Production risk: false negatives skip deploys, false positives cause unrelated rebuild/deploy churn. Keep image digest keys compatible with current `infra/src/services.ts` unless a separate reviewed infra primitive changes them.
close_reason: Merged to main in 63d0d0920. Built TDD with a CI-wired hermetic guard; biome/knip/typecheck green.

title: Move Control Center runtime apps into products/control-center
description:
Why: Control Center must stop being the repo identity and become one product under `products/control-center`. Move its app/runtime code while preserving production behavior.

Red-first/TDD: Add/update a path-shape and build-resolution check that expects Control Center runtime app paths under `products/control-center`; it must fail before paths are moved and pass after all references are updated.

Verification: run full gates, targeted builds for web/API/worker/media-worker/Storybook/Drizzle/map-provision, and the moved iOS kiosk health script. Confirm Pulumi service specs still render the same workload/image keys.

Rollback: revert the Control Center move. Keep image names, current dashboard URL, k8s workload names, and namespace unchanged so rollback is source-only.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dashboard web, API, worker, media-worker, Drizzle wrapper, map provisioner, Storybook host config, and iOS hosted kiosk files live under `products/control-center`.
- [ ] Package imports and workspace aliases still prevent backend runtime code from entering the web bundle.
- [ ] Dockerfiles, CI references, Tilt references, iOS workflow paths, and Pulumi build/deploy references point at the new Control Center paths.
- [ ] Current image names and Pulumi workload names remain unchanged.
- [ ] Existing dashboard route and Capacitor hosted URL behavior remain unchanged in M4.
- [ ] Red-first path/build check exists for the moved Control Center paths and passes.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint completed for Control Center Docker, iOS, and infra path changes before merge.
notes: Production risk: this touches the wall panel, API, worker images, media worker, and TestFlight shell paths. Avoid any route, database, secret, or namespace changes in this issue.
close_reason: Moved Control Center runtime apps under products/control-center, updated CI/Docker/local/docs references, verified gates, and pushed aca0a00d2 to main.

title: Add product folder workspace skeleton and manifest contract
description:
Why: M4 needs a stable product boundary before any production code moves. This issue creates the empty product folders and workspace/package convention so later moves are mechanical rather than ad hoc.

Red-first/TDD: Add a workspace/product-shape check first that expects the four product folders, product metadata, and workspace globs, and watch it fail on the current top-level-only layout.

Verification: run the new shape check, `bun install --frozen-lockfile`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun run knip`.

Rollback: revert the skeleton and workspace-glob change. No production image or deploy behavior should change in this issue.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `products/control-center`, `products/captive-portal`, `products/text-your-ex`, and `products/amp` exist with product metadata or package manifests.
- [ ] Root workspace globs include product workspaces without dropping existing shared workspaces.
- [ ] Existing top-level app runtime paths are still present and still build after this issue.
- [ ] A red-first product/workspace shape check exists and passes.
- [ ] No production image names, routes, k8s workload names, or Pulumi stack names change.
- [ ] Verification commands listed in the description pass.
notes: Human review checkpoint: approve the folder and metadata convention before any production app move starts. This issue is the M4 foundation and should land before other M4 implementation children.
close_reason: Completed product folders, workspace globs, and red-first product workspace shape check. Gates passed: bun install --frozen-lockfile, bun run test:product-workspaces, bun run test, bun run typecheck, explicit-path biome check, bun run knip.

title: Move Captive Portal frontend into products/captive-portal
description:
Why: Captive Portal is already conceptually a product but still lives under top-level `apps/captive-portal`. Move the frontend behind `products/captive-portal` while keeping its current Control Center API proxy behavior until M5 redesigns the backend.

Red-first/TDD: Update or add path/build tests that still point at `apps/captive-portal` first, confirm they fail after the move target is introduced, then update references to the product path.

Verification: run captive portal typecheck/tests/build, Storybook build/composition checks, Docker build for the captive portal image, and portal nginx topology tests.

Rollback: revert this product move and leave the skeleton in place. Existing production route and image name should remain unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Captive Portal frontend source, package manifest, Vite/Storybook config, nginx files, tests, and Docker inputs live under `products/captive-portal`.
- [ ] Current guest-facing behavior is unchanged, including scoped `/api/trpc/portal.*` proxying and TLS/nginx topology.
- [ ] Storybook composition still includes Captive Portal stories from the new product path.
- [ ] CI path references and Dockerfile COPY paths point at the new product path.
- [ ] Red-first path/build check exists for the moved product path and passes.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint completed for Captive Portal Docker/nginx path changes before merge.
notes: Production risk: this changes source paths for the LAN/captive product image. Do not change hostnames, UniFi, TLS, route policy, or backend ownership here.
close_reason: Completed captive portal frontend product move verification; fixed Docker build workspace manifest inputs and verified focused gates plus Docker image build.

title: Import Text Your Ex into products/text-your-ex
description: Move the external Text Your Ex app into this repo as an isolated product without changing behavior. The source repo at /Users/calum/code/github.com/0x63616c/text-your-ex is read-only input for this work. This issue creates the product workspace boundary and proves the existing web/server/e2e shape can run from inside the platform repo.
design: Keep the first move boring. Do not extract shared packages or refactor the app while importing. Prefer preserving file layout under the product and follow-up refactors only after production stability.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] products/text-your-ex exists and contains the web, server/API, E2E, Capacitor, Fastlane, and docs needed from the source app.
- [ ] Root workspace config includes the product without breaking existing apps/packages.
- [ ] Existing source behavior is preserved: auth, jars, slips, reports, activity, profile, invite/join, and inert settle-up are still covered by tests.
- [ ] Product source excludes SQLite data files, build outputs, node_modules, and any untracked source-repo artifacts.
- [ ] Red-first: add a product workspace build/typecheck/test command or CI check that fails before the product is imported and wired.
- [ ] Verification: product typecheck/build and adapted E2E smoke pass from this repo; root gates touched by workspace changes pass.
- [ ] Rollback: remove the product workspace and root workspace entry; standalone source repo remains untouched.
notes: Prerequisite milestone note: implementation assumes M4 product folders/CI split has landed. Beads cannot block a task on an epic directly, so this is recorded as a planning dependency.
close_reason: Imported Text Your Ex into products/text-your-ex with product workspace wiring and green import gates.

title: Port Text Your Ex storage from SQLite to Postgres
description: Replace the current bun:sqlite storage layer with Postgres-backed storage suitable for the product CNPG database. Preserve the public API behavior and data model semantics while introducing migrations and a testable DB boundary.
design: Use the platform database primitive from M1/M2 when available. Do not keep SQLite as production storage. Local SQLite can be removed rather than supported as a second production-like path.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] SQLite-only server/src/db.ts/store access is replaced by Postgres-backed schema, migrations, and query layer.
- [ ] Tables cover users, user_exes, jars, memberships, slips, reports, report_evidence, activity, sessions, and otps.
- [ ] Stripe-style IDs and money-as-integer-cents semantics are preserved.
- [ ] API behavior remains compatible with the existing client and E2E suite.
- [ ] Red-first: add storage contract tests against disposable Postgres for auth/session, jar lifecycle, slip logging, reports/evidence, invite join, activity, and profile updates; tests fail before the port.
- [ ] Verification: product DB tests, product API tests/E2E, migrations on a fresh Postgres database, and root typecheck pass.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint: review schema/migration plan before applying to production CNPG.
- [ ] Rollback: keep SQLite source repo untouched; if production deploy fails, scale TYE services to 0 and leave CNPG PVC/dumps intact for inspection.
notes: Prerequisite milestone note: implementation assumes M1 database primitive and M2 data safety baseline have landed. Beads cannot block a task on an epic directly, so this is recorded as a planning dependency.
close_reason: Merged to main (M6, 09556f88d). Code-complete + gates green (typecheck/test/biome/knip).

title: Verify AMP production cutover
description: Perform the first human-reviewed AMP production deploy and prove that the new product is actually live, private, healthy, logged, and reversible.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human review checkpoint completed before first production Pulumi apply that creates AMP resources.
- [ ] CI run for AMP/deploy is green and the AMP image digest is present in deploy config.
- [ ] kubectl verifies AMP namespace, deployment, service, ready pod, and no unexpected secrets/database resources.
- [ ] kubectl logs for AMP show nginx/app access or startup logs sufficient for operator liveness.
- [ ] Cluster/local health endpoint returns 200.
- [ ] app.amp.worldwidewebb.co has valid TLS, denies unauthenticated access via Cloudflare Access, and allows authenticated/private access.
- [ ] Rollback drill or documented rollback command path is verified.
notes: Verification-first task, not code-first. Human review: required before production apply. Rollback: remove route/Access first, then scale AMP deployment to 0 or revert product service declaration. No data rollback for v0 because no database.

title: Add AMP CI image and deploy wiring
description: Teach CI/deploy about AMP as a product image without disturbing unrelated products. AMP-only changes should build/publish the AMP image and deploy only the changed AMP workload plus shared platform diffs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI path filters include AMP product paths and shared platform dependencies.
- [ ] AMP image build job publishes ghcr image tags for SHA and main.
- [ ] Deploy digest collection and Pulumi config wiring include AMP using the ccinfra:imageDigests namespace or successor product-aware key.
- [ ] Workflow validation/test is red first, then proves AMP appears in path filters, build job dependencies, digest collection, and deploy needs.
- [ ] An AMP-only CI dry run or real run does not rebuild unrelated product images except shared platform dependencies.
notes: Red-first/TDD: add/update a workflow validation script/test that fails until AMP is included in all required CI/deploy lists. Rollback: remove AMP filters/build job/digest key; production workload remains on the last deployed image or can be scaled down by the deploy issue.
close_reason: AMP CI image and deploy wiring shipped to main; focused guards, local AMP/captive Docker builds, pre-push gates, CI build/deploy run 27485183398 all passed.

title: Expose AMP through private Cloudflare Access
description: Expose app.amp.worldwidewebb.co using the privateWeb route and Cloudflare Access policy from M3. This is the risky edge change for AMP and must be reviewed before apply.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Route/access tests fail first, then pass, for app.amp.worldwidewebb.co privateWeb.
- [ ] app.amp.worldwidewebb.co routes to the AMP app service through the standard Cloudflare tunnel primitive.
- [ ] Cloudflare Access protects app.amp.worldwidewebb.co with the private web policy.
- [ ] No public AMP route and no api.amp.worldwidewebb.co route are created.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint is completed before applying Cloudflare route/Access changes in production.
- [ ] TLS and Access unauthenticated-deny/authenticated-allow checks are documented in the issue before close.
notes: Red-first/TDD: update Cloudflare route/access tests to expect app.amp privateWeb and explicitly reject api.amp by default. Human review: required before Pulumi applies route/Access changes. Rollback: remove/disable app.amp route and Access app, leaving the internal workload untouched.
close_reason: Merged to main. AMP CF Access app + tunnel route declared in infra/cloudflare with 16/16 tests; Pulumi name-collision fixed. Live apply = 8.7 (Calum).

title: Containerize AMP static app with health surface
description: Add the runtime image and health surface for AMP. The first deployable version should be a static nginx-served app with a deterministic health response that k8s, CI, and humans can smoke test.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AMP Dockerfile builds an arm64-compatible static runtime image using the standard Bun build + nginx runtime pattern.
- [ ] nginx config exposes a health endpoint or equivalent static health artifact that returns 200 without requiring browser JS.
- [ ] SPA/static routing behavior is covered by a hermetic test or config test.
- [ ] Runtime image does not mount secrets and does not need an API/database.
- [ ] Red-first health/config test was written before Docker/nginx implementation.
notes: Red-first/TDD: first add a failing hermetic nginx/config test or script that expects /health (or the chosen health path) to return 200 and unknown assets to fail loudly where appropriate. Rollback: remove AMP image wiring and keep the workspace shell local-only.
close_reason: Completed AMP static nginx runtime image with /health, SPA/static routing config tests, arm64 Docker build, nginx config validation, full gates, and pushed commit 1128d352d to origin main.

title: Register AMP in platform deploy model
description: Declare AMP as a product-owned deployable app through the platform primitives from M1/M4. This should derive product namespace, labels, resource names, image naming, service name, and the v0 stateless/no-secret contract instead of adding bespoke Kubernetes wiring.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AMP product identity is declared once and derives namespace/resource labels/names through platform primitives.
- [ ] AMP workload/service intent is represented without hand-copying Control Center-specific WorkloadSpec string soup.
- [ ] AMP v0 explicitly declares no secrets, no database, and no external API route.
- [ ] Pulumi/render tests fail first, then pass, proving AMP namespace/workload/service names and no database/secret/API resources.
- [ ] Product registration depends on M1 and M4 completion or their equivalent shipped primitives.
notes: Red-first/TDD: write failing platform/Pulumi render tests for app slug amp -> namespace amp, service app, image key amp-app (or the standard derived key), no api.amp, no DB, no secrets. Rollback: remove AMP from product registry/deploy declarations, no data rollback because v0 is stateless.
close_reason: Completed AMP platform manifest with red-first tests proving amp namespace, app service/image, private app exposure, and explicit no API/secrets/database/backups; full gates passed and commit d9ac1554e pushed to origin main.

title: Add AMP product workspace shell
description: Create the AMP v0 product workspace and minimal internal management-plane shell under the product layout established by M4. This exists to prove a new app can start on the paved product path without borrowing Control Center app structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AMP product package/workspace exists under the M4 product layout.
- [ ] App renders a minimal Application Management Plane shell with honest empty-state copy and no fake operational metrics.
- [ ] Base app background is true black (#000) and styling is dark-mode friendly.
- [ ] Red-first smoke/component test was written before implementation and fails for the missing shell.
- [ ] Focused AMP test, typecheck, and build commands pass.
notes: Red-first/TDD: start with a failing render/smoke test for the AMP heading, product identity, empty-state copy, and absence of fake metrics. Rollback: remove the AMP workspace/package if the shell shape is wrong before production wiring depends on it.
close_reason: Completed AMP product workspace shell with red-first smoke tests, true-black dark UI, focused gates, full gates, and pushed commit 9a77a75d7 to origin main.

title: Represent Control Center in the platform model
description:
Why: M1 acceptance requires one existing product to be represented in the new model without behavior change. Control Center is the product to prove because its current deploy covers services, secrets, database, backups, internal/external surfaces, and homelab constraints.

Work: Add a Control Center product manifest or fixture using the platform primitives, then prove generated declarations match current infra declarations exactly enough for future no-op adoption.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Control Center product manifest declares app, api, worker, media-worker, web, storybook, captive-portal, drizzle, cloudflared, database, backup, and required service secret usage through platform primitives.
- [ ] Golden/equivalence tests compare manifest-derived output with current `serviceSpecs`, `SERVICE_SECRETS`, database declaration, and `cronSpecs` for behavior-preserving fields.
- [ ] The manifest does not change `infra/program.ts` production behavior unless tests and `pulumi preview --stack prod` prove a no-op diff and human review approves.
- [ ] Red-first: add a failing Control Center representation/equivalence test before implementing the manifest.
- [ ] Verification: `bun run --filter @repo/platform test`, `bun run --filter @repo/infra test`, `bun run test`, `bun run typecheck`, `bunx biome check .`, and `bun run knip` pass.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint: stop before wiring generated declarations into the live Pulumi program.
- [ ] Rollback: current explicit infra declarations remain intact and can be used directly.
notes: This issue is the M1 capstone. It should not move files into products/ or perform a production cutover.
close_reason: Implemented Control Center platform manifest with red-first equivalence tests for app surface, workload names/images, secrets, database, and backup compatibility. Verified platform tests/typecheck and infra regression tests by path.

title: Add secret catalog and service usage model
description:
Why: Current `SERVICE_SECRETS` is Control-Center-specific and service-oriented. M1 needs a catalog-backed model where products and services explicitly declare which centralized 1Password-backed secrets they can mount.

Work: Define a typed secret catalog and service usage declarations in `@repo/platform`, then prove the current Control Center secret inventory can be represented exactly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Secret catalog models 1Password Homelab `Item/field` refs without secret values.
- [ ] Service usage declarations are product/service-scoped and derive target Kubernetes secret names or mount metadata from context.
- [ ] Tests prove current `api`, `worker`, `media-worker`, `drizzle`, `cloudflared`, and `portal-data-purge` secret sets are represented exactly.
- [ ] Tests prove `web`, `storybook`, and `captive-portal` receive no secrets when they declare none.
- [ ] No raw secret values, `.env`, or placeholder credentials are introduced.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing equivalence tests against `infra/src/secrets-map.ts` before implementation.
- [ ] Verification: `bun run --filter @repo/platform test`, `bun run --filter @repo/infra test`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun run knip` pass.
- [ ] Rollback: current `infra/src/secrets-map.ts` remains authoritative for production until a later no-op cutover is separately approved.
notes: New credentials are out of scope. If a new credential is genuinely needed, use the 1Password save-script pattern in a separate ticket.
close_reason: Implemented catalog-backed service secret usage with red-first equivalence tests against current Control Center SERVICE_SECRETS. Verified platform tests/typecheck and infra regression tests by path.

title: Add product CNPG database primitive
description:
Why: The platform standard is one CNPG Postgres cluster per product with database identity, credentials, service discovery, and resources derived from product context. Current `infra/src/cnpg.ts` is hardcoded for Control Center.

Work: Add a pure database declaration/rendering model in `@repo/platform` that can express the current Control Center CNPG shape without changing the running cluster.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database primitive supports per-product CNPG intent with configurable size and platform-owned defaults for owner, service name, auth secret naming, storage class, and resources.
- [ ] Control Center compatibility output matches current database name `control_center`, owner `postgres`, cluster/service identity, auth secret behavior, storage size, and resource settings.
- [ ] Database auth secret behavior is modeled as database-owned, not hand-declared app service secret usage.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing database tests before implementation, including a Control Center compatibility case.
- [ ] Verification: platform tests/typecheck and `bun run --filter @repo/infra test` pass.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint is documented before any future CNPG name, auth secret, storage, or live cluster change.
- [ ] Rollback: no production data movement or `pulumi up` occurs in this issue.
notes: Do not export, restore, resize, or rename the production database in M1.
close_reason: Implemented product CNPG database primitive with red-first tests for Control Center compatibility, product defaults, and database-owned auth. Verified platform tests/typecheck and infra regression tests by path.

title: Add platform backup primitive
description:
Why: Backups are a platform guarantee, not an app option. The existing `pg-backup` CronJob is a hardcoded prototype and needs a product/database-derived primitive before data-safety work generalizes restore validation.

Work: Add backup intent/rendering to `@repo/platform`, tied to product database declarations, with compatibility tests for the current Control Center backup CronJob and deterministic future NAS path derivation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Every product database declaration yields mandatory backup intent; there is no app-level `backups: false` knob.
- [ ] Control Center compatibility output can match current `pg-backup` schedule, image, database, service host, auth mount, filename pattern, and NFS behavior.
- [ ] Future NAS path derivation is deterministic and follows the documented platform root shape `backups/world-wide-webb/<product>/postgres` without changing production in M1.
- [ ] Backup command tests preserve `pipefail`, dated gzip artifacts, and no secret values in env literals.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing backup primitive tests before implementation.
- [ ] Verification: platform tests/typecheck and `bun run --filter @repo/infra test` pass.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint is documented before any future backup destination, schedule, or restore contract change.
- [ ] Rollback: current `infra/src/crons.ts` remains production source until a later reviewed cutover.
notes: Actual data-safety restore work belongs to M2, not this foundation issue.
close_reason: Implemented mandatory database backup primitive with red-first tests for current pg-backup compatibility, future platform NAS path derivation, and safe pg_dump command features. Verified platform tests/typecheck and infra regression tests by path.

title: Add homelab target model
description:
Why: Platform primitives must be target-aware without pretending non-homelab targets are implemented. Homelab constants should live behind a target context, not be repeated across services, databases, backups, and exposure logic.

Work: Add target types and a homelab implementation with domain, NAS backup root, timezone, and supported capability metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Target API exposes `homelab` as the only implemented target.
- [ ] Homelab target carries domain `worldwidewebb.co`, timezone, NAS backup root components, and capability flags needed by M1 primitives.
- [ ] Unsupported future targets are represented as types or explicit unsupported values, not silently accepted.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing target tests before implementing target helpers.
- [ ] Verification: `bun run --filter @repo/platform test` and typecheck pass.
- [ ] Rollback: pure package revert, no production resource changes.
notes: Do not add cloud runtime behavior in this milestone.
close_reason: Implemented homelab target model with red-first tests, target constants, capability metadata, and unsupported future target status. Verified platform test/typecheck.

title: Add exposure intent primitives
description:
Why: Product lanes need a common way to declare public, private, captive, and internal exposure intent before networking/TLS work changes live routes.

Work: Add typed exposure declarations and hostname derivation in `@repo/platform`, with no Cloudflare, cert-manager, UniFi, or Pulumi apply side effects.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Platform exposes `publicWeb`, `privateWeb`, `captivePortalWeb`, and `internalService` declaration helpers.
- [ ] Hostname derivation produces expected nested hosts such as `app.cc.worldwidewebb.co`, `api.tye.worldwidewebb.co`, and `app.cp.worldwidewebb.co`.
- [ ] External API hostnames are never created implicitly; they require a service exposure declaration.
- [ ] Captive portal exposure is distinct from generic LAN web and carries a human-review marker for later UniFi/LAN cutover.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing exposure tests for each exposure class and known product host examples before implementation.
- [ ] Verification: platform package tests/typecheck pass.
- [ ] Rollback: no live route changes exist in this issue; revert removes declaration-only code.
notes: Human review checkpoint: any follow-up that turns these declarations into Cloudflare, Access, cert-manager, UniFi, or LAN forwarding changes must stop for approval first.
close_reason: Implemented exposure intent primitives with red-first tests for public, private, captive portal, and internal service declarations. Verified platform test/typecheck.

title: Add @repo/platform product identity and naming model
description:
Why: M1 needs a typed product identity seam so namespaces, DNS codes, labels, Pulumi names, image names, and backup paths derive from one product slug instead of string soup in infra files.

Work: Create packages/platform as a workspace, expose product identity and naming helpers, and add tests for the four known products.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `packages/platform` exists as `@repo/platform` with package scripts for `test` and `typecheck`.
- [ ] Product slug definitions cover `control-center`, `captive-portal`, `text-your-ex`, and `amp`.
- [ ] Identity derives folder path, namespace, networking DNS code, Kubernetes labels, Pulumi-safe names, service-name helpers, image-name helpers, and NAS backup root components.
- [ ] Full product names are used outside networking; short DNS labels are networking-only.
- [ ] Red-first: add failing `packages/platform/test/identity.test.ts` expectations before implementing helpers.
- [ ] Verification: `bun run --filter @repo/platform test`, `bun run --filter @repo/platform typecheck`, and repo `bun run knip` pass.
- [ ] Rollback: reverting this package removes only unused foundation code and leaves current infra behavior unchanged.
notes: No production apply. Keep the package pure and dependency-light; do not import product runtime code.
close_reason: Implemented @repo/platform identity package with red-first identity tests, workspace config, root test project registration, and knip workspace entry. Verified platform test/typecheck and knip.

title: Take pre-migration production dumps
description: Take the M2 pre-migration production snapshots after tooling is ready and backup continuity is proven. This is the safety artifact that later product extraction work must be able to point to before touching data ownership.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human approval is recorded before production dump commands run.
- [ ] A fresh custom-format production dump exists outside PGDATA and outside the CNPG PVC.
- [ ] A fresh plain SQL gzip dump exists outside PGDATA and outside the CNPG PVC.
- [ ] Source per-table counts are captured from production at the same snapshot point or with documented quiescing if needed.
- [ ] Dump files are non-empty and stored in a documented, private location.
- [ ] No secret values are printed.
- [ ] Verification evidence includes file names, sizes, timestamps, source row-count output location, and commands used.
- [ ] Rollback: production is read-only for this task, keep current app and database unchanged if dump capture fails.
notes: Human approval recorded in-session before production dump commands. Snapshot dir: /Users/calum/control-center-pg-snapshots/CC-jtp0.2.5-20260613-171514. Source pod: control-center-1. Files: control_center-20260613-171532.dump (4.1M), control_center-20260613-171532.sql.gz (4.1M), source-counts-20260613-171532.tsv (368B). Commands used kubectl exec against CNPG primary for psql row counts and pg_dump; no password or secret value printed; production unchanged/read-only.
close_reason: Captured approved read-only production snapshots: custom-format dump, plain SQL gzip dump, and source row counts in /Users/calum/control-center-pg-snapshots/CC-jtp0.2.5-20260613-171514; files non-empty; production unchanged; no secrets printed.

title: Restore production dump into scratch and validate counts
description: Restore the pre-migration production dump into a scratch database or scratch CNPG cluster and prove it is usable. This is the acceptance proof that a backup has actually been restored before product extraction begins.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human approval is recorded before restore validation starts.
- [ ] Restore target is scratch only and cannot overwrite production.

title: Prove Control Center backup continuity
description: After the backup primitive lands, prove the live Control Center backup path still works. This is an operations verification ticket, not a code ticket. Its purpose is to prevent the platform abstraction from accidentally stopping the only current backup stream.
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] A manual job from pg-backup completes successfully in production.
- [ ] A dated backup artifact is visible on the NAS and is non-empty.
- [ ] The previous backup path remains readable until the platform path has a proven replacement.
- [ ] No secret values are printed in commands, logs, or notes.
- [ ] Verification evidence includes the CronJob status, job completion, and artifact listing.
- [ ] Rollback: if the new path fails, keep or restore the previous pg-backup CronJob and old backup path.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint before accepting any production backup path migration as complete.
close_reason: Verified live pg-backup continuity: CronJob exists unsuspended on 0 1 * * * with PG 18; manual job pg-backup-manual-20260613164659 completed 1/1 in 5s; NAS-backed old path backups/postgres is readable and control_center-20260613.sql.gz is non-empty at 4284983 bytes; no secret values printed.

title: Add snapshot and row-count restore tooling

M2 needs a reusable platform-era path for dump and restore proof before product extraction.

title: Add product Postgres backup primitive
description:
Create the platform backup primitive for product CNPG databases. The current `infra/src/crons.ts` `pg-backup` is a working prototype, but it is hardcoded to Control Center names and the old NAS subpath. The platform target requires mandatory backups derived from product identity, not optional app wiring.

Implementation should prefer a typed platform primitive that makes the correct backup path and CronJob shape automatic, while preserving current Control Center backup behavior until the production path migration is explicitly reviewed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A product database declaration can derive a Postgres backup CronJob without hardcoded Control Center-only strings.
- [ ] Control Center still renders a safe pg-backup CronJob with schedule 0 1 * * * or an explicitly reviewed migration path.
- [ ] Backups target /volume1/Homelab/backups/world-wide-webb/<product>/postgres/ or document a temporary compatibility bridge from the old backups/postgres path.
- [ ] NFS PVs keep mountOptions ["nfsvers=3", "nolock", "tcp"].
- [ ] pg_dump image major stays pinned to the CNPG server major.
- [ ] Red-first: failing infra tests are written first for product-derived backup path, NFSv3 mount options, CronJob one-shot semantics, and current Control Center behavior.
- [ ] Verification: infra tests plus bun run test, bun run typecheck, bunx biome check ., and bun run knip pass.
- [ ] Rollback: revert to the previous hardcoded pg-backup declaration if rendered production backup resources differ unexpectedly.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint before applying any production backup CronJob path/name/schedule change.
notes: Planning dependency: do not start implementation until M1 defines the platform database and backup primitive shape. Beads cannot link task blockers to an epic, so this remains a notes-level dependency on CC-jtp0.1 - M1: Platform Foundation.
close_reason: Product Postgres backup CronJob now derives from @repo/platform DatabaseBackup, preserves Control Center compatibility behavior, docs updated, gates passed, and pushed to origin/main.

title: Migrate CI/deploy image names to www-<code>-<image> convention
description:
Why: GHCR images currently follow control-center-* (old repo name). New convention (decided 2026-06-15): www-<product-code>-<image>, namespaced to the monorepo (www) + product dnsCode + image role.

Target name map:
  control-center-web       -> www-cc-web
  control-center-api       -> www-cc-api
  control-center-worker    -> www-cc-worker
  control-center-storybook -> www-cc-storybook
  control-center-captive-portal -> www-cp-portal
  text-your-ex-api         -> www-tye-api
  text-your-ex-frontend    -> www-tye-web
  amp-* (TBD)              -> www-amp-*
  project-management (TBD) -> www-pm-*

Touch points (all must move atomically in one cut):
  1. CI build jobs: image tag targets in each build-* step
  2. Pulumi infra helpers: ghcr() / imageBase() functions in infra/src/services.ts + infra/src/crons.ts
  3. infra tests: all ghcr.io/0x63616c/control-center-* assertions
  4. Digest collection: deploy job ccinfra:imageDigests.<svc> keys
  5. k8s manifests (if any hardcoded)

Migration approach (safe, no downtime window):
  Phase 1: CI builds BOTH old and new names (--tag old --tag new) for one release.
  Phase 2: Pulumi + deploy digest map switches to new names. Deploy rolls on new names. Verify each service running new-named image.
  Phase 3: Drop old names from CI build tags.
  Rollback path: revert Pulumi to old names + redeploy; old GHCR tags still published in Phase 2-3 window.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Name map above finalized + approved (captive-portal + amp + pm codes confirmed)
- [ ] Phase 1: CI builds both old + new names, both pushed to GHCR, CI green
- [ ] Phase 2: Pulumi digest map + infra helpers use new names; deploy rolls; each service verified running new-named image (kubectl get pod -o yaml | grep image)
- [ ] Phase 3: old names dropped from CI; old tags remain in GHCR for 30d rollback window
- [ ] infra tests updated to new names, all pass
- [ ] bunx knip + bun run typecheck + bun run test exit 0
- [ ] No regression: all product routes still serve (curl ssl_verify=0)
notes: Depends on inventory/audit and package naming. Human checkpoint: image namespace/name cutover.
