comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
DISCOVERY (local UPnP/SOAP on port 1400, full read+write confirmed, no cloud/auth needed):

Devices (5 logical rooms, 6 units):
- Living Room - Sonos Beam (S31) @ .193
- Desk - STEREO PAIR of 2x Era 300 (S41) @ .152 (coord, LF) + .161 (RF, invisible/bonded)
- Bedroom - Era 300 (S41) @ .63
- Bathroom - Era 100 (S57) @ .149
- Kitchen - Era 100 SL (S62) @ .179

Topology: all currently in ONE group coordinated by Bedroom. Groups are dynamic.

Controllable surface (all tested working):
- RenderingControl: GetVolume/SetVolume, GetMute/SetMute (per device, Master channel)
- AVTransport: GetTransportInfo (PLAYING/PAUSED/STOPPED), Play/Pause/Stop/Next/Previous, GetPositionInfo (track title/artist/album/duration/position + AlbumArtURI)
- ZoneGroupTopology: GetZoneGroupState (groups+coordinators), SetAVTransportURI for group join/leave

Live state snapshot: vols 53-70, all idle/stopped except Bedroom paused. Nothing playing now.

Notes for tile design:
- Discovery = SSDP M-SEARCH (ST urn:schemas-upnp-org:device:ZonePlayer:1) -> device_description.xml for roomName/model.
- Must collapse bonded/invisible members (Desk RF) into their coordinator; show 5 rooms not 6.
- node-sonos / sonos-ts libs exist but raw SOAP is ~30 lines and dependency-free.
- Coordinator owns transport+now-playing for a group; volume is per-device.

Acceptance items 1-3 (enumerate / document surface / live control test) DONE.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
DISCOVERY pt.2 - Home Assistant is the better backbone (already wired into apps/api):

- HA client exists: apps/api/src/integrations/homeassistant/index.ts (ha singleton, REST + callService + renderTemplate). Reachable at homelab:8123, token = op://Homelab/Home Assistant Token/credential. Prod HA_URL=host.docker.internal:8123 (HA runs ON homelab).
- HA exposes 8 media_player entities unifying the whole house: all 5 Sonos rooms + Apple TV (media_player.living_room_tv) + HomePod + 'Evee Media player' (Spotify).
- KEY: HA reads Apple TV now-playing that raw Sonos SOAP CANNOT - app_name (YouTube/Netflix/etc), media_title, artist, position/duration, full source_list of installed apps. Verified live (caught a YouTube video title).
- Apple TV audio-output device is NOT exposed by HA; infer it by which media_player is 'playing' (HomePod/Beam idle => audio on TV speakers).
- Sonos control still available via HA callService(media_player, ...) OR raw SOAP. Raw SOAP gives deeper Sonos-only controls (EQ, line-in routing, TV-input grab, favorites, grouping).

LIVE-TESTED controls this session (all worked against real hardware):
- volume get/set (+restore), mute read, pause/stop, play/resume
- set source: Desk line-in (x-rincon-stream:RINCON_804AF28AAB20...)
- grab TV audio onto Beam: SetAVTransportURI x-sonos-htastream:RINCON_74CA60932558...:spdif + Play => Beam PLAYING TV (confirmed audible by Calum)
- read group topology; observed groups dissolve when TV turned on (Beam auto-pulls TV source) => tile MUST read topology fresh each poll, never cache grouping.

LEANING: tile = whole-house 'what's playing' media board on HA media_player domain (Sonos + AppleTV + HomePod unified), with Sonos-specific power actions (group, line-in, send-TV-to-Beam) via SOAP/HA services. Decision still open.

title: Add 2x2 'wall' tile border ring around the pannable world (31x31 → 35x35)
description:
Expand the bento world by one 2x2-tile-thick decorative wall ring on every edge, like a wall enclosing the dashboard.

Current: WORLD_COLS/ROWS = 31x31 (odd so the centered Clock lands on an integer cell). The whole world is already tiled edge-to-edge by the decorative bento fill in apps/web/src/lib/placeholder-tiles.ts (guillotine split, validated gap/overlap/sliver-free).

Change:
- grid-constants.ts: WORLD_COLS/ROWS 31 -> 35 (2-cell ring per side, +4 total, stays odd; CLUSTER offset auto-recenters from 13 to 15).
- placeholder-tiles.ts: shift the existing 87 inner tiles by WALL_THICKNESS (2) so they fill the inner 31x31 (cells 2..32, matching the recentred cluster), then add a generated 2-cell-thick ring of 2x2 tiles (one 3-wide/3-tall tile per band absorbs the odd cell since edges are 35/31 long). Same styling as bento (no new component).

Downstream (Board windowing/centering/snap, Minimap) derives from WORLD_W/H, auto-scales.
acceptance_criteria:
- WORLD_COLS === 35 && WORLD_ROWS === 35 in grid-constants.ts
- placeholderViolations() returns [] (placeholder-tiles.test.ts green): wall + shifted inner fully tile the 35x35 world, no gaps/overlaps/slivers, no cluster collision
- Wall ring is 2 cells thick on all 4 sides, predominantly 2x2 tiles
- bun run test green, bun run typecheck green, biome clean
- Visual: at 1366x1024 the board still centers on the Clock; panning to any edge reveals the 2x2 wall border (agent-browser screenshot)

title: Idle timeout: reset board to default home (clock) view after 60s inactivity
description:
After 60s of no user interaction (no touch/tap/pointer/scroll/key events on the 1366x1024 wall panel), the board should automatically navigate back to the default home view (the clock view). Any interaction resets the 60s idle timer. This keeps the panel showing the clock as the resting/default state when nobody is using it.

Implementation notes:
- Hook into the board's view/pan state (the scroll-snap pan modes / board navigation introduced in CC-t2gw).
- Listen for interaction events (pointerdown, touchstart, wheel/scroll, keydown) at the board root; debounce/reset a 60s timer on each.
- On timeout fire, animate/snap back to the default home (clock) view using the existing navigation mechanism (not a hard reload).
- Timer should not fire while already on the home view (no-op), and should pause/not navigate mid-interaction.
- 60s should be a single named constant (e.g. IDLE_RESET_MS) so it's tunable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single named constant defines the 60s idle window (e.g. IDLE_RESET_MS = 60_000)
- [ ] Interaction events (pointerdown/touchstart/wheel/scroll/keydown) on the board reset the idle timer
- [ ] After 60s with zero interaction while on a non-home view, the board navigates to the default home (clock) view via existing nav (no full page reload)
- [ ] Timer is a no-op when already on the home view
- [ ] Timer is cleaned up on unmount (no leaked intervals/timeouts)
- [ ] Unit test: fake timers verify navigation-to-home fires at 60s and is reset by a simulated interaction
- [ ] agent-browser screenshot at 1366x1024 shows the board returned to the clock home view after the idle window elapses

title: Coverage report-only: drop failing CI thresholds

Combined with CC-hjvu merged (unit+browser) coverage - which is slightly nondeterministic - the ratchet locks a high-water mark and a normal lower run fails CI ('functions 81.63% < 82.57%'). Per Calum: coverage % must be REPORTED (badge) but never fail a job/block deploy. Remove the thresholds block; keep include/exclude/reporters.
acceptance_criteria: vitest.config.ts has no coverage.thresholds; vitest --coverage exits 0 regardless of %; coverage-summary.json still produced and badge still published; CI test job fails only on real test failures, not coverage %

title: Board pan: native scroll-snap modes, trackpad jitter fix, A/C slider no longer pans board
description:
Mac trackpad panning was jittery: the hand-rolled JS SmoothDamp spring re-centered on every scrollend, but trackpad scroll fires no pointer events so the code couldn't tell the user was still scrolling and the spring fought live input. Also the A/C setpoint slider dragged the whole board (onPointerDown started a board pan on any mouse press incl. on inner controls; sliders had no touch-action). And the 'fly to center' feel + viewport-crop comments were stale/misleading.

Fix: replace the JS spring with native CSS scroll-snap (compositor-thread, identical across trackpad/touch/mouse, nothing to fight). Add a live on-device switcher (proximity/mandatory/none + legacy spring) persisted to localStorage so Calum can A/B the feel on Mac + iPad, winner to be locked in later. Suspend scroll-snap during mouse-drag so the drag doesn't fight per-frame re-snap. Guard onPointerDown against interactive targets + touch-action:none on .range/.range-thumb so slider drags adjust the slider, not the board. Correct the stale 'iPad crop' comments (the stage is full-window, not cropped).
acceptance_criteria:
- Trackpad pan is smooth on Mac (no spring fighting live scroll)
- Native scroll-snap modes selectable via on-device switcher, persisted to localStorage
- Mouse click-drag pan is smooth (snap suspended mid-drag, re-snaps on release)
- Dragging the A/C setpoint slider adjusts the slider and does NOT pan the board
- bun run typecheck clean; bunx biome check clean
- All apps/web tests pass (bun run test)
- Comments no longer claim a viewport crop that does not exist
- NOTE: switcher + 4 modes are intentionally retained until Calum picks the winning feel on iPad; follow-up ticket to strip the losers

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: Shipped in 1d4c149 (pushed to main). All fixes live: native scroll-snap pan, smooth Mac trackpad, mouse-drag suspends snap, A/C slider no longer pans the board, stale crop comments corrected. INTENTIONALLY left open: the 4-mode switcher stays until Calum picks the winning feel on iPad. Strip follow-up filed as CC-nj0w (blocked by this).

title: Unify board cell rendering: real tiles + placeholders into one render path
description:
Board.tsx renders real tiles (PLACED from TILE_REGISTRY) and decorative bento placeholders (BENTO_RECTS) as TWO separate filtered lists with TWO near-identical render loops. The positioning + scroll-snap-align + is-centered logic is duplicated across both, which is an active maintenance tax: adding scroll-snap-align required editing the same line in two places, and the two blocks can silently drift.

The centering layer already unifies them (CENTER_TARGETS merges both), so only the render is inconsistently split.

Goal: a single board-cell list the Board iterates ONCE. A cell is either a real tile (has a registry entry → interactive, mounts component + BoundedTile + click/keyboard/modal) or a placeholder (no entry → inert, pointerEvents:none). Shared geometry (position, size, scroll-snap-align, is-centered) written exactly once.

NOTE on 'single tile registry': placeholders genuinely lack component/label/gridArea and use world-absolute cell coords (vs real tiles' cluster-relative coords + deriveGridAreas/Storybook usage). So the unification is at the board-cell layer (one merged list consumed by the board), NOT by polluting TileRegistryEntry with optionals. TILE_REGISTRY stays the real-tile source; BENTO stays the placeholder source; they merge into one typed BoardCell[] the render consumes.

Paint order MUST be preserved: placeholders render under real tiles.
acceptance_criteria:
- Board iterates a SINGLE merged cell list (one .map); the separate `visible`/`visiblePlaceholders` lists and the two render loops are gone
- A cell's position + size + scrollSnapAlign + is-centered class is defined in exactly ONE place (shared by tile and placeholder)
- Placeholders stay non-interactive (pointerEvents:none) and never open a modal or recenter
- Paint order preserved: placeholders render beneath real tiles
- targetAt / centering still works (CENTER_TARGETS or equiv derived from the same single list)
- bun run typecheck clean; bunx biome check clean
- All existing apps/web Board tests pass (bun run test)
- agent-browser screenshot at 1366x1024 shows the board visually identical to before: centered real cluster + flush bento fill, no gaps/overlaps
close_reason: Shipped in 1d4c149. Board renders all cells via one BOARD_CELLS list + one map; verified (typecheck/biome/835 tests/screenshot).

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
Implemented + verified.

Board.tsx now renders every cell through ONE list (BOARD_CELLS) and ONE .map. A cell with an `entry` is an interactive tile; without one it's inert bento fill. Geometry (position/size/scrollSnapAlign/is-centered) written once and shared. Placeholders sort first → paint beneath. Removed: PLACED, CENTER_TARGETS, visible/visiblePlaceholders, the two render loops, the duplicated scroll-snap-align. cellAt() + Minimap tiles/ghosts derive from the same single list.

Verification:
- bun run typecheck: clean (all 4 workspaces)
- bunx biome check src/components/Board.tsx: clean
- bun run test: 835/835 pass
- agent-browser screenshot @1366x1024 (docs/screenshots/board-unified-cells.png): board visually identical - centered real cluster + flush bento fill, no gaps/overlaps, Clock centered highlight intact.

NOT yet committed: lives in the same working-tree Board.tsx as the in-flight snap-mode experiment (switcher + 4 modes), which Calum is still testing on-device. Will commit once the snap winner is picked and the switcher is stripped.

title: Dev environment reachable from iPad for live testing
description:
Stand up a way to hit a running dev build of control-center from the iPad (wall panel) for occasional hands-on testing, without deploying to prod. Today the only live target is prod (dashboard.worldwidewebb.co via the Swarm stack); there's no dev surface to poke at from the iPad.

Options to evaluate (cheapest/simplest first):
1. Tailscale-only (likely sufficient, ~zero cost): run the Tilt dev stack on the MacBook (or homelab), and hit it from the iPad over the tailnet at http://<device>:<port> (e.g. calums-macbook:5173 for web / :4201 api). Both iPad and MacBook are already on the tailnet (calums-ipad-pro, calums-macbook). Need: bind Vite/api dev servers to 0.0.0.0 (not just localhost) so they're reachable over the tailnet, and confirm MagicDNS name resolves. Possibly a Tailscale Serve to get HTTPS + a stable name.
2. A persistent 'dev' surface on homelab: a second Swarm stack / compose project (dev.worldwidewebb.co) tracking a dev branch or manual deploy, behind the existing Cloudflare tunnel. More setup, always-on.
3. Cloud dev box (Hetzner/etc.): only if local options prove insufficient. Cost + another machine to manage.

Decide the approach, implement the minimal viable one, and document it (host file table / README) so it's reproducible. Lean toward option 1 if it works.
design: Recommendation: start with option 1 (Tailscale + 0.0.0.0 bind, optionally Tailscale Serve for HTTPS). The board is a fixed 1366x1024 iPad Pro target, so testing on the actual device matters. Open question for Calum: should the dev surface track a branch automatically, or is manual 'run it on the macbook when I want to test' enough? The board's QueryClient + tiles assume same-origin /api proxy - verify the dev setup proxies /api like prod nginx does (Vite dev proxy), or the api calls break cross-origin.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented, repeatable way to open a live dev build of the web app on the iPad (calums-ipad-pro) over the tailnet, NOT prod.
- The web dev server binds to an address reachable over Tailscale (0.0.0.0 or Tailscale Serve), and /api requests proxy to the dev api (no CORS breakage).
- Verified by actually loading the board on the iPad at 1366x1024 and confirming at least one live tile renders against the dev api.
- The setup (commands / Tiltfile change / Tailscale Serve config) is written down in the repo (README or docs + host file table) so future sessions can reproduce it.
- Chosen approach + rejected alternatives recorded (decision note).

P2 = refactors+hardening+payoff libs. P3 = speculative/larger. Quick-win batch (all S/XS) clears every silent prod bug first.

title: Stabilize Storybook test suite + merge into CI coverage
description: The Storybook browser-test project (vitest --project storybook, Playwright/Chromium) has never run in CI and has rotted: full-suite run shows ~8 suites failing under parallel load (flaky; pass in isolation) + 4 real failures (Modal>Open, Tile>CssCheck, TeslaTileView>Populated/Charging). Goal: make the suite deterministic+green, then add it to root test:coverage so coverage is MERGED (unit jsdom + storybook chromium), wire Playwright into the CI test job, and regenerate the self-hosted coverage badge from the real (merged) number. Follow-on from CC-afz (badge currently shows undercounted 36.8% unit-only).
acceptance_criteria: bunx vitest --project storybook runs deterministically green (no flaky suite failures across repeated runs); 4 real failing stories fixed; root test:coverage merges unit+storybook coverage; CI test job installs Playwright chromium and runs merged coverage; .github/badges/coverage.json reflects merged number; deploy still gated on the test job; bun run test (unit) stays fast and Chromium-free

title: VariantSwitcher trapped behind modal: portal escapes #stage stacking context, switcher does not
description:
When a tile detail modal is open, the floating VariantSwitcher pill bar (which swaps between a tile's alternative modal variants) is unclickable/visually behind the modal.

Root cause: #stage (apps/web/src/components/Board.tsx:510) is position:fixed, which ALWAYS establishes a stacking context. The shared <Modal> portals to document.body (zIndex 100, body-level stacking context), but VariantSwitcher renders inside #stage via TileModalHost (Board.tsx:615) and is NOT portaled. Its zIndex:110 is scoped to #stage's context, so it can never beat the body-level Modal at 100. The Modal backdrop paints over the entire #stage subtree, burying the switcher.

Fix: portal VariantSwitcher to document.body (same as Modal) so its zIndex:110 shares the Modal's stacking context and actually layers above it.
acceptance_criteria:
- VariantSwitcher is rendered via createPortal to document.body
- With a multi-variant modal open, the VariantSwitcher pill bar is visible above the modal backdrop and its buttons are clickable (onSelect fires)
- Variant tabs switch the active modal variant when clicked
- Vitest: a test asserts the switcher is a body-level node, not nested under #stage, and that clicking a tab calls onSelect
- agent-browser screenshot at 1366x1024 shows the switcher pill bar on top of an open modal with a variant selectable
- bun run test, bun run typecheck, bunx biome check . all green

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
Fixed in eb791d9. VariantSwitcher now createPortal()s to document.body so its zIndex:110 shares the Modal's body-level stacking context and layers above the zIndex:100 backdrop. Added VariantSwitcher.test.tsx (4 tests) asserting the tablist portals out of its #stage-standin render parent + tab render/select. Gates green: 816 tests, typecheck, biome.

title: Board pans behind self-managed modals (ControlsTile expanded view)
description: React portals bubble events through the React tree, not the DOM. The shared Modal portals to <body> but is a React descendant of #stage, so a press on a modal's backdrop bubbles to #stage's onPointerDown and drives the board drag-pan. Board's freeze (overflow:hidden + modalOpenRef guard) is gated on activeModal only. ControlsTile manages its OWN modal state (ExpandedControlsModalView) and bypasses activeModal, so the board is fully draggable/scrollable behind it. Repro: open Controls > More, drag dim backdrop -> board pans (1910,1575 -> 1548,1242). Fix at the right layer: make the shared Modal the single source of truth for 'any modal open' via a ref-counted store; Board freezes whenever count>0, not just when activeModal is set.
acceptance_criteria: 1. With ANY modal open (incl. ControlsTile expanded view), #stage computed overflow is 'hidden' and touchAction 'none'. 2. agent-browser: open Controls>More, mouse-drag the dim backdrop -> #stage scrollLeft/scrollTop UNCHANGED. 3. Wheel over backdrop with that modal open -> scroll UNCHANGED. 4. New unit test covers the modal-open store ref-counting + Board freeze on a non-activeModal modal. 5. bun run test, typecheck, biome all green.
close_reason: Centralized 'any modal open' in a ref-counted store (modal-open-store.ts); shared Modal registers while open; Board freezes pan on it (OR'd with activeModal). Verified in-browser on local dev: Controls>More now sets #stage overflow:hidden/touchAction:none, mouse-drag + wheel behind it leave scroll unchanged; closing restores panning; activeModal path + variant switcher unaffected. 804 tests + typecheck + biome green.

title: Fix font flash (FOUT) on page load/reload
description: Every @font-face (Space Grotesk + Space Mono) uses font-display: swap, so the browser paints fallback (SF Pro/system-ui) then swaps to the web font on each load/reload - a visible font flash. No preload means the woff2 fetch starts only after CSS parses. Fix: self-host latin woff2 under public/fonts with stable paths, declare own @font-face with font-display: block (no swap to wrong font), and <link rel=preload> the primary fonts so the block window is imperceptible (instant on warm kiosk cache).
acceptance_criteria:
- theme.css no longer imports @fontsource swap CSS; declares @font-face with font-display: block
- Space Grotesk Variable + Space Mono 400/700 woff2 self-hosted under apps/web/public/fonts with stable /fonts/ paths
- index.html preloads space-grotesk + space-mono-400 woff2 (crossorigin)
- bun run typecheck + bun run build green
- agent-browser reload at 1366x1024 shows no font swap flash
close_reason: Self-hosted latin woff2 under public/fonts; @font-face now font-display: block (was swap); index.html preloads grotesk + mono-400. Verified: bun typecheck + build green, bundled CSS has 0 font-display:swap / 3 block, dist/fonts + 2 preload links present. The swap was the flash; block+preload removes it.

title: Retheme board to Vercel-black: pure neutral surfaces + vercel-blue accent

title: cmdUp/cmdSecrets concurrent op reads race on cold op daemon, corrupting config + failing the first deploy on a fresh agent
description: cmdUp (and cmdSecrets) resolve all secret refs with Promise.all -> concurrent 'op read' processes. On a FRESHLY-rolled bosun-agent the op daemon/config is uninitialized, and the concurrent first reads race on daemon/config init, corrupting /root/.config/op/config ('[ERROR] cannot read config at /root/.config/op/config: invalid JSON: unexpected end of JSON input'). That bosun up then throws at the secrets step and aborts BEFORE docker stack deploy, so nothing rolls. Intermittent: once the daemon is warm, single AND concurrent reads succeed (verified by exec test in the agent). This nearly masked the CC-czg proof (commit ddea6e1's first deploy failed this way; re-firing with a warm daemon worked). Fix: serialize op reads in OpProvider (instance-level promise chain) so the first read inits the daemon alone, then the rest proceed - or warm the daemon with one read before the Promise.all. Keep it at the provider layer so cmdUp/cmdSecrets/routes are all covered.
acceptance_criteria:
- OpProvider serializes op reads (no concurrent op invocations from one instance); unit test asserts reads do not overlap
- a deploy on a freshly-rolled agent completes secrets sync without the op config-corruption error
- bun run test + typecheck + biome green

title: Restart-safe cron scheduler: derive dedupe/overlap from swarm slot label (Option A)
description:
PROBLEM: scheduler dedupe (lastRunMinute) + overlap guard (inFlight) are in-memory (scheduler.ts), so an agent restart wipes them. A restart within a job's matching minute re-fires it (double run); a restart during a long run loses the in-flight guard.

DESIGN (Option A - minimal, keeps create-per-run):
1. Stamp each fired job service with --label bosun.cron-slot=<slotKey> (slotKey = per-minute key YYYY-M-DTHH:MM of the run).
2. Inject a JobInspector: inspect(svc) => { slot: string|null, inFlight: boolean }. slot = the existing service's bosun.cron-slot label (null if no service). inFlight = service has a task in a non-terminal state. Real impl shells docker service inspect / docker service ps; pure/injected for tests.
3. runDueJobs decision per due job: if slot===currentSlot -> skip (already ran this slot, restart-safe dedupe); else if inFlight -> skip (overlap guard, restart-safe); else fire (rm+create with the slot label).
4. Drop the in-memory lastRunMinute/inFlight. Log every decision with the slot key so recovery is checkable from logs.

CRASH SEMANTICS: killed after create -> restart sees slot match -> skip (no double-fire). Killed between rm and create -> no service -> fire (at-least-once). Long run + restart -> task in-flight -> skip until done.

PROVE IN PRODUCTION: deploy hardened scheduler; add a temp every-minute job that sleeps ~120s; let it fire; kill the bosun-agent task mid-run; swarm restarts it; show via logs the restarted scheduler does NOT double-fire (sees slot label / in-flight) and the original run completes; remove temp job.
acceptance_criteria:
- JobInspector injected; runDueJobs skips on slot-match and on in-flight, fires otherwise (unit-tested with a fake inspector+runner via vitest)
- fired job service carries --label bosun.cron-slot=<slotKey>; buildJobCommand asserts this
- in-memory lastRunMinute/inFlight removed; decisions derived from swarm
- bun run test + typecheck + biome green
- PROD PROOF: logs captured showing bosun-agent killed mid-run, restarted by swarm, scheduler re-reads swarm and SKIPS the in-flight/already-ran slot (no duplicate job), original run completes 1/1
- temp crash-test job removed from config after proof; ofelia/no-fake-data/commit-msg guards pass

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: Implementation done + shipped (4d4a2ee on main). Scheduler now stateless: removed lastRunMinute/inFlight; added slotKey + JobInspector (reads bosun.cron-slot label + task liveness from swarm); runDueJobs skips slot-already-ran / in-flight, else fires stamping the slot. 783 tests/typecheck/biome green. README crash-recovery section added. Temp every-minute crash-test job (sleep 150) added for the prod proof. CI 26990763115 building+deploying. Next: once agent rolls to new image, fire crash-test, docker kill the bosun-agent mid-run, capture logs showing the restarted scheduler skips the in-flight/already-ran slot (no double-fire), then remove crash-test.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: PROD PROOF COMPLETE. Fired crash-test slot 2026-5-5T2:11 (sleep 150), SIGKILLed the bosun-agent (docker kill, 02:12:14Z) mid-run. Results: (1) original run survived - crash-test task aya4z3ztcdkt stayed Running 45s+ after the kill (swarm-owned, not scheduler-owned). (2) Restarted agent task 44yppzi22p4h booted with FRESH in-memory state ('managing 2 cron job(s)') and logged 'crash-test still in flight (per swarm), skipping slot 2:12/2:13' - read swarm, declined to double-fire. (3) crash-test never had >1 task. (4) Resumed normally: 'running crash-test slot 2:14' once the original completed, plus 'already ran slot 2:14, skipping' showing the slot-dedupe guard too. Both restart-safe guards demonstrated live. Log proof saved + sent to Calum. Removing temp crash-test now (config removed in 44c5254; CI rebuilding; will force-roll agent + rm leftover service).

title: Add bosun run-job on-demand cron trigger + prove new scheduler e2e in Portainer
description:
The Ofelia->Swarm-job migration (CC-79k, commit 9033c9a) shipped the in-process scheduler but left no way to fire a cronJob on demand: jobs only run when their 5-field cron matches. This blocks e2e verification (can't wait until 03:00) and is an operational gap (no manual re-run).

Add a 'bosun run-job <name>' CLI command that loads the config, selects the cronJob by name, and runs the SAME buildJobCommand() path the scheduler uses (docker service create --mode replicated-job). Prove the new cron system end-to-end by running a job against the live homelab swarm and observing the resulting control-center-cron-* job service in Portainer. Also finish the live migration: ensure the deployed agent reconciles away the stale control-center_ofelia and old docker-image-prune stack services.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'bosun run-job <name>' exists; selects job by name; errors clearly on unknown/non-job name (pure selector unit-tested via vitest)
- run-job emits the identical docker service create --mode replicated-job invocation as the scheduler (shares buildJobCommand)
- bun run test + bun run typecheck + biome check all green
- A control-center-cron-docker-image-prune replicated-job is created on the homelab swarm via the new code and is VISIBLE in Portainer (screenshot)
- Stale control-center_ofelia and old stack docker-image-prune services are gone from docker service ls after reconcile
- no-ofelia + commit-msg + fake-data guards pass

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: E2E PROVEN in Portainer. Ran new 'bosun run-job docker-image-prune' (DOCKER_HOST=ssh://homelab) -> created replicated-job control-center-cron-docker-image-prune (id ymv2i09q8mi3), task Complete (1/1 completed), no error, label bosun.cron-job=docker-image-prune. Visible in Portainer service list + detail (screenshots sent to Calum). Gates green: 779 tests, typecheck, biome. Shipped cc49fda to main. Pending: CI run 26988922805 redeploys current (already-new-code) agent so 'bosun up --prune' removes the stale control-center_ofelia + old control-center_docker-image-prune stack services; verify after deploy then close.

title: Migrate cc_ resource namespace (secrets + pgdata volume) to stackName-derived
description:
The cc_ prefix on docker secrets (cc_<NAME>_<hash>) and the pgdata volume (cc_pgdata) is legacy. Long-term we want the namespace derived from stackName so the swarm stack is the single source of truth (no magic prefix). CANNOT be a code-only change folded into a normal deploy - the auto-deploy webhook (push to main -> bosun up) makes both renames hazardous:
- VOLUME: renaming cc_pgdata -> control-center_pgdata points postgres at a new EMPTY volume on next deploy unless data is migrated first (docker volume create + busybox copy + verify row counts).
- SECRETS: reconcileSecrets prunes old secrets BEFORE deployStack runs, and 'docker secret rm' refuses an in-use secret. Renaming every secret at once -> prune of still-in-use cc_* fails -> bosun up throws -> deploy breaks. Fix the prune ordering (prune AFTER deploy) OR do it in a maintenance window.
Sequenced ops task, do both together once: (1) fix reconcileSecrets to prune after deploy (or force/retry), (2) on box migrate pgdata volume + verify, (3) flip secretDockerName + stack volume render to stackName-derived, (4) deploy + verify postgres has data and all services roll cleanly, (5) remove old cc_* secrets + cc_pgdata volume, (6) confirm grep -ri 'cc_' is clean in code.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] reconcileSecrets prune ordering fixed so renaming in-use secrets cannot break a deploy (covered by a test)
- [ ] control-center_pgdata created on box with cc_pgdata data copied + verified (row counts match)
- [ ] secretDockerName + stack volume render switched to stackName-derived (no cc_)
- [ ] Deploy verified: postgres comes up with existing data; every service rolls to new secret names cleanly
- [ ] Old cc_pgdata volume + cc_* secrets removed after a safe window
- [ ] cc_ prefix fully gone from the codebase
notes:
DONE + verified in prod (2026-06-05). Two-deploy migration:
- Commit eb504b1: split reconcileSecrets into create + pruneSecrets(after deploy, tolerant of in-use). Old agent deployed it safely (no rename).
- Commit 2e48ed7: secretDockerName + stack volume render now stackName-derived.
- pgdata: cold-copied cc_pgdata -> control-center_pgdata on box (postgres scaled to 0) right before the B-agent cutover deploy. NOTE the rename needed TWO deploys because the deploying agent uses its OWN baked render code (same self-deploy lag as CC-czg): first deploy rolled the agent to B code, second deploy (re-run) actually emitted control-center_ names.

VERIFICATION:
- secrets: 13 control-center_*, 0 cc_*. api references control-center_* names.
- volume: postgres on control-center_pgdata; row counts intact (device_commands=85, device_state=9, integration_sync_status=2 exact; weather grew 56064->56256 from live ingest on top of copied history = no loss).
- live data: tRPC climate.get returns real HA state (cool/73/Cooling) => renamed HA_TOKEN secret works.
- all services 1/1, dashboard 200, /up OK, postgres accepting connections. Transient api crash-loop during the DB-down window (migrate-on-boot ENOTFOUND) self-recovered by design.
- codebase grep for cc_ clean (only explanatory comments remain).

LEFTOVER: cc_pgdata volume kept as rollback safety net (unreferenced). Remove after a safe window: docker volume rm cc_pgdata.
Found unrelated pre-existing bug -> CC-hya3 (stale /api/climate.now probe).

title: renderStackYml: cloudflared command $(cat secret) never works - shell-less image, token must be env
description: deploy.config.ts cloudflared uses command 'tunnel --no-autoupdate run --token $(cat /run/secrets/TUNNEL_TOKEN)'. The cloudflare/cloudflared image is shell-less (distroless), so $(cat ...) is passed as literal exec args -> 'Provided Tunnel token is not valid' -> crashloop. This render has NEVER produced a working cloudflared; the live tunnel was hand-deployed with TUNNEL_TOKEN as an env var. Discovered when 'bosun up' (CC-a8p bootstrap) clobbered the working stack. Fix options: (a) bosun resolves the secret at deploy time and renders TUNNEL_TOKEN as a service-spec env literal + args 'tunnel --no-autoupdate run' (token then visible in docker inspect - acceptable on this root-equiv single box per design Part 13, needs Calum sign-off); (b) custom cloudflared image with an entrypoint that exports the secret file to env. Recommend (a).
acceptance_criteria: bosun-rendered cloudflared starts 1/1 and the tunnel connects (dashboard 200) with no hand 'docker service update'; token is sourced from op://Homelab/Cloudflare Tunnel evee-webhooks/connector_token; running 'bosun up' twice is idempotent (no churn)
close_reason: Fixed by CC-6cq (cloudflared --token-file). Verified: cloudflared 1/1, dashboard 200, bosun up idempotent.

title: renderStackYml drops named volumes/configs - postgres pgdata has NO mount (data-loss risk)
description: Live control-center_postgres has null Mounts: the pgdata named volume + postgresql.conf config + initdb scripts declared in deploy.config.ts postgres({volume,config,init}) are NEVER rendered by packages/bosun/src/reconcile/stack.ts renderStackYml (it only emits image/env/secrets/volumes-as-bind/labels/placement/command). The postgres() builder in spec.ts also doesn't surface volume/config/init onto the ServiceSpec. Result: postgres persistence is in the container writable layer; any task recreation (image bump, node drain, manual redeploy without pinned digest) loses the DB. Not triggered by CC-a8p code-push auto-deploy (pinned postgres image -> service not recreated), but must be fixed before relying on bosun up for full reconcile. Fix: render named volumes (top-level volumes: block + service mounts) + docker configs; carry them through postgres()/ServiceSpec; add stack.test.ts coverage.
acceptance_criteria: renderStackYml emits a top-level 'volumes:' block and a service-level mount for postgres pgdata; emits docker config(s) for postgresql.conf; stack.test.ts asserts pgdata volume + config rendered; deploying the rendered yml yields control-center_postgres with a named-volume mount (docker service inspect shows pgdata mount, not null)
close_reason: Fixed by CC-11c (renderStackYml emits named volumes; postgres pinned to cc_pgdata). Verified live: postgres has cc_pgdata mount.

title: Auto-deploy webhook broken: empty GH deploy token + bosun receiver origin down (CF 521)
description: On push to main, the CI deploy job curls https://hooks.worldwidewebb.co/deploy/control-center with 'Authorization: Bearer ' (the GH repo deploy secret is EMPTY) and the request returns Cloudflare 521 (origin/bosun-serve receiver on homelab is down). So merges to main do NOT auto-deploy; the weather-ingest deploy had to be applied manually via 'docker service update --image ...:main' on homelab. Fix: (1) set the GH Actions deploy token secret, (2) bring up/repair the bosun-serve webhook receiver on homelab (CF tunnel origin). Related to CC-5ag deploy epic.
acceptance_criteria: push to main triggers a successful deploy webhook (HTTP 2xx); bosun reconciles the stack to the new image automatically; no manual docker service update needed
close_reason: Auto-deploy works end-to-end. Built the missing receiver: bosun-agent service (serve.ts /deploy/<stack> handler), webhook token in 1Password+GH secret, live CF route client (createRoute/deleteRoute), $ escape in renderStackYml, GHCR login in the agent so deploys can pull. Wired CF hooks. -> bosun-agent:4202 (removed stale hooks-test) + created the hooks CNAME. VERIFIED: push 1f12d8b -> CI deploy job 2xx (run 26975894529 deploy=success) -> agent bosun up auto-rolled control-center_api 217eca0d->54116457 with zero manual docker commands; dashboard 200 throughout. Note: a bad bootstrap bosun up briefly broke prod (render was never validated); recovered, postgres now on a persistent volume. Residuals filed: DNS-record automation in routes sync, agent verify noise; CC-chy rescoped to POSTGRES_DB-on-fresh-volume.

title: Deploy pipeline: arm64 images + bosun secret resolution + api DB wiring

Plus bosun secret sync failed on op refs with spaces and a drifted Postgres item name, and the new slim api (migrate-on-boot) had no path to the prod DB password. Fixed across commits f23be61..25cb92b on main.
acceptance_criteria: 1) CI builds multi-arch (amd64+arm64) images so the arm64 homelab node can pull them (was amd64-only -> 'no matching manifest for linux/arm64/v8'). 2) bosun op provider quotes refs (spaces in item names no longer split argv). 3) deploy.config Postgres ref matches real op item 'Control Center Postgres'. 4) slim api builds DATABASE_URL from mounted /run/secrets/POSTGRES_PASSWORD so migrate-on-boot reaches postgres. 5) web/api/storybook services running latest main sha on arm64; dashboard 200, api /up 200, migrations applied.

title: ac_restart_recovers: stack self-heals after orb restart
description: Restart survival (Part 11).
acceptance_criteria: orb restart then poll until all 1/1 and portainer/storybook 200 again; HA VM pid unchanged and tailscale stays connected throughout. Per ac_restart_recovers. Engine restart, not full OS reboot.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
VERIFIED - OrbStack engine restart, stack self-heals.

The swarm runs on the OrbStack Docker engine (single node 'orbstack'), restarted via 'orbctl stop; orbctl start' (orb restart only restarts Linux machines, not the engine; this is why HA - a separate qemu/UTM VM, not an OrbStack machine - is untouched).

- During restart: all 6 services dropped to 0/1, containers gone, docker engine cycled (node returned Ready/Leader).
- Self-heal: swarm rescheduled every task back to 1/1 (api, web, postgres, storybook, cloudflared, bosun-agent) with NO manual intervention; container healthchecks web/api/postgres all 'healthy'.
- portainer.worldwidewebb.co + storybook.worldwidewebb.co + dashboard.worldwidewebb.co all back to HTTP 200.
- HA VM pid UNCHANGED: 3402 (pre) == 3402 (post) - engine restart, not OS reboot, HA VM independent of OrbStack.
- tailscale stayed connected throughout (host-level, unaffected by engine restart; ssh-over-tailnet held the whole time).
Per ac_restart_recovers.

The modal contains the SAME controls as the small tile (Lamps / Lights / Fan toggles) PLUS extra lamp controls that exploit the Hue bulbs' full color/brightness capability (rgb + brightness + colorTemp; see apps/api/src/config/lights.ts - all 6 lamp-kind entities are Hue 'light.' domain).

title: Climate tile: optimistic value snaps back to stale server value before cooldown refetch
description:
Setting a climate setpoint or switching mode shows the OLD value for ~5s before the new value appears.

Repro:
1. Climate in cool at 74. Drag slider to 71.
2. UI flips back to 74 almost immediately.
3. ~5s later it shows 71.
Same happens pressing the mode buttons (off / cool / heat / heat_cool): the active mode/setpoint reverts then re-applies a few seconds later.

Root cause (apps/web/src/components/tiles/ClimateTile.tsx):
- handleSetTarget/handleSetRange/handleSetMode set an optimistic overlay (localTarget/localLow/localHigh/localMode) and start a 5s cooldown that PAUSES the climate.get refetch (makeRefetchInterval returns false during cooldown).
- Each mutation passes { onSettled: clearLocal }. HA returns 200 almost instantly, so clearLocal fires within ~tens of ms and nulls the optimistic overlay.
- With the overlay gone AND the query paused, the view falls back to query.data which is still the STALE pre-change value -> snaps back to 74 / old mode.
- Only when the cooldown timer expires (5s) does the useEffect invalidate + refetch, finally pulling the fresh value -> 71 appears.

So the optimistic value is discarded too early; the cooldown-expiry invalidation is the only thing that brings truth, leaving a ~5s window showing stale data.
design: Make the cooldown-expiry path the SINGLE owner of clearing the optimistic overlay. Likely fix: drop onSettled: clearLocal from all three mutations and let the existing cooldown useEffect (which already calls clearLocal after the timer + invalidate) be the only clear point. Verify the invalidate completes/refetch lands before clearing so there's no flash. Cover with a container test: after a slider change, advancing fake timers past mutation settle but BEFORE cooldown expiry must still render the optimistic value (not stale server). Add the same assertion for a mode-button change.
acceptance_criteria:
Dragging the setpoint (cool/heat) shows the new value continuously with no snap-back to the old value. Dragging both heat_cool thumbs likewise. Pressing any mode button (off/cool/heat/heat_cool) keeps the new mode/setpoints visible with no revert. A vitest test fails on the current onSettled-clearLocal behavior and passes after the fix. All existing climate tests still pass.

Fan-toggle parity (per memory controls-fan-is-ac-fan-mode-not-a-fan - ControlsTile shares this exact cooldown/optimistic pattern, so it has the same snap-back):
- Toggling the Fan control in the Controls tile shows the new on/off state continuously with no revert-then-reapply window.
- The fix must NOT change the fan_mode semantics: ON still sends set_fan_mode:'on', OFF still sends set_fan_mode:'auto' (there is no 'off' fan_mode on this AC), targeting climate.home via findFanClimate.
- A ControlsTile vitest test asserts the fan toggle does not snap back (optimistic state survives mutation-settle until the cooldown refetch lands), mirroring the climate test.
- While here, note (do not necessarily fix in this ticket) that findFanClimate uses an unsorted .find() - the same fragility class as the old alphabetical climate-entity bug, currently masked only because the Tesla entity has fan_modes:null.
close_reason: Dropped onSettled:clearLocal from setTarget/setRange/setMode; cooldown-expiry effect is the single overlay-clear point and clears only after invalidate().then() lands. 3 climate regression tests (setpoint/range/mode) + 1 ControlsTile fan-parity test, all fail-before/pass-after. 538 tests green. Commit 8c6428e.

title: Web: ClimateTile container - explicit mode + optimistic single/range, drop fake 'auto'
description: Rewire the container to the real model. Delete modeFromTarget and the cosmetic 'auto' band. Mode is explicit user choice. Optimistic state covers target OR (low,high) OR off.
acceptance_criteria:
- modeFromTarget removed; no derived/cosmetic mode anywhere.
- Optimistic state: localMode + (localTarget | localLow/localHigh). 5s cooldown + debounce preserved.
- Switching to heat_cool when server low/high are null seeds optimistic defaults from current target (low=clamp(target-3), high=clamp(target+3)) or 70/76 from off.
- Switching heat_cool→cool/heat seeds single target from midpoint of low/high.
- onSetRange debounced like onSetTarget; setMode immediate.
- Tests: mode click calls setMode with real hvac string; range drag commits clamped low/high; no 'auto' string emitted.

title: Web: ClimateTileView - 4 mode buttons, single slider + dual-thumb track, off state
description: Presentational rework. 4 mode buttons (Off/Cool/Heat/Heat·Cool). cool/heat → existing single .range slider. heat_cool → dual-thumb single track (two stacked range inputs over one bar, colored fill between). off → no setpoint control. Pure clamp helpers enforce 2° gap + 65-80 bounds; thumbs can't cross/overlap (z-index/pointer-events so each thumb stays grabbable when close).
acceptance_criteria:
- Pure helpers clampLow(next,high)=min(max(next,65),high-2) and clampHigh(next,low)=max(min(next,80),low+2), unit-tested across extremes.
- Props are a discriminated union mirroring API; impossible to pass both target and low/high.
- cool/heat: single slider rendered (testid 'slider'), no dual sliders.
- heat_cool: two sliders (testid 'slider-low','slider-high'), no single slider; low always < high by >=2°.
- off: no slider; setpoint area shows 'Off'; 4 buttons present, Off active.
- Dragging low toward/over high clamps to high-2; dragging high toward/under low clamps to low+2 (no overlap, ever).
- onSetMode/onSetTarget/onSetRange fire with correct payloads. Ambient 'Now' caret still renders.
- New dual-thumb CSS in tokens.css; dark-mode + thumb hit-areas verified.

title: API: write paths - setMode / setTarget / setRange with validation
description: Three write procedures mapping to HA services, with server-side validation so bad input returns a clean tRPC error, never an HA 500.
acceptance_criteria:
- setClimateMode(entityId, 'off'|'cool'|'heat'|'heat_cool') → set_hvac_mode {hvac_mode}.
- setClimateTarget(entityId, temp) → set_temperature {temperature} (cool/heat).
- setClimateRange(entityId, low, high) → set_temperature {target_temp_low, target_temp_high} (heat_cool).
- Router: climate.setMode (enum incl off), climate.setTarget (int 65-80), climate.setRange ({low,high} ints, refine low+2<=high & both 65-80).
- Invalid range (low>=high, gap<2, out of band) is REJECTED server-side before any HA call.
- Tests: each service sends the correct HA service+params; setRange rejects low+1==high and low>high.

title: API: target the house thermostat (climate.home), not the Tesla
description: resolveClimateEntityId() picks the alphabetically-first climate entity, which is climate.evee_climate (Calum's Tesla) - wrong device, and the source of the set_temperature 500s. Select the real house thermostat instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- New env CLIMATE_ENTITY_ID (apps/api/src/env.ts) defaulting to 'climate.home'.
- resolveClimateEntityId() returns that entity when present; falls back to first NON-tesla climate entity otherwise; undefined if none.
- getClimate()/setClimate* all operate on the resolved house entity.
- Test: given entities [climate.evee_climate, climate.home], resolver returns climate.home (NOT alphabetical-first).
- Test: env override respected.

title: API: climate state model - real modes + single/range setpoints
description: Replace ClimateMode 'cool|auto|heat' with real HA modes and make getClimate read the right setpoint attrs per mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- ClimateMode = 'off' | 'cool' | 'heat' | 'heat_cool'.
- getClimate() reads entity.state as the mode; cool/heat → {target: attrs.temperature}; heat_cool → {targetLow: target_temp_low, targetHigh: target_temp_high}; off → no setpoint.
- ClimateState is a discriminated union on mode (target and low/high CANNOT coexist).
- Router get output = zod discriminatedUnion('mode', ...). ambient + action present in all variants.
- Tests: parse a cool entity, a heat_cool entity (low/high), an off entity - each yields the correct variant.

title: Climate tile: real HA modes (off/cool/heat/heat_cool) + single & dual setpoints
design:
Replace the tile's FAKE mode model (modeFromTarget invents 'auto' for 71-75°, chips just nudge one slider) with real Home Assistant hvac modes from climate.home: off / cool / heat / heat_cool.

CONTRACT:
- Modes are explicit (4 buttons: Off, Cool, Heat, Heat·Cool), NOT derived from setpoint.
- cool & heat: single setpoint (HA attr 'temperature', service set_temperature {temperature}).
- heat_cool: TWO setpoints (HA attrs target_temp_low/target_temp_high, service set_temperature {target_temp_low,target_temp_high}). UI = dual-thumb single track.
- off: no setpoint UI; tapping a mode turns the system on.
- Min deadband gap = 2°F between low/high; thumbs can NEVER meet or cross. Enforced by PURE clamp helpers + server-side validation (clean error, never an HA 500).
- Entity targeting MUST hit the house thermostat (climate.home), not the alphabetical-first entity - currently picks climate.evee_climate, which is Calum's TESLA, causing 500s. See bd memory ha-evee-is-tesla-not-home-climate.
- API state crosses tRPC as a discriminated union on mode so target and low/high can't coexist (illegal states unrepresentable).
- Visual band stays 65-80°F (existing design constant); HA hard range is 60-92.

Decisions (confirmed with Calum): dual-thumb single track; 4th Off button; 2°F gap.

title: ClockTile seconds dial animates backwards on 59→0 wrap
description: The Dial component has a CSS transition on stroke-dashoffset (Dial.tsx:53). When the seconds dial wraps from 59 to 0 the value drops from ~0.983 to 0, so the arc animates the full sweep backwards to empty, which looks weird. It should reset instantly. Fix: suppress the transition on the wrap frame (when value decreases) so the dial jumps to 0 instead of animating back.
close_reason: Fixed in 55af050 (SecondsRing.tsx): wrap now snaps instantly.

title: Connection-lost notification: surface a clear affordance when the API/data goes stale
design: Detect via React Query global state (a useIsRestoring/onError or an isFetching+isError aggregate, or a dedicated health.ping query that the QueryClient already retries infinitely). Render as a lightweight overlay element scoped inside the fixed 1366x1024 board (absolute-positioned, top-right corner), dark-theme tokens, no layout shift. This is the FIRST concrete notification and should be built so it can later be hosted by the notification center in the title-bar ticket - emit through a small shared notifications source (e.g. a useNotifications hook / store) rather than hardcoding the DOM, so the bar can consume the same events. Keep it self-contained until that bar lands.
acceptance_criteria: When the tRPC/React Query connection is lost (queries failing/retrying past a short threshold) the board shows a single, calm global notification that data is stale + reconnecting - not just every tile shimmering silently forever; it auto-dismisses when connectivity returns; it does not cover or shift tile layout; threshold tuned so a brief blip doesn't flash it; covered by a story + vitest test (simulate query failure -> notification appears; recovery -> it clears); gates green.
notes: Merged ship-p0p1/CC-dck into main (conflict in Board.tsx imports resolved - union of BoundedTile/QueryErrorResetBoundary from main and ConnectionLostBanner from branch). Review fixes applied: (1) extracted MESSAGE constant so DOM and notifications store use identical text, (2) added raiseNotification/clearNotification integration tests with toHaveBeenCalledWith assertions and DOM↔store parity test, (3) created ConnectionLostBanner.stories.tsx with Connected/ConnectionLost/Reconnecting stories. Typecheck + biome + scoped tests all green (7/7 pass).

title: [Storybook] Consolidate tile stories: dedupe shared scaffolding
description: After the 9 tile stories exist, drive them toward a 'define a tile -> it appears in Storybook automatically' architecture, eliminating duplicated story scaffolding. Storybook's story glob auto-discovers each tile, so adding a new tile needs only its state declaration - no per-tile boilerplate. Extract repeated decorators, mock/sample data, and render wrappers into this shared layer (e.g. .storybook/preview decorator + apps/web/src/components/tiles/__stories__/factory + a tiles registry the board can also consume). Refactor every *.stories.tsx to thin declarations over the factory. Keep behavior identical; reduce LOC + divergence; file follow-up bd issues for larger tile-component refactors surfaced.
acceptance_criteria: A shared tile-story factory/registry exists; each tile story is a thin declaration of its states over that factory (no duplicated decorator/mock/args boilerplate); adding a tile auto-surfaces it in Storybook with minimal code; net LOC reduced; dark contract still met; gates green; stories still render + pass as tests.

title: Next12Hours: hour labels are static placeholders, not real clock hours from now

title: ClimateTile: optimistic setpoint/mode with cooldown poll-pause (parity with controls)
description: Climate feels weird: dragging the slider / tapping a mode chip gets overridden when the next poll returns server state, causing snap-back/jumpiness. Apply the SAME pattern the ControlsTile evee-port uses: hold an optimistic local target+mode immediately on change, and pause/extend the refetch for a cooldown window (COOLDOWN_AFTER_TOGGLE_MS ~5s) so the backend set_temperature/set_hvac_mode confirms before the poll reconciles. Frontend-only optimistic is sufficient - the backend desired-window overlay only stores boolean on/off (DeviceStateValue), so a numeric setpoint can't use it without a schema change (note as a separate follow-up if backend overlay is later wanted). Reuse the cooldown helper/approach the controls work introduces; do not regress mutations. Update ClimateTile + tests. Coordinate with CC-70w (presentational split) and CC-6k8 (heat>=76).

title: Port evee controls no-revert pattern: auto-upsert desired-window overlay for unregistered lamps/lights, cooldown polling in ControlsTile, On/Off lamp display
Fan control: use climate fan_mode (evee parity), not a fan.* entity
description: Fan toggle did nothing because control-center targeted a non-existent fan.* domain entity. evee models the fan as the AC climate entity's fan_mode (set_fan_mode on/auto), read from the climate entity that advertises fan_modes. Ported: getControlsState derives fan from climate fan_mode; toggleControl('fan') calls climate.set_fan_mode on/auto; fan sub shows On/empty. Tests updated to the climate model.

title: Controls toggles are silent no-ops when device_state is unpopulated
description: CC-5yh routed all control commands through commandDevice, which only acts on entities present in the device_state table. When that table is empty (Calum's env - no devices registered), toggleControl found zero rows and dispatched nothing to HA, while the read path still showed live HA state. Result: clicking lamps/lights/fan did nothing. Fix: dispatchControls now calls ha.callService directly (config-correct domain) for any entity without a device_state row, keeping the commandDevice overlay path for registered devices. Regression test added.
close_reason: Fixed: direct HA dispatch for unregistered devices

title: CI: blocking pre-commit guard - reject fake-data sentinels outside sanctioned files
ControlsTile lamps/lights map to wrong entities: port evee's explicit lights config (Hue lamps vs switch fixtures)
description:
ControlsTile 'Lamps' and 'Lights' do not map to the correct real HA entities the way evee does. evee gets this right; control-center guesses and misses devices.

EVEE (correct, reference apps/api/src/config/lights.ts): an explicit config lists every entity with kind 'lamp' | 'fixture', domain, room, label, capabilities. LAMPS = Hue bulbs on the 'light.' domain with full caps (onOff/brightness/colorTemp/rgb) - e.g. light.living_room_globe, light.living_room_corner_lamp, light.living_room_floor_lamp, light.kitchen_lamp, light.bed_lamp_left/right (kind:'lamp', HUE_CAPABILITIES). LIGHTS/FIXTURES = OVERHEAD + KITCHEN lights controlled by a DIFFERENT device on the 'switch.' domain, on/off only - e.g. switch.overhead_lights (domain:'switch', kind:'fixture', SWITCH_CAPABILITIES). LAMP_IDS is derived from kind==='lamp'. The split is DECLARED in config, not inferred.

CONTROL-CENTER (broken, apps/api/src/services/controls-service.ts): classifyLights() (lines 85-104) only calls ha.getEntities('light') (line 125) - it NEVER fetches the 'switch' domain, so the overhead/kitchen switch-controlled fixtures are invisible to the dashboard. It then keyword-guesses lamp vs light from the entity id substring list \['lamp','floor','table','accent','corner','side'\] (lines 89-97). This is fragile and wrong: it can't see switch-domain fixtures at all, and misclassifies anything not matching the keywords.

FIX: port evee's approach.

1. Add apps/api/src/config/lights.ts (or controls config): an explicit entity list with kind (lamp = Hue light.*; fixture = overhead/kitchen switch.*), domain, label, room, capabilities - mirroring evee's LIGHTS. Real entity ids should come from config/env, not hardcoded guesses.
2. controls-service: replace classifyLights() heuristic with config-driven resolution. Fetch BOTH ha.getEntities('light') AND ha.getEntities('switch') and map by the config's entityId+domain. 'Lamps' = the lamp-kind (Hue) group; 'Lights' = the fixture-kind (overhead + kitchen switch) group.
3. Toggling 'Lights' must call the correct service on the switch domain (switch.turn_on/off) and 'Lamps' on the light domain - exactly the evee device split.
   Coordinate with CC-bh5 (ControlsTile cleanup) and CC-5yh (anti-flicker desired-window, which also keys off these entity/domain mappings).

## Acceptance Criteria

Lamps map to the Hue light.\* entities and Lights map to the overhead + kitchen fixtures on the switch.\* domain (or whatever real devices control them), driven by an explicit config like evee's apps/api/src/config/lights.ts - NOT id-substring guessing. controls-service fetches both light and switch domains. Toggling Lights actuates the switch-domain device and Lamps the Hue light device. No fixture is invisible. No fake/placeholder entities; entity ids come from config/env. Verified against evee config/lights.ts mapping.

title: Controls anti-flicker: backend optimistic-state overlay + HA websocket reconcile (port from evee)
description:
When a user taps a control (lights/lamps/fan on), Home Assistant does NOT immediately report the new state. control-center today does frontend-only optimistic updates (ControlsTile.tsx:89-134 React-Query onMutate flip + onSettled invalidate) over a stateless controls-service.ts that just calls HA and reads back. On the next poll (or the onSettled invalidate) it re-reads stale HA state and the tile FLICKERS back to the old value. Per-client only; defeated by the very next refetch.

evee already solved this by pushing the logic to the BACKEND in a durable way. Port the same mechanism. VERIFIED in evee source:

Mechanism (evee apps/api/src/services/device-sync-service.ts):

* OPTIMISTIC_WINDOW_MS = 12_000 (12s).
* writeOptimisticState(): right after a command is sent, persist the DESIRED value on the device row - columns optimisticState, optimisticCapability, optimisticUntil = now+12s, lastCommandTs (DB-backed via drizzle 'devices' table; NOT in-memory).
* mergeDeviceState(device, now): the dashboard reads a MERGED view - while now < optimisticUntil and optimisticState set, trust the desired value (optimistic:true); otherwise return HA truth.
* reconcileFromHa({db,event}): driven by an HA WEBSOCKET RELAY (apps/api/src/integrations/homeassistant/ws-relay.ts emits HaStateChangedEvent on HA 'state_changed'). Always stores latest HA truth (haState); if HA now agrees with the optimistic value, clears the overlay EARLY (confirmed).
* sweepExpiredOptimistic(db): timer sweep that snaps back to HA truth after the window if HA never confirms (failed command doesn't lie forever).

So state flows: command -> write optimistic overlay -> tile shows desired value instantly and stably -> HA eventually pushes real state over websocket -> overlay cleared on confirm (or expires after 12s). No flicker, and consistent across ALL dashboard clients (state lives in the DB, not one browser cache).

PORT into control-center:

1. apps/api: add a device-sync-service equivalent (writeOptimisticState / mergeDeviceState / reconcileFromHa / sweepExpiredOptimistic) + the optimistic columns on the controls/devices store. Today controls-service.ts is stateless - give it (or a new service) the overlay + merge.
2. apps/api: add an HA websocket relay (port integrations/homeassistant/ws-relay.ts) and wire reconcileFromHa to it; start the sweep timer.
3. trpc: controls.list returns MERGED state incl. an 'optimistic'/'pending' flag; controls.toggle (and climate setTarget / fan setSpeed) writes the overlay then returns merged state.
4. apps/web: ControlsTile drops the onMutate cache hack and renders backend-merged state; poll faster while anything is pending (evee uses an adaptive interval), slower when idle. Optional subtle 'pending' indicator.
5. Apply the same overlay to ClimateTile setpoint and fan speed (non-boolean desired values).

Reference files (evee): apps/api/src/services/device-sync-service.ts, device-state-mapping.ts, device-command-service.ts; apps/api/src/integrations/homeassistant/ws-relay.ts; apps/api/src/db/schema.ts (devices optimistic\* columns, deviceCommands); apps/api/src/trpc/routers/controls/{index,fans,climate}.ts; apps/web/src/hooks/use-auto-reload.ts; apps/web/src/components/tiles/lights/lights-tile{,-view}.tsx + fan/fan-tile{,-view}.tsx.
NOTE: an earlier exploration mis-described this as an in-memory Map with 8s TTL - the VERIFIED design is DB-backed, 12s window, websocket-driven reconcile. Follow the source.

## Acceptance Criteria

Tapping a control updates the tile instantly and it does NOT flicker back while HA catches up. Optimistic value is held server-side (survives refetch and is consistent across multiple clients), confirmed early when HA reports the matching state over websocket, and snaps back to HA truth if unconfirmed after the optimistic window. controls.list/toggle (+ climate/fan) return merged state with a pending flag. ControlsTile no longer relies on React-Query onMutate for correctness. No fake/placeholder data introduced (outage still shows shimmer per CC-nra).
<!-- bd-idempotency: 2b757111daf8 -->
acceptance_criteria: Tapping a control updates the tile instantly and does NOT flicker back while HA catches up. Desired state is held SERVER-SIDE in the DB (survives refetch, consistent across multiple dashboard clients), confirmed early when the 1s reconcile poll sees HA match, and snaps back to HA truth (command marked timeout) if unconfirmed after the 5s window. Implemented with a polling reconcile loop - NO websockets anywhere. controls.list/toggle (+ climate setpoint, fan speed) return merged state with a pending flag. ControlsTile no longer relies on React-Query onMutate for correctness. No fake/placeholder data (outage still shows shimmer per CC-nra).

* Every tile uses the shared Tile + TileHeader + Stat/Pill/Chip/StatusDot/Skeleton primitives; no tile keeps an inline section header or bespoke card shell.

title: Design system: shared tile primitives (Tile, TileHeader, Stat, Pill, Chip, StatusDot, Skeleton)
description:
Every tile hand-rolls the same primitives, so spacing/typography/behavior drift between tiles. Build them ONCE so the whole board is consistent and production-grade.

Current duplication:
- Section header rebuilt inline in ControlsTile.tsx:151-168 and NetworkTile.tsx:88-127 (fontSize 17.5/weight 600/letterSpacing -.015em), a THIRD variant in TilePlaceholder.tsx (.sec/.ic/.lbl), and the design's <Sec icon right> in /private/tmp/design-bundle/evee/project/evee-tiles.jsx:5-13. Three headers, none shared.
- Tile shell: every tile repeats className=tile + height:100%/flex/column/padding.
- Stat block (design <Stat label value accent sub> evee-tiles.jsx:16-24), mode Chip, Pill, live StatusDot - all inlined per tile.

Build under apps/web/src/components/ui/ (or components/primitives/):
- Tile: card shell (className=tile, height:100%, flex column, configurable padding prop) so per-tile padding (clock 28, weath 24, ctrl/net 22, events 18x26) is a prop not a copy.
- TileHeader (port of design Sec): props { icon, title, right?, iconSize=19, titleSize=17.5 } - must cover BOTH the small WiFi/Controls header AND the larger Tesla header by varying size props.
- Stat { label, value, accent?, sub? }
- Pill { tone: default|on|amber } and Chip { active }
- StatusDot (the pulsing .dot)
- Skeleton: reusable shimmer block with a @keyframes shimmer gradient-sweep (added to styles/globals.css). This is the building block for ALL loading/outage states. TilePlaceholder.tsx is only a static icon scaffold - replace it.

Reference good componentization: evee/apps/web/src/components/tile.tsx. Design tokens already in styles/tokens.css + evee.css.

This is the foundation epic: the per-tile fidelity/outage issues depend on it.

title: Seed ticket-builder runs with deterministic goals
description: Add deterministic goal seeding to the Temporal ticket-builder workflow so every builder run starts from a clear end-state objective. title: Show and filter automated tickets on issues page
description: The Project Management issues page should make it easy to distinguish tickets that are part of the automated ticket workflow from ordinary manual issues. Use the existing ticket workflow labels, likely labels with the ticket-* prefix, as the signal unless implementation discovers a better canonical marker. Add a subtle right-side badge/indicator on issue rows or cards and provide a filter to show only automated tickets.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issues page identifies automated tickets using a canonical signal, with ticket-* labels considered as the expected source unless a better existing marker is found.
- [ ] Automated tickets show a clear but unobtrusive Auto/Automated indicator on the right side of each relevant issue row/card.
- [ ] Issues page includes a filter option to show only automated tickets.
- [ ] Manual/non-automated issues are not incorrectly marked as automated.
- [ ] The indicator and filter match the existing dark Project Management UI styling and remain keyboard accessible.
- [ ] Run the relevant Project Management UI lint/typecheck/test command for the touched files and record the result in the ticket or closing reason.

title: Show exact ticket workflow exhaustion failures in UI
description: The ticket workflow currently ends with a generic message like 'Ticket workflow exhausted 2 builder attempt(s) or 2 reviewer attempt(s).' That hides the actual failing phase, attempt, command/check, stderr/log excerpt, reviewer finding, and stop reason from the operator. The UI should make the terminal failure evidence visible so Calum can see what exactly failed and why without digging through raw workflow logs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A workflow run that exhausts builder attempts shows the last failed builder attempt in the UI, including ticket id/title, attempt number, phase, command/check name, exit status when available, and a concise stderr/log excerpt.
- [ ] A workflow run that exhausts reviewer attempts shows the last failed reviewer attempt in the UI, including reviewer role, finding summary, file/line references when available, and the blocking acceptance criterion or gate.
- [ ] The generic exhausted message is replaced or supplemented by a clear stop reason that names whether the builder limit, reviewer limit, or both limits were hit.
- [ ] The UI links from the exhausted summary to the underlying full log/transcript artifact when one exists.
- [ ] Regression coverage exercises builder-exhausted and reviewer-exhausted states with structured failure details, not only the generic string.
- [ ] agent-browser screenshot @1366x1024 verifies the exhausted-workflow details render in the Project Management UI.
- [ ] Gates: bun run test + bun run typecheck + bunx biome check . green.
- [ ] No fake data, check-fake-data guard green.

title: Show ticket workflow token totals

It must not write app tables into Temporal's temporal database/tables; create/use a separate project_management database via Tilt init and point PROJECT_MANAGEMENT_DATABASE_URL at it. No Pulumi/prod database work in this ticket.

title: Persist ticket workflow stdout and stderr
description: Persist ticket workflow tmux stdout/stderr into Project Management Postgres so completed ticket evidence survives local log cleanup. This covers builder, reviewer, and merge-fix attempts.
design: Use ticket_workflow_run and ticket_workflow_log_chunk tables from the local Project Management DB. At run start, upsert a run row. During wait/poll, append only new stdout/stderr bytes as ordered chunks. On completion, final-sync both streams and mark the run completed/failed/timed_out. Keep existing filesystem log links as fallback.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Starting builder, reviewer, and merge-fix activities creates or updates a ticket_workflow_run row with ticket id, phase, attempt, tmux session, prompt/log paths, and started timestamp.
- [ ] waitForAgentRunCompletionActivity appends stdout and stderr chunks separately without duplicating already-captured bytes.
- [ ] Completion performs a final stdout/stderr sync and stores completed_at plus completed/failed/timed_out status.
- [ ] Unit tests cover idempotent chunk append, stdout/stderr separation, and final status updates.
- [ ] Existing Beads metadata log path behavior remains available as a fallback.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run test exits 0.
- [ ] From products/project-management, bun run typecheck exits 0.

title: Replace project-management Temporal SQLite with Postgres dev stack
description: Project Management's local Temporal runtime currently uses temporal server start-dev with a SQLite --db-filename. That is the wrong persistence layer for the ticket workflow: Tilt resets/restarts can lose or wedge the SQLite store, and the dev stack should exercise a real Postgres-backed Temporal persistence path. Replace the local Tilt Temporal persistence with a persistent Docker Postgres container and a Temporal server that auto-initializes/migrates its schema, while keeping the existing worker/UI entrypoints pointed at the same Temporal address.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] products/project-management/Tiltfile starts a persistent Postgres resource for Temporal before Temporal/worker/ui
- [ ] Temporal persistence uses Postgres, not --db-filename SQLite, in Tilt and package scripts
- [ ] Temporal schema initialization/migrations run automatically on a fresh empty Postgres volume
- [ ] Temporal Postgres data survives stopping/restarting Tilt without deleting the named Docker volume
- [ ] README documents the Postgres-backed Temporal dev stack and the explicit volume reset command
- [ ] products/project-management tests/typecheck pass: bun run --cwd products/project-management test && bun run --cwd products/project-management typecheck
- [ ] Relevant repo gates pass for changed files, including Biome
- [ ] No fake data or plaintext secrets are introduced
- [ ] Committed as chore(project-management/www-rzh2): desc; branch merged/pushed; bd closed
close_reason: Implemented and verified, moving to shipped

title: Design serialized merge queue workflow
description: Multiple child ticket workflows can finish review concurrently, but merging, waiting for CI, closing Beads, and pushing Beads must be globally serialized. Record the signal-based workflow contract before implementation so the merge queue has stable ownership boundaries.
design:
# Serialized Merge Queue Workflow Design

## Decision
Build a real long-lived Temporal workflow named ticket_merge_queue. The existing ticketWorkflow remains responsible for claim/build/review. After reviewer verification, ticketWorkflow must stop merging inline and instead enqueue a merge request with ticket_merge_queue, then wait for a merge result signal.

## Workflow IDs
- Merge queue workflow id: ticket_merge_queue
- Ticket workflow id remains ticket_<ticketId>

## Core Types
- MergeQueueWorkflowInput: repoRoot, taskQueue, finalGates, runtimeLogRoot?, maxMergeAttempts default 3, maxHistoryEvents default 100.
- MergeQueueRequest: requestId, ticketId, ticketWorkflowId, title, branch, commitSha, strategy, acceptanceCriteria, comments, requestedAt.
- MergeQueueResult discriminated union:
  - merged: ticketId, requestId, mergeCommitSha, pushed true, closed true
  - retryable-failure: ticketId, requestId, failedStep, attempt, reason
  - human-blocked: ticketId, requestId, failedStep, reason
- MergeQueueStep registry: assert-clean-main, update-main, merge-ticket-branch, final-gates, push-main, close-ticket, push-beads.

## Signals And Queries
- merge queue signal enqueueMerge(request: MergeQueueRequest)
- merge queue signal cancelMerge(ticketId: string, reason: string)
- ticket workflow signal ticketMergeResult(result: MergeQueueResult)
- merge queue query mergeQueueSnapshot(): queued entries, active entry, completedCount

## Queue Semantics
- FIFO by enqueue order.
- Deduplicate by ticketId and requestId if request is already queued, active, or completed.
- Only one active merge at a time.
- continueAsNew after maxHistoryEvents completed entries, carrying queued requests and compact counters forward.

## Ownership Boundary
The merge queue owns all main mutations and finalization: assert clean repo, update/fast-forward main, merge ticket branch, run final gates, push main, clean workflow labels, set shipped metadata, bd close, bd dolt push, and signal ticket workflow with the result.

ticketWorkflow owns build/review only. After review passes it enqueues a MergeQueueRequest and waits for ticketMergeResult. It does not call merge activities directly.

## Failure Policy
- Dirty local main: human-blocked before fetch/merge. Never stash, reset, or overwrite anonymous changes.
- Local main ahead/diverged unexpectedly: human-blocked.
- Remote moved before push: retryable, restart from update-main, never force-push.
- Merge conflict or final gate failure: retry through bounded merge-fix/final-gate attempts, then human-blocked.
- Main pushed but Beads close/push fails: human-blocked/recoverable finalization failure, do not rerun git merge.
- Default max merge attempts: 3.

## Startup Reconciliation
Worker startup idempotently ensures ticket_merge_queue. It discovers open ticket-verified tickets and signals them into the queue if they are not already queued/active/completed. Closed shipped tickets and ticket-human tickets are ignored.

## Rejected Alternatives
- Keep inline runSerializedMergeWorkflow inside each ticketWorkflow: rejected because multiple ticket workflows can mutate main concurrently.
- Use a process-local mutex: rejected because worker restarts lose state and Temporal history would not show queue state.
- Put failed merges back into ticket-ready: rejected because merge failure is not a builder queue concern.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design records the merge queue workflow id, signal payload shape, FIFO/ordering semantics, and idempotency behavior.
- [ ] Design records how ticketWorkflow signals the merge queue, waits for merge result, and handles merge success, retryable failure, and human-blocked failure.
- [ ] Design records that the merge queue owns final Beads cleanup, shipped metadata, bd close, and bd dolt push.
- [ ] Design records startup behavior for existing ticket-verified tickets when the worker boots.
- [ ] Design records continue-as-new or equivalent history compaction strategy.
- [ ] Decision/rationale is stored in the Beads ticket design or notes field.
close_reason: Merge queue workflow contract recorded in design field

title: Add workflow metrics and quota to Project Management
description: Expose useful throughput and cost/usage signals in the Project Management UI. Quota is best-effort and must never fail the workflow if unavailable or unparseable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] UI shows started, built, reviewed, merged, closed, failed, retried, and human escalated counts
- [ ] UI shows average, p50, p90, and p95 durations
- [ ] UI shows OpenCode stats when available
- [ ] UI shows quota/plugin snapshot when available
- [ ] Quota parsing failure is non-fatal and visible
- [ ] Tests cover percentile and metrics aggregation
close_reason: Added workflow metrics aggregation for counts, durations percentiles, OpenCode totals, and best-effort quota snapshots with visible non-fatal parse errors. Exposed metrics in board-data and dashboard metrics strip. Verified tests/typecheck/config/focused Biome and live metrics API smoke.

title: Implement merge-fix agent fallback
description: Add bounded agent fallback for merge conflicts or post-merge gate failures. The deterministic merger tries first; if it breaks, a merge-fix agent can repair, gates rerun, and repeated failures escalate to ticket-human.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Merge failures can invoke a ticket-mergefix agent
- [ ] Merge-fix agent works in the merge/worktree context
- [ ] Merge-fix is bounded by attempt count
- [ ] Deterministic gates rerun after merge-fix
- [ ] Repeated failure escalates to ticket-human without closing
- [ ] Tests cover deterministic failure to agent fallback to retry to human escalation
close_reason: Implemented merge-fix fallback agent, bounded tmux/OpenCode activity, human escalation without close, final-gate rerun behavior, and verified with project-management tests/typecheck/config check/focused Biome plus regression coverage for failed merge-fix launch.

What needs to be done: make namespace-local postgres naming the default and encode tests that prevent product-slug DB names from returning.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] defineProductDatabase defaults clusterName to postgres, not product.slug
- [ ] defineProductDatabase defaults rwServiceName/roServiceName/readServiceName to postgres-rw/postgres-ro/postgres-r
- [ ] defineProductDatabase defaults authSecretName to postgres-auth unless an explicit migration override is documented
