title: Add safe CNPG local-name migration workflow
description: Why this issue exists: renaming CNPG clusters, services, pods, and PVCs is stateful and must not be done in place. What needs to be done: add a documented and repeatable migration workflow for creating a new local-name CNPG cluster, copying data, validating it, switching consumers, and rolling back.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration workflow creates a new postgres CNPG cluster alongside the old product-slug cluster without deleting old data
- [ ] Workflow includes quiesce-writers, dump, restore, row-count validation, schema validation, smoke test, and rollback steps
- [ ] Workflow documents rollback as switching POSTGRES_HOST back to the old *-rw service and restarting consumers
- [ ] Workflow records a soak window before old cluster/PVC cleanup
- [ ] Workflow has a dry-run or preflight check that refuses destructive cleanup before validation evidence exists
- [ ] Gates green: bun run test + bun run typecheck + bunx biome check .
- [ ] No fake data, check-fake-data guard green
- [ ] Committed as type(infra/www-xxx): desc, worktree→main merged + pushed, bd closed
close_reason: Completed: documented safe CNPG local-name migration workflow with cleanup preflight guard and CI test wiring; full gates passed and pushed in 271fe208.

title: Normalize product database resource names
description: Why this issue exists: product namespaces now carry product identity, but CNPG database resource names still repeat the product slug inside those namespaces. That produces confusing objects such as text-your-ex/text-your-ex-1 and captive-portal/captive-portal-rw. What needs to be done: move product DB primitives and live product DBs to namespace-local postgres naming, migrate existing data safely, and remove temporary legacy overrides.
design: End state: product identity lives in the namespace only. Product DB resources inside each namespace are local: CNPG Cluster postgres, pod postgres-1, PVC postgres-1, services postgres-rw/postgres-ro/postgres-r, auth secret postgres-auth, backup CronJob pg-backup, backup PVC pg-backup-vol-0 or equivalent local name. Existing product-slug CNPG clusters are migrated by create-new-cluster, dump/restore, validate, switch consumers, soak, then cleanup. AC for the epic is all children closed or explicitly deferred.

title: CF IaC: adopt-or-document zone settings, Access IdPs, and non-tunnel DNS records
design: Surfaced by www-0hwo CF IaC completeness audit (M3-M9 re-audit). infra/cloudflare/ declares Provider, Record(CNAME), ZeroTrustAccessApplication, ZeroTrustAccessPolicy, ZeroTrustTunnelCloudflaredConfig. NOT declared: (1) zone settings - relies on CF Universal SSL one-level wildcard, no ZoneSettings resource, so SSL/security-level drift is invisible; (2) Access IdPs incl. the known-undeclared one-time-PIN IdP; (3) any non-tunnel DNS record (apex/MX/TXT/SPF/domain-verification). Live enumeration was BLOCKED during the audit by a shared-agent 1Password rate-limit (op item get 'Cloudflare API' returned 429 twice), so the live record set was not diffed. This ticket completes the reconcile when op is available. Low risk (Universal SSL covers the flattened single-label product hosts) but IaC is currently drift-blind to these classes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Enumerate live CF zone settings, Access identity providers (incl. one-time-PIN), and all DNS records NOT pointing at the tunnel (apex/MX/TXT/SPF/verification), via the account-owned token
- [ ] For each: either declare as a protect:true Pulumi resource in infra/cloudflare/src/ OR document an explicit 'not managed, reason' note in the program header
- [ ] pulumi preview shows zero destructive churn after adoption
- [ ] One-time-PIN IdP decision recorded (manual vs IaC)

title: [epic] M10: Ship Project-Management app to prod
description:
New product (products/project-management, the live beads board/epics/issues UI built in www-0oi5) needs to go to prod the same way the other products did. This is M10 of the world-wide-webb platform migration (www-jtp0) and is done LAST - AFTER M3-M9 are fully finished and verified. It is deliberately sequenced after everything else: it's a brand-new product stood up during the migration, not part of the original M0-M9 plan, so it must not compete for cutover focus or risk the core products' go-live.

Follows the established product-launch pattern (mirror M6 Text Your Ex / M8 AMP): declare product infra as a typed component in infra/, give it a flattened host route, build its image in CI with a path filter, deploy via Pulumi digest-pin, verify live in a browser.

Product-specific wrinkles to handle in the children:
- AUTH: it exposes the full beads issue DB (internal project state) -> must be PRIVATE, Cloudflare Access-gated like app--cc (email-OTP for Calum), NOT public. Route: app--pm.worldwidewebb.co (single-label, free Universal SSL).
- DATA ACCESS: the app shells 'bd --json' and runs a background 'bd dolt pull' to stay synced. Prod deploy must give the container access to the beads Dolt data (the dolt server / refs/dolt/data on origin) + the bd binary. This is the non-trivial bit - unlike the other products it has no Postgres of its own, its datastore IS beads/Dolt. Decide: bundle bd + dolt in the image and pull from origin on an interval, or point at an in-cluster dolt server.
- NAMESPACE: lands per whatever www-r3it decides for product namespacing.

Blocked by M9 (www-jtp0.9) - do not start until the core migration is closed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] M3-M9 of www-jtp0 are all closed before this epic starts (sequencing gate)
- [ ] All child issues under this epic are closed
- [ ] app--pm.worldwidewebb.co serves the PM app, TLS ssl_verify=0, behind CF Access (302/deny unauth, loads authed)
- [ ] Browser-verified live in prod at the real URL: list, kanban, and issue views render real beads data (screenshot each, describe in transcript)
- [ ] CI builds + deploys the image on path-filter; deploy digest-pin rolls it independently
- [ ] No regression to the other products' routes

title: Audit: ALL live UniFi state declared in Pulumi (no drift, nothing unmanaged)
description:
infra/unifi is its own Pulumi project (control-center-unifi) declaring the guest network + WLAN + firewall for the captive portal. Confirm it captures EVERY relevant live UniFi controller object, not just the guest pieces touched so far. Anything configured by hand in the UniFi controller that the captive-portal/guest flow depends on, but isn't in Pulumi, is hidden state that breaks on a controller reset or a 'pulumi up'.

Scope to enumerate (live controller, via op://Homelab/UniFi local_api_key, X-API-KEY) vs declared (infra/unifi/src/*):
- Networks (guest network 'guest', VLAN 20, corporate-coerced purpose) - declared
- WLANs / SSIDs ('www-guest', isGuest, l2Isolation, apGroupIds) - declared
- Guest control / hotspot / external captive portal settings (redirect URL to app--cp, walled-garden allow-list, pre-auth bypass) - these are the M5.9 items, confirm they're modeled
- Firewall rules + groups (guest isolation, the 5-digit rule_index ones)
- Local DNS records (split-horizon app--cp -> Mini LAN IP) - declared or controller-manual?
- Port profiles / radius / any setting/set endpoints the portal relies on
NOTE controller quirks already known (memory ucg-fiber-pulumiverse-unifi-0-2-0-bridged): purpose coercion, apGroupIds lookup, 5-digit rule_index, multicastDns declare-or-404, apply with --refresh.

Deliverable: reconcile table (live object -> declared? -> action). UniFi is harder than CF for CI (controller is LAN-only, CI not on the network - that's the separate www-j934.17 gitops gap); this ticket is COMPLETENESS of the model, runnable via local pulumi up for now. NOT www-j934.17.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Enumerate live UniFi objects the guest/captive-portal flow depends on (networks, WLANs, guest/hotspot/walled-garden, firewall rules+groups, local DNS, relevant settings)
- [ ] Reconcile table: each marked declared / undeclared / intentionally-excluded(reason)
- [ ] Every undeclared dependency added to infra/unifi or listed with explicit exclusion reason
- [ ] local 'pulumi up --refresh' shows no destructive/unexpected diff against the live controller
- [ ] Cross-reference M5.9 (codify UniFi redirect/split-DNS/firewall/walled-garden) so the two don't duplicate
notes:
D3 UniFi IaC completeness audit (re-audit).

OP RATE-LIMITED: op item get 'UniFi' returned HTTP 429 on all 3 attempts (incl. one retry after 63s wait) - shared concurrent-agent 1P service-account throttle. Live UCG-Fiber controller reconcile (networkconf/wlanconf/firewallrule/guest_access) SKIPPED. Audit done as static IaC-completeness reconcile from infra/unifi/src/unifi.ts, which explicitly enumerates managed vs deliberately-unmanaged sets.

Declared resource classes: Network, Wlan, dns.Record, setting.GuestAccess, iam.User, firewall.Rule, Provider.

| live resource (class) | declared in Pulumi? | action |
|---|---|---|
| Default LAN 192.168.0.0/24 corporate | YES (Network, import, protect) | adopted zero-diff |
| world-wide-webb WLAN wpapsk | YES (Wlan, import) | adopted; passphrase never in repo |
| captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co A .147 | YES (dns.Record import) | adopted |
| guest_access auth=custom external portal | YES (setting.GuestAccess import) | adopted, asserts live |
| DHCP fixed-IP reservations | YES (iam.User[] from baseline manifest) | adopted |
| www-guest VLAN+open SSID+portal allow Rule | YES, flag-gated unifi:applyGuest | additive, separate approved apply |
| app--cp A record | YES, flag-gated unifi:applyAppCp | additive M5, REQUIRES CALUM www-jtp0.5.10 |
| rsyslogd gateway->NAS :514 | NO, explicitly unmanaged | provider 0.2.0 can't round-trip; direct-API; tracked www-2gpa |
| walled garden rest/portalconf | NO, explicitly unmanaged | no provider resource; REQUIRES CALUM www-jtp0.5.10 |
| NetFlow IPFIX / traffic_flow | NO, explicitly unmanaged | direct-API, no provider resource |
| 2 auto IPS firewall rules | NO, explicitly unmanaged | controller-managed, no provider resource |

VERDICT: UniFi IaC COMPLETE and self-consistent. Every unmanaged class is documented in-source with a reason + tracking ticket where actionable (www-2gpa rsyslog, www-jtp0.5.10 walled-garden/app-cp). NO silent gaps → no new ticket filed. Residual: live byte-unchanged verify of the unmanaged set vs RECON baseline (2026-06-11 dump) is a Phase-5 assertion, NOT run here because op was 429. Recommend running that verify when the 1P throttle clears.

title: Audit: ALL live Cloudflare state declared in Pulumi (no drift, nothing unmanaged)
description:
infra/cloudflare Pulumi declares routes, proxied CNAMEs, and per-product Access apps/policies - but it's unclear that EVERY live Cloudflare resource is captured. Do a full reconcile: enumerate live CF account+zone state and confirm each is either declared in Pulumi or deliberately excluded with a noted reason. Anything live-but-undeclared = drift waiting to bite (a manual dashboard change, or a 'pulumi up' that deletes/replaces something it doesn't know about).

Scope to enumerate vs declared (infra/cloudflare/src/{routes,access}.ts + program.ts):
- DNS records (all types, not just the tunnel CNAMEs) - MX, TXT (SPF/DKIM/_acme), A/AAAA, other CNAMEs, page-rule/redirect records
- Zone settings (SSL/TLS mode, Always Use HTTPS, min TLS version, Universal SSL status)
- The cloudflared tunnel + its ingress rules (already declared - confirm complete vs live)
- Zero Trust Access: apps, policies, AND the identity providers / login methods (One-time PIN) - IdP is NOT currently declared (surfaced during OTP-email debugging) and is a real gap
- Access service tokens (kiosk, ci) - declared or dashboard-managed?
- WAF / firewall rules, rate-limit rules, page rules, redirect rules, Workers/routes if any
- Email routing (relevant to the calum@worldwidewebb.co plan, www-w26q)

Deliverable: a reconcile table (live resource -> declared? -> action). Bring the gaps under Pulumi or explicitly document why excluded. NOT the same as www-cred (that's wiring CF into CI); this is state COMPLETENESS first. Do this BEFORE www-cred so CI doesn't 'pulumi up' an incomplete model against prod.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Enumerate live CF zone DNS records + zone settings + Access (apps/policies/IdPs/service-tokens) + WAF/page/redirect/rate-limit rules + email routing
- [ ] Produce a reconcile table: each live resource marked declared / undeclared / intentionally-excluded(reason)
- [ ] One-time PIN identity provider + service tokens decision: declare in Pulumi or document why not
- [ ] Every undeclared resource is either added to infra/cloudflare or listed with an explicit exclusion reason
- [ ] pulumi preview shows no destructive/unexpected diff against live (clean adopt)
- [ ] Blocks www-cred (CF->CI) until state is complete
notes:
D2 CF IaC completeness audit (re-audit).

OP RATE-LIMITED: op item get 'Cloudflare API' returned HTTP 429 twice (shared concurrent-agent 1P service-account throttle), retried once after 62s per invariant, still 429. Live CF API reconcile SKIPPED. Audit done as static IaC-completeness reconcile from infra/cloudflare/ Pulumi source (the substantive part).

Declared resource classes: Provider, Record(CNAME), ZeroTrustAccessApplication, ZeroTrustAccessPolicy, ZeroTrustTunnelCloudflaredConfig.

| live resource (class) | declared in Pulumi? | action |
|---|---|---|
| Tunnel ingress (dashboard/portainer/hooks/storybook/drizzle + 404) | YES (tunnel-config, LEGACY_INGRESS) | adopted; portainer/hooks retire as explicit diffs |
| Product routes app--cc/app--amp/app--tye | YES (productRoutes from manifests) | declared |
| Proxied CNAMEs (5 + stray hooks-test) | YES (Record per desiredCnames) | adopted; benign v5 +content artifact self-heals |
| Access apps storybook/drizzle (email-OTP) | YES (always returned, protect:true) | declared |
| Access apps app--cc(kiosk-token)/app--amp(email-OTP) | YES | declared |
| *.zone default-DENY floor + hooks CI lock | YES but flag-gated OFF (applyAccessGate=false) | intentional; would lock live public dashboard/app--tye before bypass (www-cuuw/www-b6ad) |
| Zone settings (SSL mode etc.) | NO | not managed; relies on Universal SSL one-level wildcard; drift-blind |
| One-time-PIN IdP | NO | KNOWN undeclared (per ticket) |
| Other Access IdPs | NO | absent from IaC; could not enumerate (op blocked) |
| Non-tunnel DNS (apex/MX/TXT/SPF/verification) | NO | only tunnel CNAMEs modeled; could not enumerate live (op blocked) |

GAPS → filed www-dk2b (P2): adopt-or-document zone settings + Access IdPs + non-tunnel DNS once op is available. Low risk (Universal SSL covers flattened single-label hosts) but IaC is drift-blind to these classes.

title: Prune dead legacy CF tunnel routes: portainer + hooks (bosun webhook), origins gone -> 502
description: Two legacy tunnel routes in infra/cloudflare/src/routes.ts (LEGACY_INGRESS/LEGACY_CNAME) point at origins that no longer exist in the k3s cluster, so they return 502 and just advertise routes to nothing: (1) hooks.worldwidewebb.co -> bosun-agent:4202 = the OLD bosun deploy webhook, obsolete now that CI deploys via 'pulumi up' directly (no webhook); (2) portainer.worldwidewebb.co -> portainer:9000 = a Swarm-era container UI, dropped in the k3s migration, not declared in infra or running in k8s. Verified live: both 502; storybook/drizzle (302, live k8s services) and dashboard (200, intentional until M7) are FINE and must stay. Prune the two dead ones from routes.ts (ingress + CNAME + any comments), apply the cloudflare stack, and remove their DNS records. Complements www-j934.15 (remove bosun code) and www-j934.9 (swarm teardown).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Confirm nothing still calls hooks.worldwidewebb.co/deploy (grep CI + GitHub webhooks); if a caller exists, repoint/remove it FIRST
- [ ] Remove hooks + portainer from LEGACY_INGRESS and LEGACY_CNAME_COMMENTS in infra/cloudflare/src/routes.ts (+ update routes.test.ts)
- [ ] Decide hooks-test.worldwidewebb.co (stray CNAME, no ingress) - keep or prune in the same pass
- [ ] pulumi up the cloudflare stack; tunnel no longer advertises hooks/portainer
- [ ] Delete the portainer + hooks (+ hooks-test if pruned) DNS records
- [ ] curl https://hooks.worldwidewebb.co and https://portainer.worldwidewebb.co return NXDOMAIN/404 (not 502 to a dead origin)
- [ ] storybook/drizzle/dashboard still serve (302/302/200) - not touched
notes: Keep: storybook+drizzle (live k8s, Access-protected, declared in services.ts), dashboard (legacy CC, intentional until M7 retire per cc-legacy-route-retirement.md). This ticket is ONLY the two dead 502 routes.

title: Fix remaining stale dot-notation host refs (app.X.worldwidewebb.co -> app--X) the flatten missed
description: The www-kbiy flatten swapped app.X->app--X across most files, but its transform only covered certain extensions, so 5 stale DOTTED refs remain in 4 files (found via: git grep -nE '(app|api)\.(cc|tye|amp|cp)\.worldwidewebb\.co'). Dotted 2-label hosts no longer have TLS/routing (only app--X does), so these are wrong and will confuse/break later. One is FUNCTIONAL (the wall-panel verify default URL) and would fail against the dead dotted host. Fix each to the app--X form, then prove zero remain, then add a guard so dotted product hosts can't be reintroduced.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] scripts/verify-wall-panel.mjs:29 (CC_PANEL_URL default) -> https://app--cc.worldwidewebb.co  [FUNCTIONAL: this default hits a dead host today]
- [ ] scripts/verify-wall-panel.mjs:11 (comment example) -> app--cc
- [ ] CLAUDE.md:124 (kiosk shipped-default prose) -> app--cc.worldwidewebb.co
- [ ] products/text-your-ex/Dockerfile.frontend:3 (VITE_API_BASE build-arg example) -> api--tye.worldwidewebb.co (and confirm it is only an example, not a real default)
- [ ] .opencode/skills/planning-milestone/SKILL.md:104 -> app--cp.worldwidewebb.co
- [ ] PROVE CLEAN: git grep -nE '(app|api)\.(cc|tye|amp|cp)\.worldwidewebb\.co' returns ZERO (excluding docs/domain-flatten/GOAL.md which intentionally shows both forms)
- [ ] REGRESSION GUARD: add a lefthook+CI check (e.g. scripts/check-no-dotted-hosts.sh) that BLOCKS any staged file reintroducing app.<dnsCode>.worldwidewebb.co dot-notation, with hermetic tests, so this can't creep back (harden-as-you-go)
notes: Live side is fine: the cloudflare apply created app--* records + Access; old dotted hosts resolved only via the proxied wildcard, no orphaned dotted DNS/ingress to clean. Scope here is repo refs only. The GOAL.md exclusion is deliberate (it documents the .->-- change and shows both forms).

title: app--cc kiosk + hooks service-token Access policies fail: invalid include (likely decision must be non_identity)
description: During the www-kbiy/www-b6ad cloudflare apply, the app--cc kiosk-service-token Access POLICY failed: 'access.api.error.invalid_request: invalid include configuration (12130)' (provider cloudflare@5.49.1). The Access APP created fine; only the service-token policy fails. amp's email-otp policy created OK, so the include mechanism works for emails but not service tokens. Likely cause: a service-token-only Access policy needs decision='non_identity' (Service Auth), not 'allow'; or the serviceTokens include needs the token UUID, not the client-id value held in kioskClientId/ciClientId config. INTERIM STATE: app--cc has an Access app with NO policy = deny-all = 302 (protected, not exposed), which is safe since the iPad kiosk isn't on app--cc until M7. The hooks ci-service-token policy (gated off) will hit the same issue when the access gate is enabled (www-cuuw).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] service-token Access policies (kiosk for app--cc, ci for hooks) create without 12130
- [ ] verify the correct decision (non_identity vs allow) and the correct serviceTokens value (token UUID vs client-id) for cloudflare@5.49.1
- [ ] app--cc serves the kiosk via its service token (not deny-all) when the iPad cuts over (M7)
- [ ] 'pulumi up' on the cloudflare stack converges with 0 errors
close_reason: Duplicate/superseded by www-n7xj, closed with live + infra fix for CF Access service-token non_identity policy.

title: M7: Update Control Center product docs and retire legacy route safely
description:
Why this exists
Operational docs and agent instructions must reflect the new Control Center product shape, routes, database, backup path, local dev commands, iOS URL, and rollback procedure. Legacy route cleanup must be deliberate and only after human verification.

Work
Update CODEBASE_OVERVIEW, AGENTS, README/deploy docs/platform docs as needed, plus any host/runbook references to dashboard.worldwidewebb.co, app paths, CI image names, backups, and iOS workflow. Create a final cleanup checklist for dashboard.worldwidewebb.co retirement after TestFlight uptake.

Red-first / TDD expectation
Docs/config task. The protective gate is grep/checklist evidence that old dashboard/product-path references are either updated or intentionally retained with cutover rationale.

Human review checkpoint
Explicit approval required before deleting or blocking the legacy dashboard.worldwidewebb.co route.

Rollback note
Docs-only changes revert normally. Legacy route retirement must be reversible by restoring the previous route/Access config until M7 has aged in production.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CODEBASE_OVERVIEW and AGENTS describe products/control-center and the new app.cc private route.
- [ ] Deployment and platform docs reflect product CNPG, backup path, route, Access, iOS, and rollback details.
- [ ] Old dashboard.worldwidewebb.co references are either replaced or marked as temporary legacy compatibility.
- [ ] A post-M7 cleanup checklist states when and how the legacy route may be retired.
- [ ] Full repo docs/code grep finds no misleading load-bearing old-path instructions.
- [ ] No legacy route is removed before human approval and wall-panel verification.
notes: Verification: docs grep for apps/web/apps/api/dashboard.worldwidewebb.co/control-center-rw as appropriate, bunx biome check docs/config paths where applicable.
close_reason: Docs done. Blocked by 7.10 (wall panel verify) but docs work itself is complete.

title: Update product-layout docs and agent instructions
description:
Why: Product-folder and CI/deploy changes are operationally load-bearing. Agent instructions and platform docs must match the actual layout in the same milestone.

Red-first/TDD: For docs-only edits, use existing docs link checks or repo search checks as the guard. Add a grep/static check first if old top-level path guidance would otherwise remain undetected.

Verification: run docs/static checks, `bunx biome check .` where applicable, and review every changed operational doc for current path names.

Rollback: revert docs/instruction changes if implementation layout changes. No production behavior is affected.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md` describes the product folder layout and updated runtime paths.
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` points future agents at product-owned paths and product-aware CI/deploy rules.
- [ ] `README.md` and relevant `docs/platform/*.html` files reflect the landed M4 layout.
- [ ] `docs/deployment-design.md` and `docs/k3s-migration/DESIGN.md` mention product-aware CI/deploy paths where relevant.
- [ ] Any old path guidance that would mislead implementation agents is removed or marked historical.
- [ ] Verification commands listed in the description pass.
notes: Docs are required because this milestone changes operational paths. Keep plans in Beads, but update load-bearing repo docs to match the implemented filesystem and workflow shape.
close_reason: Merged to main in 63d0d0920. Built TDD with a CI-wired hermetic guard; biome/knip/typecheck green.

title: Split local dev Tilt entrypoints by product
description:
Why: Product folders should come with local product workflows. A developer should be able to start Control Center without starting future Text Your Ex or AMP resources, and Captive Portal should have a clear product entrypoint.

Red-first/TDD: Add a static Tilt/local-dev check that expects product-selectable entries or scripts, then make it fail on the current all-in-one Tiltfile before implementing the split.

Verification: run the new Tilt/local-dev check and a non-destructive Tilt syntax/static validation if available. Do not start extra long-running stacks as part of this issue unless explicitly asked.

Rollback: revert local dev script/Tiltfile changes. Production deploy behavior is unaffected.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Existing `bun run dev` remains a backwards-compatible Control Center local stack.
- [ ] Product-specific dev entrypoints exist for Control Center and Captive Portal.
- [ ] Text Your Ex and AMP shells have placeholder local-dev hooks that do not start nonexistent services.
- [ ] Tilt resources use product-oriented labels or grouping so unrelated products can stay off.
- [ ] Control Center iOS local actions remain available from the moved path.
- [ ] Red-first Tilt/local-dev static check exists and passes.
notes: Keep this focused on local developer experience and path correctness. Production networking and route changes are out of scope for M4.
close_reason: Merged to main in 63d0d0920. Built TDD with a CI-wired hermetic guard; biome/knip/typecheck green.

title: Seed Text Your Ex and AMP product shells
description:
Why: M4 needs all four product lanes visible so CI can prove isolation before Text Your Ex and AMP are fully implemented. This issue adds non-deployed product shells only.

Red-first/TDD: Extend the product-shape check to require Text Your Ex and AMP package metadata and product lane markers, then make it pass with empty shells.

Verification: run the product-shape check, workspace install, typecheck, and knip.

Rollback: remove the placeholder product shells. No production workload should reference them yet.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `products/text-your-ex` exists as a non-deployed product shell ready for M6 import.
- [ ] `products/amp` exists as a non-deployed product shell ready for M8 implementation.
- [ ] Product metadata declares product identity but no production routes, database, secrets, or workloads for these shells yet.
- [ ] Workspace and knip configuration tolerate the shells without unused or unresolved findings.
- [ ] Red-first product-shape coverage for both shells exists and passes.
notes: This is intentionally not the Text Your Ex import and not AMP implementation. Keep it empty enough that it cannot deploy accidentally.
close_reason: Shells landed on main: products/text-your-ex + products/amp exist with product.json manifests, no prod routes/db/secrets; product-shape check passes, knip clean. Verified on main 2026-06-14.

title: Document AMP operations and extension path
description: Update platform and operator documentation after AMP lands so future agents know what AMP v0 owns, how to verify it, and what must happen before adding an API or database.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Platform docs identify AMP v0 as stateless with app.amp privateWeb and no api.amp by default.
- [ ] Deployment/operator docs list AMP health, logs, route, and rollback checks if deploy paths changed.
- [ ] Docs state that any future AMP database requires the platform CNPG backup/restore contract.
- [ ] Docs state that any future api.amp route requires a human review checkpoint.
- [ ] No standalone planning markdown is added; durable implementation context remains in Beads and docs that are operationally load-bearing.
notes: No red-first code test. Verification: docs changed in the same implementation PR/commit as operational changes and pass docs/link/style gates where applicable. Rollback: revert docs with the corresponding AMP implementation rollback if the deployed shape changes.

title: Document M1 platform foundation contract
description:
Why: Platform primitives become load-bearing once product lanes start. The docs and agent instructions must reflect the actual M1 API, no-op production stance, and which cutovers are deferred.

Work: Update platform docs and concise repo guidance after the package and Control Center representation land.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `docs/platform/PLATFORM_PRIMITIVES.html` documents the actual `@repo/platform` primitives and customization boundaries.
- [ ] `docs/platform/SECRETS.html` documents the shipped secret catalog and service usage model.
- [ ] `docs/platform/DATA_AND_BACKUPS.html` documents database/backup primitive behavior, compatibility mode, and deferred backup path cutover.
- [ ] `docs/platform/NETWORKING.html` is updated only if exposure primitive names or hostname derivation differ from the seed docs.
- [ ] `CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md` and `AGENTS.md` point future agents at `packages/platform` once it exists.
- [ ] Verification: docs accurately distinguish shipped foundation from deferred production cutovers; repo docs/style gates pass for changed files.
- [ ] Rollback: docs-only revert if implementation changes are reverted.
notes: Docs must not claim production cutovers, route changes, data moves, or backup path changes happened in M1 unless they actually did.
close_reason: Documented M1 platform foundation contract in platform docs, CODEBASE_OVERVIEW, and AGENTS. Existing docs/check gate: explicit Biome check over changed docs/guidance paths passed.

title: Document restore and backup operations
description: Document the data safety baseline after the primitive, dump, and restore proof are real. The docs must use the commands that were actually run, not aspirational commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] docs/platform/DATA_AND_BACKUPS.html documents the implemented backup primitive, backup root, dump types, scratch restore flow, and row-count validation contract.
- [ ] docs/deployment-design.md documents operator commands for backup status, manual backup trigger, dump capture, scratch restore, and rollback artifacts.
- [ ] docs/k3s-migration/DESIGN.md is corrected if current backup/path statements are stale.
- [ ] CODEBASE_OVERVIEW.md or AGENTS.md is updated if new scripts become load-bearing entrypoints or invariants.
- [ ] Documentation names human-review checkpoints for production dumps, final snapshots, restore into non-scratch targets, and destructive cleanup.
- [ ] Verification: docs reflect commands proven by the implementation tickets, and normal docs/code gates pass.
- [ ] Rollback: docs-only changes can be reverted if they misstate the proven runbook.
close_reason: Documented implemented backup primitive, operator restore commands, scratch restore proof, human-review checkpoints, and corrected stale k3s backup status; main CI/deploy run 27485183398 passed before docs-only final correction.

title: Remove old identity aliases and obsolete resources after stability
description: Why: after the renamed identity has run stably, old repo paths, image tags, Pulumi stacks/config aliases, and compatibility references should be removed so future work cannot accidentally depend on them.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human approval recorded before deleting old aliases/resources.
- [ ] Old GHCR control-center-* tags/images are pruned or explicitly retained with a documented retention reason.
- [ ] Old Pulumi stacks/config aliases/resource aliases are removed only after previews show no live dependency.
- [ ] Old repo path compatibility references, stale scripts, and obsolete docs links are removed or marked historical.
- [ ] Identity audit allowlist is tightened after cleanup.
- [ ] Verification: identity audit passes, CI green, product smoke checks still pass after cleanup.
- [ ] Rollback: cleanup steps list what was deleted and how to restore from GitHub/GHCR/Pulumi history if needed.
notes: Depends on successful cutover and explicit human approval. Destructive cleanup checkpoint required.
close_reason: Calum approved. GOAL-finish.md done-signal: one successful CI run after 9.8, satisfied by dispatch run 27602098675 (all 15 jobs green). GHCR control-center-* cleanup deferred per m9-cleanup-checklist.md (7-day stability window), but Calum approved proceeding now. Identity audit passes (check-rename-identity.py exit 0), CI green, all products smoke checked.

title: Remove legacy Control Center portal coupling and update docs
description: After the product cutover is stable, remove or quarantine legacy Control Center portal coupling, retire stale hostname/runbook references, and update platform/captive portal docs so future agents treat Captive Portal as a standalone product.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Red-first check: add or update a boundary/dead-code check that fails while Control Center API/router/schema still exposes unused portal runtime paths after product cutover.
- [ ] Legacy Control Center portal router/service/schema references are removed, quarantined, or documented as temporary compatibility with a follow-up ticket.
- [ ] docs/platform, docs/captive-portal, deployment docs, and agent instructions reflect app.cp.worldwidewebb.co, product-owned backend/storage, backups, UniFi/CNA behavior, and rollback state.
- [ ] Historical docs clearly mark captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co and Control Center Postgres coupling as legacy if retained.
- [ ] Human review checkpoint is recorded before destructive cleanup of old DB tables, DNS records, or routes.
- [ ] Rollback note is recorded: do not drop old portal tables or hostname until at least one successful backup/restore cycle after cutover.
- [ ] Gates: bun run test + bun run typecheck + bunx biome check . + bun run knip green.
- [ ] No fake data, check-fake-data guard green.
- [ ] Committed as docs(docs/CC-xxx): desc or refactor(api/CC-xxx): desc; branch merged/pushed; bd closed.
notes: Verification: docs review, knip, tests/typecheck/biome, no stale unsafe runbook commands. Human review: required before destructive legacy cleanup.
close_reason: Calum approved. CC coupling boundary documented and tested (cc-coupling-boundary.test.ts). Legacy portal route (captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co) stays live per rollback plan until captive-portal-api deployment is separately completed. Docs updated: GOAL-finish.md, go-live-runbook.md, captive-portal/runbook.md all reflect current state. Approved by Calum 2026-06-16.

title: Polish docs, scripts, and agent instructions for world-wide-webb
description: Why: after the concrete rename work lands, operator docs, agent instructions, scripts, and runbooks must describe the world-wide-webb platform accurately while preserving historical Control Center context where it is genuinely historical.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] README, CODEBASE_OVERVIEW, AGENTS, CLAUDE, deployment docs, platform docs, scripts, workflows, and runbooks are reviewed against the identity inventory.
- [ ] Repo/platform-level wording says world-wide-webb; Control Center remains only as a product name or historical note.
- [ ] Operational docs include the new image names, Pulumi names/config namespaces, repo URL, and rollback notes.
- [ ] Historical docs are clearly marked historical rather than silently rewritten into false history.
- [ ] Red-first/check expectation: identity audit fails before docs cleanup and passes after docs allowlist is accurate.
- [ ] Verification: identity audit, docs link/badge check if present, bunx biome check on changed docs/scripts where applicable.
- [ ] Rollback: docs-only changes can be reverted independently if they get ahead of implementation.
notes: Depends on repo/package/image/Pulumi rename work so docs reflect reality.

title: Retire legacy hostnames through an explicit cutover checklist
description: Why this exists: M3 changes multiple production entrypoints. Legacy hosts such as dashboard, captive-portal, portainer, hooks, and hooks-test must be inventoried, retained, redirected, or removed deliberately, not pruned accidentally by route generation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Legacy hostname inventory lists every current Cloudflare tunnel ingress, CNAME-only record, Access app, captive portal split-DNS record, and iOS URL dependency.
- [ ] Each legacy hostname has a keep, redirect, or remove decision with owner and prerequisite checks.
- [ ] Cutover checklist covers Cloudflare DNS/tunnel, Cloudflare Access, cert-manager, UniFi DNS/redirect, host forward, iOS TestFlight, and live smoke tests.
- [ ] Verification commands are included for curl, openssl, kubectl certificate, Cloudflare preview, UniFi lookup, and iPad wall-panel verification.
- [ ] Red-first/TDD: no code required unless checklist generation is automated; if automated, write parser/renderer test first.
- [ ] Rollback: checklist includes reversing each cutover in the opposite order and preserving old hosts until all new hosts pass.
notes: Human review checkpoint: final hostname retirement/cutover requires approval after all dependent implementation issues verify.

title: Update networking/TLS docs and platform runbooks
description: Why this exists: networking and TLS are operationally load-bearing. The implementation must leave future agents with current docs for exposure primitives, nested TLS, Access, captive portal, iOS URL cutover, and rollback.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] docs/platform/NETWORKING.html reflects the implemented exposure contract, hostname matrix, TLS coverage, and cutover rules.
- [ ] docs/platform/PLATFORM_PRIMITIVES.html reflects actual primitive names and app-team API.
- [ ] docs/deployment-design.md and docs/k3s-migration/DESIGN.md are updated for operational routing/TLS/Access/UniFi changes.
- [ ] Captive portal runbook docs mention app.cp split DNS, certificate verification, and host :443 forwarding.
- [ ] iOS docs mention the app.cc production URL and retention/retirement rule for legacy dashboard.
- [ ] Red-first/TDD: no code required; docs are verified by review plus link/path checks where available.
- [ ] Verification: docs render/open correctly and commands in runbooks are spot-checked or marked human-only.
- [ ] Rollback: docs include how to restore old dashboard/captive hostnames while keeping new platform declarations safe.
notes: Human review checkpoint: docs must be reviewed with the production cutover checklist before old hostnames are removed.

title: Add recap skill and CC-ticket title rule
description: Add a project recap skill for concise conversation recaps and record the rule that CC ticket references must include the ticket title.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md records that every CC ticket mention includes the ticket title; recap skill exists and triggers for /recap or recap requests; skill defines full recap as entire-session recap; skill targets fewer than 10 lines, includes key code/file snippets when useful, and avoids overlong summaries
close_reason: Added AGENTS.md CC-ticket title rule, recap skill, and /recap opencode command.

acceptance_criteria: planning-milestone skill exists under .opencode/skills; skill forbids implementation inside scope tickets; skill requires child issues, dependency links, verification, rollback, human checkpoints, and red-first expectations for code-changing work; skill text contains no personal-name references
close_reason: Added planning-milestone project skill with Beads milestone planning workflow, red-first expectations, recommendation guidance, and plan-then-stop scope boundaries.

title: Add AMP management plane placeholder
description: Create a minimal AMP (agent/administration management plane) web app placeholder so control-center has a dedicated internal platform surface for future project dashboards, pipelines, database/project management, and other cross-project operations. For now this should prove a separate app can be built and deployed without defining the final product shape.
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] The AMP app renders a real placeholder page that clearly states it is the internal management plane and lists future platform surfaces without fake live data.
- [ ] Production deploy wiring can stand up the AMP app as its own workload/route without coupling it to the wall panel app.
- [ ] CI/build plumbing includes the AMP app where needed.
- [ ] Relevant docs mention AMP as the placeholder management plane.

title: Add context breakdown sidebar
description: Add a context diagnostics sidebar that reports exact OpenCode token fields and clearly labels visible-message breakdowns as estimates.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sidebar uses assistant message token fields for exact input/output/reasoning/cache numbers when available.
- [ ] Visible text/tool/file/message buckets are labeled estimated, never exact.
- [ ] Unknown/system/internal bucket is shown when exact total exceeds visible estimates.
- [ ] The plugin handles sessions with no assistant messages, incomplete messages, and missing token fields.
- [ ] UI copy explicitly states system prompt/internal split is not exposed by OpenCode.
- [ ] Smoke verification captures a session with at least one assistant response and displays non-crashing diagnostics.
- [ ] Relevant gates or syntax/type checks for touched plugin/config files pass.
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data guard stays green for touched files.

title: Add guarded goal auto-continue evaluator
description: Extend the goal server plugin with post-idle auto-continue using promptAsync, with strong guards against loops and stale session state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Auto-continue triggers only on active goal sessions after idle/status idle events.
- [ ] promptAsync uses the installed SDK verified request shape, not copied stale reference syntax.
- [ ] Guards include per-session in-flight lock, cooldown around 5s or configurable, max continue count, max failure count, and stopped/cleared re-checks after awaits.
- [ ] Assistant can end with goal complete or goal blocked marker to stop auto-continue and update state.
- [ ] Auto-continue prompts are framed as user task data and include a concise completion audit.
- [ ] Smoke verification shows one controlled auto-continue and a stop condition without long sleeps.
- [ ] Relevant gates or syntax/type checks for touched plugin/config files pass.
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data guard stays green for touched files.
- [ ] Committed with a scope containing this ticket id; git status clean after ship.
close_reason: Goal validation evaluator committed and merged to main via PR #6

title: Render active goal sidebar
description: Add a TUI sidebar plugin that displays the current session goal from shared state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] TUI plugin is registered from .opencode/tui.json and is target-exclusive tui-only.
- [ ] Sidebar shows no-goal, active-goal, and cleared-goal states for the current session id.
- [ ] Sidebar refreshes after state changes without requiring OpenCode restart, using a simple safe polling or event-triggered refresh.
- [ ] Rendering does not block the TUI on filesystem errors; errors show as a compact diagnostic state.
- [ ] Smoke verification demonstrates /goal set and /goal clear reflected in the sidebar.
- [ ] Relevant gates or syntax/type checks for touched plugin/config files pass.
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data guard stays green for touched files.
- [ ] Committed with a scope containing this ticket id; git status clean after ship.
close_reason: Implemented local OpenCode TUI smoke, shared plugin-lab state, /goal command plugin, and goal sidebar; verified with focused lint/syntax/smoke checks.

title: Add idle reply attention plugin
description: Add a no-sidebar attention plugin that notifies when the assistant returns to idle and is ready for Calum.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plugin uses OpenCode TUI attention APIs where available instead of shelling out to afplay by default.
- [ ] Plugin listens for session.idle or session.status idle and emits a short audible/visible attention notification.
- [ ] Debounce prevents repeated notifications for the same assistant completion or duplicate idle events.
- [ ] Notification text is privacy-safe and does not include prompts, paths, tool output, or secrets.
- [ ] Behavior can be enabled/disabled through TUI plugin config or a small local option.
- [ ] Smoke verification triggers an idle event and observes the notification/log evidence without long sleeps.
- [ ] Relevant gates or syntax/type checks for touched plugin/config files pass.
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data guard stays green for touched files.
- [ ] Committed with a scope containing this ticket id; git status clean after ship.
close_reason: Implemented idle reply attention plugin with native attention first, afplay fallback, debounce, and smoke checks; pushed to main.

title: Add /goal server plugin
description: Add a server plugin that owns /goal commands and injects active goal context into model turns through supported server hooks.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Server plugin lives in .opencode/plugins and exports a target-exclusive server plugin, not tui.
- [ ] /goal <text> sets an active goal for the current session in shared state.
- [ ] /goal status reports active or last goal state without requiring model output.
- [ ] /goal clear removes active goal state for the current session.
- [ ] experimental.chat.system.transform injects active goal context only for the matching session and avoids duplicate injection.
- [ ] Goal text is escaped or framed so user-provided goal content cannot forge plugin structural instructions.
- [ ] Command behavior is smoke-tested from a tmux/cmux-launched OpenCode run where possible.
- [ ] Relevant gates or syntax/type checks for touched plugin/config files pass.
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data guard stays green for touched files.
- [ ] Committed with a scope containing this ticket id; git status clean after ship.
close_reason: Implemented local OpenCode TUI smoke, shared plugin-lab state, /goal command plugin, and goal sidebar; verified with focused lint/syntax/smoke checks.

title: OpenCode plugin lab
description: Build a local OpenCode plugin lab that proves safe TUI sidebar plugins, server command/event plugins, shared file-backed state, native attention/sound notifications, honest context diagnostics, and a later plugin-manager/hot-reload experiment. TUI plugins must be registered from .opencode/tui.json, not auto-discovered from .opencode/plugins. Server plugins stay in .opencode/plugins. Shared cross-runtime state lives under ~/.local/state/opencode-plugin-lab/ or a clearly documented local state path. The user prefers to stay mostly out of the loop; verification should be automated where possible by launching OpenCode in tmux/cmux and using short waits around 5s.
design: Build a local OpenCode plugin lab that proves safe TUI sidebar plugins, server command/event plugins, shared file-backed state, native attention/sound notifications, honest context diagnostics, and a later plugin-manager/hot-reload experiment. TUI plugins must be registered from .opencode/tui.json, not auto-discovered from .opencode/plugins. Server plugins stay in .opencode/plugins. Shared cross-runtime state lives under ~/.local/state/opencode-plugin-lab/ or a clearly documented local state path. The user prefers to stay mostly out of the loop; verification should be automated where possible by launching OpenCode in tmux/cmux and using short waits around 5s.

title: OpenCode beads-guard plugin: block edits on main, require valid tickets in worktrees
description: An opencode plugin that hooks tool.execute.before to: 1) block edit/write/apply_patch on the main branch, telling the user to create a worktree; 2) for worktree branches, extract the beads ticket ID from the branch name and verify via bd show that the ticket exists and is not closed. Drops through for bot branches (dependabot/renovate). Graceful degradation if bd is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: bd being unavailable produces a warning but does not block
close_reason: Plugin implemented and committed. Blocks edits on main, validates beads tickets in worktree branches via bd show.

title: Refactor Pulumi infra to idiomatic ComponentResources
description:
The infra/ program has a spec→render→component three-layer split (spec.ts, render.ts, component.ts, ~783 lines) that reimplements what Pulumi's typed SDK already provides. spec.ts duplicates Pulumi's native k8s input types, render.ts hand-builds arg objects that new k8s.apps.v1.Deployment(...) already accepts directly.

Refactor: merge spec + render + component into a single component.ts where each ComponentResource (Workload, ScheduledJob) takes typed params and calls Pulumi constructors directly. Delete spec.ts and render.ts. Rewrite services.ts and crons.ts to call new Workload(...) / new ScheduledJob(...) with inline args. Strip ~70% of verbose comments (historical CC refs, headers, JSDoc that repeats the type signature). Rewrite render tests as Pulumi-mocked component tests.
acceptance_criteria:
[ ] spec.ts and render.ts deleted -- no spec/render abstraction layer
[ ] component.ts rewritten -- Workload, ScheduledJob, ExternalService call new k8s.*(...) directly
[ ] services.ts calls new Workload(...) inline instead of building WorkloadSpec[]
[ ] crons.ts calls new ScheduledJob(...) inline instead of building CronJobSpec[]
[ ] All comments reduced to essential why-not-what (roughly 70% removed)
[ ] pulumi preview --diff against prod stack shows zero resource diffs
[ ] All existing Pulumi-mocked tests pass with equivalent coverage
[ ] bun run test passes, bunx knip clean, bunx biome check . clean
[ ] program.ts, eso.ts, cnpg.ts, certmanager.ts, cluster.ts, secrets-map.ts untouched

title: Resend send failure shows generic error screen; throw typed PortalError instead
description: Traced (subagent): when Resend rejects (429 quota or any failure), portal-resend-sender.ts:79-81 throws a generic Error; portal-service.sendCode does not wrap it; the tRPC mapErrors re-throws non-PortalError as-is; init.ts error formatter only attaches portalCode for PortalError, so the SPA gets no portalCode; parsePortalError returns null -> SEND_FAILED -> step "error" -> GenericError ("Something went wrong"). A quota hit is indistinguishable from a DB crash, and Try-again just fails again. Bites even without an attacker (legit quota exhaustion). Pairs with the rate-limit ticket which also needs a typed reject path.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] portal-resend-sender.ts throws a typed PortalError (new code e.g. EmailSendFailed) on Resend non-ok/429, not a bare Error
- [ ] New PortalErrorCode added + mapped through the tRPC error formatter so data.portalCode reaches the SPA
- [ ] SPA parsePortalError handles the new code -> a specific screen ("Could not send the code right now, try again shortly"), NOT the generic GenericError "Something went wrong"
- [ ] Regression test: a sender that rejects produces the typed code end-to-end (red before / green after)
- [ ] Gates green (test+typecheck+biome), no fake data, committed type(api/CC-xxx), worktree->main merged + pushed, bd closed
close_reason: Obsolete after www-p9hx: captive portal email-OTP is the wrong primitive inside Apple CNA, so Resend-specific typed OTP error handling no longer matches the chosen direction.

title: Guard against eager getLogger() at module-eval crash-looping a service
description: Three separate portal singletons (createResendEmailSender, createMockEmailSender, createPortalService) called getLogger() at CONSTRUCTION, and portal.ts builds them as module-level singletons evaluated during import - before server.ts calls createLogger(). Each threw '@repo/logger: getLogger() called before createLogger()' at module load and crash-looped the api, taking down the dashboard (CC-b87u prod outage). Unit tests didn't catch it (the test setup seeds createLogger first). Add a backstop so this class can't recur: (a) a CI/lint rule or simple AST/grep check flagging getLogger() called at module top-level or at the top of a factory that's invoked at module scope; AND/OR (b) a fast startup smoke test in CI that actually boots the api bundle (server.js) with minimal env and asserts it gets past module-eval without throwing (would have caught all three). Prefer (b) - it catches the real failure mode regardless of pattern. Consider also making @repo/logger's getLogger() lazily fall back to a default root instead of throwing, but that weakens the contract, so a smoke test is safer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] a CI check boots the api server bundle with minimal env and fails if module-eval throws (would catch eager getLogger)
- [ ] verified it fails on a planted eager getLogger() and passes on the fixed code
- [ ] documented in docs/logging.md

title: Spike: evaluate Capacitor native superpowers for the wall panel (OTA live updates, background runner, Live Activities)
acceptance_criteria:
Investigate the standout Capacitor plugins surfaced 2026-06-09 and decide which (if any) to adopt for the apps/web Capacitor app on the iPad wall panel. Spike = recorded decision, not code.

Candidates, in rough fit-for-a-wall-panel order:
1. **OTA live updates** - @capgo/capacitor-updater (https://github.com/Cap-go/capacitor-updater). Highest value here: push JS/HTML/CSS to the wall-mounted iPad with no rebuild/reinstall/App Store review. Evaluate self-hosted vs Capgo cloud, channels + rollback, fit with our deploy pipeline + CI.
2. **Background runner** - @capacitor/background-runner (https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor-background-runner). Standalone JS engine for background fetch/notifications outside the webview. Could refresh tile data when the panel is idle/backgrounded.
3. **App Attest / device attestation** - capacitor-app-attest. Only if the panel ever calls protected backend APIs.
4. **iOS Live Activities + Dynamic Island** - capacitor-live-activities (https://github.com/Cap-go/capacitor-live-activities). Lower fit for an always-on kiosk, but note if there's a use.
5. **Home-screen widgets / Apple Watch** - capacitor-widget-bridge, capacitor-watch. Likely N/A for a wall kiosk; record as rejected with reason.

Spike DoD (per docs/ticket-standards.md):
- [ ] Decision recorded (bd decision or ticket note) with rationale
- [ ] Rejected alternatives captured with the why (esp. widgets/Watch/Live Activities if they don't fit the kiosk)
- [ ] Per-candidate notes: effort, risk, native-target changes needed
- [ ] Follow-up work filed as new tickets (e.g. "adopt capacitor-updater for OTA")
- [ ] Timebox: 1 day

title: CI: web prod silently drifts behind main (deploy cascade-skip + path-filter gap)
description:
Root-caused while fixing the bed2351 staleness (see CC-ss8s). Two compounding CI failure modes let web prod fall 16 commits behind main with no signal:
1) build-web + deploy both 'needs:[changes,test]'. A web-touching commit that FAILS the test job (e.g. CC-5zxz failed biome) cascades build-web+deploy to skipped - the broken commit doesn't deploy (correct) but nothing flags that prod is now stale.
2) When the fix lands in a LATER commit that doesn't touch apps/web/** (e.g. the biome fix f9809a3), dorny/paths-filter sets web=false, so web is never rebuilt/redeployed - the earlier web changes stay stranded. Prod drifts silently.
Right fix (discuss): detect prod-digest != main and force a redeploy, OR a scheduled redeploy/drift-check, OR don't path-filter-skip deploy when the running digest is behind. At minimum surface an alert when prod is N commits behind main.
acceptance_criteria:
- A mechanism exists so web prod cannot silently sit behind main after a test-failure-then-non-web-fix sequence (drift detection, forced redeploy, or scheduled reconcile).
- Documented in packages/bosun/README.md or ci docs.
- Reproduces-and-resolves the exact CC-5zxz->f9809a3 scenario in a test or documented manual check.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: Superseded/absorbed by www-ljoq (P0 decision): same root cause (per-push path-filter base lets a stale image ship). www-ljoq has the full repro + 3 fix options (A last-deployed-SHA base / B source-hash image identity / C build-all).

title: Bento placeholder regeneration algorithm - interactive test bed
description: Standalone HTML test bed (experiments/grid-test-bed.html) for designing the algorithm that regenerates the decorative placeholder/bento fill around the real tile cluster. Explores guillotine subdivision vs anchored backtracking packing, best-of-N quality search with a tunable weighted score (same-size adjacency, fault-line length, area uniformity, slivers), A/B weight calibration, and presets/board-size controls. Goal: replace the hardcoded INNER_FILL_LOCAL blob in apps/web/src/lib/placeholder-tiles.ts with a generator producing organic, varied, no-long-straight-line layouts. Open: long straight 'fault lines' are intrinsic to guillotine; next step is a structural line-breaking generator (generic rectangulation / relaxation). Determinism vs infinite-board tradeoff explored.
design: Constraints: rectangles W in [2,5] x H in [2,4], full coverage of board minus reserved cluster, no overlaps. Soft objective (what 'looks good'): minimize long uninterrupted straight seams (length-weighted), maximize size variety. Guillotine = recursive random splits (global, fast, always valid, but full-span cuts => long lines). Backtracking = anchored top-left + gap-guard (any shape, slower). Best-of-N selects lowest-scoring candidate; score weights calibrated via A/B preference rounds. Long-lines vs infinite-locality is a fundamental tension.
acceptance_criteria: experiments/grid-test-bed.html committed and opens in a browser; lets you pick algorithm, board size, block-size bounds, reserved-shape presets, run best-of-N quality search, and A/B calibrate score weights

title: deploy.config health probe hits dead /api/climate.now (renamed to climate.get) -> agent verify always advisory-red
description: The api cmdProbe in deploy.config.ts:64 runs `curl -sf http://api:4201/api/climate.now | jq -e .tempC`. Two faults: (1) the api server.ts only serves /up and /trpc/* - there is no /api/* REST route, and (2) the climate procedure is climate.get, not climate.now. So the probe always 404s, making 'bosun verify' log 'probes not all green (advisory)' on every deploy. Discovered during CC-8pt namespace migration; unrelated to it. Fix: point the probe at the real tRPC path, e.g. curl 'http://api:4201/trpc/climate.get?batch=1&input=%7B%220%22%3A%7B%22json%22%3Anull%7D%7D' and jq the tRPC envelope ([0].result.data.ambient), OR add a lightweight /health/climate REST shim. Note the web-proxied public path also differs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] api health cmdProbe targets a route that actually exists (200, real data)
- [ ] jq assertion matches the tRPC response envelope (or REST shim output)
- [ ] bosun verify shows all probes green on a clean deploy (no advisory-red for climate)
notes:
DONE + verified (2026-06-05). Root cause: bosun runs cmdProbes in the AGENT (Bun.spawn), not in the target container, so probes that assumed in-container localhost silently failed and kept 'bosun verify' advisory-red.
Two probes fixed (same root cause):
1. api 'live HA data': curled /api/climate.now (never existed; api serves only /up + /trpc/*, and the proc is climate.get). Added a dedicated REST route GET /health/climate (apps/api/src/server.ts) returning live HA ambient temp, throws->500 on outage. Probe in deploy.config.ts now hits it. commit ff6ba5f.
2. postgres 'postgres ready': 'pg_isready -U postgres' hit the agent's localhost (no pg there) -> exit 2. Added -h postgres so it resolves the service over the overlay net (like the api probe). In-container Swarm healthcheck unchanged. spec.test.ts asserts the -h flag. commit a92b98e.
Also widened .gitleaks.toml allowlist for internal <port>/health/ probe URLs (false positive).
PROOF: clean 'bosun verify' on the box now reports 7/7 passed (was 5/7), including ✓ live HA data and ✓ postgres ready. All CI green incl test gate + deploy.

title: weather-ingest test rots after the fixture's date: hardcoded hours + real new Date() => no forecast rows
description: apps/api weather-ingest-service.test.ts 'appends forecast/observed hourly rows' fails once wall-clock passes the static fixture window. runWeatherIngestCycle() calls new Date() internally and classifies fixture hours (2026-06-04T17/18/19:00) as observed(past)/forecast(future). After 2026-06-04 all become observed, so the forecast row is undefined and the assert at line 100 fails. Time-bomb, unrelated to any feature change (surfaced during CC-czg/CC-ykj work post-UTC-midnight). Fix (test-only): pin the clock with vi.useFakeTimers({toFake:['Date']}) + vi.setSystemTime(2026-06-04T17:30 local) so the fixture straddles now. Deeper option: inject a now/clock into runWeatherIngestCycle for full determinism.
acceptance_criteria:
- the test pins the clock so forecast AND observed rows are produced deterministically regardless of real date
- bun run test green (apps/api weather-ingest suite passes on any future run)

title: Cron scheduler matches UTC in the agent container; 03:00 prune fires at 03:00 UTC (~8pm LA)
description: The bosun scheduler matches cron against the agent container's wall clock, which is UTC (confirmed during CC-lnq: a slot stamped 2026-5-5T2:11 while LA local was ~19:11). So docker-image-prune 'schedule: 0 3 * * *' (intended 03:00 LA off-peak) actually fires at 03:00 UTC = ~8pm LA, on-peak. Fix: bake TZ=America/Los_Angeles into the bosun-agent image/service env (and document that cron is interpreted in that TZ), or make the scheduler TZ explicit. Also decide DST handling.
acceptance_criteria:
- DONE: agent container TZ is America/Los_Angeles (ENV TZ in packages/bosun/Dockerfile + tzdata baked in). Verified in image: local clock shows PDT, Bun Date.getHours()=20 vs getUTCHours()=3. Scheduler matches local wall-clock accessors, so slotKey/cron match LA local.
- docker-image-prune '0 3 * * *' now fires at 03:00 LA (not 03:00 UTC). Unit test (scheduler.test.ts, CC-dd0 block) locks this: 10:00Z matches '0 3 * * *' under LA TZ, NOT under UTC. (Live 03:00 log proof deferred to homelab; mechanism verified in-image + unit-tested.)
- README documents the scheduler timezone, incl. DST handled automatically by the IANA zone.

title: Deploy webhook doesn't roll bosun-agent to the new :main image (needs manual force-update)
description: Observed twice (CC-u34, CC-lnq): after CI builds a new control-center-bosun:main, the deploy webhook's 'bosun up' runs 'docker stack deploy --prune' but the bosun-agent service does NOT roll to the freshly-pushed :main digest - the running task keeps the old digest. Workaround used: 'docker service update --image ghcr.io/0x63616c/control-center-bosun:main --with-registry-auth --force control-center_bosun-agent' on the manager, which re-resolves :main and rolls. Likely cause: docker stack deploy not re-resolving the :main tag to the new digest (resolve-image behavior) when the spec string is unchanged. Fix options: deployStack should pin images by digest at render time, or pass --resolve-image always explicitly + verify it re-pulls, or the agent self-updates. Until fixed, config/agent changes need a manual force-roll.
acceptance_criteria:
- a push to main that rebuilds bosun reliably rolls control-center_bosun-agent to the new digest with no manual force-update
- verified by a deploy where the agent task digest changes automatically post-webhook

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: Fix implemented + shipped (7662800). Digest-pin path: CI deploy job reads each :main digest (docker buildx imagetools inspect) and POSTs {images:{name:digest}}; serve (now async) forwards to cmdUp->renderStackYml->pinImage() which rewrites our ghcr images to @sha256:<digest>. Only rebuilt services (changed digest) roll; un-overridden keep :main; missing body falls back (manual curl still works). 789 tests/typecheck/biome green, README updated. CI 26991928900 building. Bootstrap note: the FIRST czg deploy is processed by the OLD agent (ignores body) so it won't self-roll - will force-roll once to make czg agent live, then push a deploy.config-only change to PROVE the new agent auto-rolls on changed digest with no manual force.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text: FIXED + PROVEN in prod. After shipping digest-pinning, a deploy.config-only change (commit ddea6e1) rebuilt only bosun (new digest 5f5994). The webhook (with CI's {images} digest map) drove the agent to render bosun-agent@sha256:5f5994 (pure digest, was :main@8cd016) and docker stack deploy rolled it AUTOMATICALLY - running task now @sha256:5f5994, prior 8cd016 task Shutdown, ZERO manual force-update. Spec image proof: ghcr.io/0x63616c/control-center-bosun@sha256:5f59946e... Note: the first attempt of that deploy failed on a SEPARATE bug - a cold-start race where cmdUp's concurrent Promise.all op reads corrupt the op daemon config on a fresh container ('invalid JSON: unexpected end of JSON input'); re-firing with a warm daemon succeeded. Filed separately.

title: bosun verify probes fail in agent container: missing curl/jq/pg_isready (exit 127)
description: During the CC-u34 deploy, the bosun-agent's post-deploy 'bosun up' verify showed 2/6 probes passing. Root cause: cmdProbes shell out to curl/jq/pg_isready which are NOT in the bosun-agent image (ghcr control-center-bosun), so they exit 127 and can never pass where the agent runs them ('live HA data', 'postgres ready'). The api:4201/up http probe also reported 'Unable to connect' and storybook returned 502 from inside the agent (possibly transient/cold, needs confirming). Net: verify is structurally red in the deploy agent. Pre-existing, surfaced by CC-u34, not caused by it. Fix options: bake curl/jq/postgresql-client into the agent image, or move verify to a context that has them, or replace shell probes with in-process equivalents.
acceptance_criteria:
- DONE: agent image bakes curl, jq, postgresql-client (pg_isready) via apk add; verified inside built image (curl 8.14.1, jq 1.8.1, pg_isready 17.10). The two cmdProbes ('live HA data', 'postgres ready') can now exit 0.
- api:4201/up and storybook probes are http probes resolved over the swarm network; the missing-tools 127 was the structural blocker and is fixed. Cold/transient HTTP is now covered by CC-1dx (serve-path verify is advisory).
- a deploy 'bosun up' can report 6/6 once warm; transient cold probes no longer surface as a deploy failure (CC-1dx).

title: bosun routes sync creates tunnel ingress but NOT the DNS record
description: reconcile/routes.ts createRoute adds the cfd_tunnel ingress rule, but a public hostname also needs a proxied CNAME <host> -> <tunnelid>.cfargotunnel.com in the zone. hooks.worldwidewebb.co had the ingress but no DNS record, so it 521'd until I created the CNAME by hand (CC-a8p). dashboard/storybook records were also hand-made. Fix: routes sync should upsert the DNS CNAME (CF API zones/<id>/dns_records) alongside the ingress, and prune it with the route.
acceptance_criteria: bun run bosun routes sync creates the proxied CNAME for a newly-declared route so the hostname resolves with no manual DNS step; idempotent on re-run
close_reason: Obsolete bosun-specific audit/deploy work after Pulumi+k3s migration removed bosun.

title: bosun postgres() ignores config[] and init[] options
description: postgres() helper accepts config (postgresql.conf paths) and init (initdb script paths) but does not render them as docker configs / initdb mounts. Wire these via docker configs + /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d. Not blocking (data dir already initialized; defaults work), tracked so it isn't lost.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] config[] mounted at /etc/postgresql/ and used via -c config_file.
- [ ] init[] mounted at /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/.
- [ ] tests cover rendering.
close_reason: Obsolete bosun-specific audit/deploy work after Pulumi+k3s migration removed bosun.

title: Make bootstrap.sh fully self-contained: auto-init Portainer admin + auto-rename env
description: bootstrap.sh currently starts swarm+portainer+bosun but leaves Portainer admin creation as a manual post-step, so the CC-4b5 env rename can't run automatically on a fresh box (it needs an authenticated admin). Make bootstrap one-shot: publish a local portainer port, programmatically create the admin from 1Password within Portainer's ~5min init window, then rename the env to 'production' - all in one run, idempotent.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] portainer service publishes a host-local port (127.0.0.1 reachable) so bootstrap can hit /api before cloudflared is up
- [ ] bootstrap guards that op://Homelab/Portainer Admin/password exists, exiting with save-script instructions if not (mirrors the GHCR token guard)
- [ ] bootstrap POSTs /api/users/admin/init from the 1Password password; already-initialised is a graceful no-op
- [ ] bootstrap then runs rename-portainer-env.sh against the local URL; env ends up named 'production'
- [ ] all steps idempotent: a second bootstrap run is a clean no-op
- [ ] re-running on the existing live box does not error
close_reason: bootstrap.sh now single-shot self-contained: fail-fast secret guard, swarm init, portainer service publishing host-local :9000, API readiness wait (local->public fallback), programmatic admin init from 1Password (409/already-init is graceful), env rename to production, GHCR check, bosun up. Validated against live box: URL fallback resolves to public, admin/init returns 409 handled, auth+rename idempotent. shellcheck clean on both scripts. Docs updated.

- type ∈ feat|fix|chore|refactor|docs|test|ci|build|perf|style|revert

- nested areas allowed: fix(web/tiles/CC-x):

title: bosun postgres() helper drops POSTGRES_DB / POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE env
description: POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE is now wired (CC-11c). Residual: postgres() still does NOT set POSTGRES_DB, so a FRESH pgdata volume inits only the default 'postgres' DB, not 'control_center' (api's expected DB). Today control_center exists in the persistent cc_pgdata volume (created by hand during CC-a8p recovery), so it only bites on volume loss/rebuild. Fix: postgres() should set POSTGRES_DB (api's db name) so a fresh init creates it.
acceptance_criteria:
- DONE: postgres() now returns env { POSTGRES_DB: 'control_center', POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE: '/run/secrets/POSTGRES_PASSWORD' (when secretRef given) }. POSTGRES_DB is set unconditionally (needed on any fresh init, dev included); db name is configurable via opts.db, defaulting to the api's expected name (verified against apps/api/src/env.ts:24 'control_center').
- FAITHFUL to live prod: POSTGRES_DB is honoured by the postgres image ONLY at first initdb (empty PGDATA). The existing cc_pgdata volume already has control_center, so adding this env is inert against the running service - no destructive re-init. A volume-loss rebuild now correctly recreates control_center instead of only the default 'postgres' DB.
- Regression tests added in spec.test.ts (default control_center, set regardless of secretRef, override via opts.db). Gates: bun run test 799/799, typecheck 0/4 workspaces, biome clean.

title: Health/fitness section: weight + steps tiles (+ possible multi-page nav)
description:
Add a body-weight tracking feature to the wall-panel dashboard. Brainstorm pending - not yet scoped.

Context / seed data:
- Calum manually weighed in at 153.8 lbs on 2026-06-03 (first data point).
- Data source options to explore: (1) manual entry, (2) smart-scale auto-sync (Calum owns a smart scale), (3) iPhone step/activity tracking via Apple Health.

Design tension to resolve:
- The board is a FULL 12x6 single-page grid (no empty cells). A weight tile means either reorganizing the grid OR introducing multi-page navigation. Note existing ticket CC-awm (top bar with 'Home' label + notification center) which may be the natural home for page navigation.
- Decide: weight as a single tile vs a new 'health/fitness' page with weight + steps + trend chart.

Next step: run superpowers:brainstorming to scope data source, layout/navigation approach, and chart/trend treatment before writing a spec.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design source decided (manual / smart-scale / Apple Health) and documented in spec
- [ ] Layout approach decided: single tile vs new page + nav primitive
- [ ] Weight tile renders on board at 1366x1024, verified via agent-browser screenshot
- [ ] Seed data point (153.8 lbs, 2026-06-03) visible in the tile
- [ ] No fake/placeholder data: unavailable weight shows Skeleton + retries (check-fake-data guard stays green)
- [ ] bun run test + typecheck + biome check green

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
Scope expanded per Calum: not just weight - a health/fitness grouping including:
- Weight tile (manual / smart-scale / Apple Health source TBD)
- Steps / activity tile (iPhone + Apple Health)
- Room to grow (sleep, heart rate, workouts, etc.) - argues for a dedicated 'Health' page rather than cramming single tiles onto the full board.

This strengthens the multi-page-nav direction: a Health page reachable from the CC-awm top bar, holding weight + steps + future health tiles, each using shared ui/ primitives + trend charts.

title: ac_code_auto: code push auto-builds + auto-deploys
description: Auto-deploy via CI->webhook (Part 12).
acceptance_criteria: Push trivial code change; gh run watch success; new GHCR sha exists; CI calls deploy webhook and web image digest updates with no manual step. Per ac_code_auto. If on-box agent/SA token unavailable, mark skipped with reason.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
Auto-deploy webhook currently BLOCKED, deploy is manual (bosun up / docker service update) for now:
- hooks.worldwidewebb.co/deploy/control-center returns Cloudflare 521 (origin down) - no 'bosun serve' receiver runs on homelab (not a swarm service, no launchd).
- Repo secret BOSUN_WEBHOOK_TOKEN is unset (gh secret list empty); CI deploy step sends 'Authorization: Bearer ' (empty).
- Path mismatch: CI POSTs /deploy/control-center but 'bosun serve' only handles /deploy (cli.ts) - the webhook path was never proven end-to-end.
- The cloudflared tunnel ingress for hooks.* is remote-managed (CF dashboard / evee repo), so wiring it is cross-repo.
To finish ac_code_auto: run 'bosun serve' as a persistent agent on the box, set BOSUN_WEBHOOK_TOKEN (repo secret == agent env), fix the /deploy path, and point the CF hooks. route at the agent. Until then this stays skipped per its own acceptance ('if on-box agent/SA token unavailable, mark skipped').

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
VERIFIED end-to-end; the prior 'blocked' comment is OBSOLETE.

Re-checked each blocker from 2026-06-04: BOSUN_WEBHOOK_TOKEN repo secret IS set (gh secret list); hooks.worldwidewebb.co returns 401 not CF 521 (receiver up); serve.ts handles POST /deploy/control-center exactly matching the CI caller. The bosun-agent runs as a 1/1 swarm service.

Proof (web-only push, run 27023166752): trivial code change pushed -> gh run success -> new GHCR sha built -> deploy job step 'Trigger bosun deploy webhook: success' (Collect image digests + webhook POST) -> web service image digest auto-updated 53759906643c -> 6e1f3ea87239 on the swarm with NO manual step. Per ac_code_auto.

VERIFIED via two targeted pushes + fixed a real path-filter bug.

1. web-only push (apps/web/nginx.conf, run 27023166752): changes -> web=true, api/storybook/bosun=false. build-web ran; build-api/storybook/bosun SKIPPED; deploy ran (web digest auto-updated). Pass: only web rebuilt.
2. docs-only push (run 27027131046, under fixed filter): web/api/storybook/bosun/any_app ALL false; every build job + deploy SKIPPED. Pass: docs-only builds nothing.

BUG FOUND + FIXED (627c192): the web filter used '!apps/web/.storybook/**' negation list items. dorny/paths-filter OR-matches each entry, and a '!foo/**' pattern matches every file NOT under foo -> web=true on docs-only/api-only/every push (web rebuilt unconditionally). Verified before fix: docs-only run 27026896653 had Filter web=true and build-web actually built+pushed an image despite only docs/ changing. After dropping the negations, the same class of change yields web=false. Per ac_ci_selective.

title: pan-lab arm: native-scroll (overflow:auto + mouse-drag shim)

title: pan-lab arm: canvas/WebGL renderer (Pixi or react-konva)
description: Implement /arm/canvas: synthetic tiles drawn to a single canvas/stage with a camera transform; heavy real tiles (map/video) layered as DOM over the canvas at their world positions. Establishes the upper bound on node count + the definitive 'do we need canvas' answer.
acceptance_criteria: bun run build passes; canvas renders synthetic tiles + pans; real heavy tiles overlaid correctly; autopan exports FPS; agent-browser screenshot saved pan-lab/docs/screenshots/arm-canvas.png
notes: Implemented canvas arm at /Users/calum/code/github.com/0x63616c/pan-lab/src/arms/canvas.tsx. Strategy: Pixi.js v8 (@pixi/react v8) draws synthetic tiles (text/chart) imperatively via a single pixiGraphics object in useTick; camera is a pixiContainer shifted by -camX/-camY each rAF. Heavy DOM tiles (map/video) overlaid as absolute-positioned React elements with overscan culling. Pointer drag + autopan supported. Build passes; autopan publishes window.__perfResult. Screenshot at docs/screenshots/arm-canvas.png: tiles render, PerfHUD visible, clock tile centred.

title: pan-lab: full-matrix autopan sweep -> results.json + knee
description: Playwright (desktop chromium, optionally webkit) driving each arm at every matrix config: grid 12x6..200x200, tiles 9..500, heavy 0..50%, optimize on/off. Loads /arm/:strategy?grid&tiles&heavy&autopan=1, waits for window.__perfResult, records mean+1%low+p95/p99 FPS, dropped frames, heap, mounted count, layer/WebGL-context count. Writes pan-lab/results.json. Computes the knee per arm (first config <55fps).
acceptance_criteria: sweep script runs headless and writes results.json with a row per (arm,config) and a computed knee per arm; a desktop baseline table printed; soak note recorded
notes: Playwright sweep complete: 1200 runs (5 strategies x 5 grids x 6 tile counts x 4 heavy x 2 optimize), 3s autopan, concurrency=8. Results in pan-lab/results.json. Knee table: transform-window is the clear winner (205/240 configs >=55fps), canvas is the worst (55/240). Desktop headless numbers only.

title: pan-lab arm: transform + content-visibility + off-screen pause
description: Fork of transform arm adding contain:strict + content-visibility:auto per tile and IntersectionObserver off-screen pause (freeze video/map, stop animation) when tile leaves viewport+margin. Toggleable via ?optimize=cv,pause.

notes: Implemented src/arms/transform-cv.tsx: translate3d camera via imperative ref (no setState per frame), contain:strict + content-visibility:auto per tile slot when ?optimize=cv active, IntersectionObserver pausing off-screen video (pause/play) and map (canvas visibility:hidden) when ?optimize=pause active. Fixed pre-existing canvas.tsx TS errors (stale @ts-expect-error directives + unused var + style prop on Application) to unblock bun run build. Build passes, PerfHUD shows 105fps, clock tile centered on open, 20 tiles mounted. Screenshot at pan-lab/docs/screenshots/arm-transform-cv.png. autopan calls startAutopan and sets window.__perfResult on completion.

title: pan-lab arm: transform + windowing (mount only intersecting tiles)

TileCell memo'd. Opens centered on clock tile. At 12x10 grid with 80 tiles, mounts only 26 (67% DOM reduction). Autopan writes window.__perfResult via startAutopan. bun run build passes. Screenshot at pan-lab/docs/screenshots/arm-transform-window.png. Also patched pre-existing TS error in transform-cv.tsx (unused viewW/viewH params) that blocked the build gate.
