title: Pannable tile-canvas perf lab
description: Throwaway pan-lab prototype to settle the rendering strategy for converting the dashboard board into a pannable 2D canvas (world > screen, drag to float, opens centered on Clock). Build ONE Vite+React app with a route per rendering arm + a launcher/results UI Calum can test on the iPad. Benchmark all 5 arms across a full stress sweep. Hybrid tiles: synthetic cheap/medium + real MapLibre + real video. Headline deliverable: the knee (grid x tiles x strategy where iPad FPS < 55) + a recommendation. Full design: control-center plan idempotent-snacking-clock.md.
design:
PERF-LAB VALIDATION CONTRACT (headline questions the sweep must answer):
A1 With the realistic tile mix, at what grid size + tile count does DOM+transform drop below 55fps on the iPad? (the 'knee')
A2 Does content-visibility + off-screen-pause alone hold 60fps, or is true windowing required?
A3 Do we ever need canvas/WebGL, or is DOM enough for the realistic ceiling (<=100 tiles, <=48x48)?
A4 How many simultaneous live maps/videos before it falls over (WebGL context cap / memory)?
DONE = one Vite+React app, 5 working arms (route each), a full-matrix autopan sweep exporting results.json with a computed knee per arm, and a launcher+results UI Calum opens on the iPad to drive config sliders and rate feel. Each arm screenshot-verified.

title: Re-explore actual iPad wall-panel screen size (reports 1366×1000 in fullscreen)
description: The iPad reports inner 1366×1000 as a FULLSCREEN web app (surprising - standalone/PWA should give the nominal 1366×1024; ~24px height is unaccounted for). For now BOARD set to 1366×1000 (what the device reports) as a best-fit placeholder so the panel renders 1:1 without letterbox scaling. Revisit: confirm the real usable area on-device, find where the 24px goes (safe-area-inset? status bar? home indicator?), and set the canonical resolution. Context: bezel is a desktop-only preview frame; on-device it's correctly OFF.
acceptance_criteria: iPad's true usable viewport confirmed via on-device ViewportDebug or equivalent; documented why a fullscreen/standalone web app reports innerHeight 1000 not 1024 (24px unaccounted); BOARD_W/BOARD_H in apps/web/src/lib/grid-constants.ts set to the verified device size; board renders true 1:1 (scale=1) on the device with no letterbox; white bezel confirmed desktop-preview-only.

title: ControlsTile findFanClimate uses unsorted .find() - same fragility class as old alphabetical climate-entity bug
description: Noted while fixing CC-59u. findFanClimate selects the fan-capable climate entity via an unsorted Array.find(), so which entity wins depends on HA's return order - the same fragility that caused the old alphabetical climate-entity bug (fixed by CLIMATE_ENTITY_ID targeting climate.home). Currently masked only because the Tesla entity (climate.evee_climate) has fan_modes:null so it's skipped. If a second fan-capable climate appears, selection becomes order-dependent. Fix: target the configured house thermostat (CLIMATE_ENTITY_ID) explicitly, or sort deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: findFanClimate selects the fan entity deterministically (explicit CLIMATE_ENTITY_ID or sorted), not via unsorted .find(); a test asserts selection is stable when HA returns multiple fan-capable climates in arbitrary order

title: Tesla tile: charge bar + range color keyed to charging state
description: In TeslaTileView, the charge progress bar and the Range stat should reflect charging state. When charging: green/accent bar and green Range value. When not charging: gray bar and gray Range value. Currently the bar always uses the accent gradient and Range always has accent=true.
acceptance_criteria: charging=true -> bar uses accent green gradient + Range stat is accent/green; charging=false -> bar is gray + Range stat is gray (non-accent). Stories cover both states; tests + typecheck + biome green.
close_reason: Charge bar + Range stat now key off charging state: green when charging, gray when idle. Stat gained reusable muted prop. Gates green (typecheck, 187 tests, biome).

title: Tests + Storybook: climate edge cases & per-mode stories
description: Cross-cutting verification + Storybook coverage so the board QA shows every mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- ClimateTileView.stories.tsx: stories for Off, Cool (single), Heat (single), Heat·Cool (dual). stories.test snapshot/smoke updated.
- Edge-case suite: low & high both at 65/80 extremes clamp correctly; rapid cross attempts never overlap; gap exactly 2° allowed, 1° rejected.
- bun run test green; bun run typecheck green; bunx biome check . clean.
- No FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER/DEMO_ introduced (pre-commit guard passes).

title: Generic BorderProgressRing primitive: fix seconds-ring offset from first principles
description: The seconds ring baked in CLOCK_TILE_W/H/padding/TILE_RX, a hand-built path with INSET fudge, a fixed viewBox that could mismatch the real rendered size (the slight offset), and a negative-margin hack. Replaced with apps/web/src/components/ui/BorderProgressRing.tsx: a reusable primitive that drops into ANY position:relative box, measures it via ResizeObserver, renders a 1:1 viewBox (no scaling distortion), auto-derives corner radius from the host's computed border-radius, and takes progress/color/strokeWidth/transitionMs(speed)/direction. Pure perimeterLength/perimeterPath helpers are unit-tested. Clock now renders <BorderProgressRing progress={seconds/60} />. SecondsRing.tsx deleted.
close_reason: Generic BorderProgressRing + rAF-driven ClockSecondsRing: smooth sweep, instant wrap, any tile/size.

title: ClockTile seconds ring: instant 59→0 wrap, smoother sweep, subtle gray
description: SecondsRing traced the tile-border perimeter with stroke var(--acc) (green) and a 0.8s linear CSS transition driven by an integer seconds prop. Problems: (1) on the 59->0 wrap the dashoffset jumps to full perimeter and the transition animates the ring backwards; (2) 0.8s transition vs 1s tick caused a per-second stutter; (3) accent green was too prominent. Fix: stroke var(--ink-3) gray; transition 1s linear to match the tick (smooth); detect wrap (seconds decreased) via a ref and drop the transition on that frame so it snaps instantly.
close_reason: Fixed in 55af050: stroke var(--ink-3) gray, 1s-linear transition for a smooth sweep, ref-based wrap detection drops the transition on 59->0 so it snaps instantly.

title: [Storybook] Shared argTypes for common tile controls (status, etc.)
description: Several tile stories define their own argTypes for the 'status' discriminator (radio: loading/populated/error). Extract a TILE_STATUS_ARG_TYPE constant from the factory or a shared argtypes.ts file so these don't drift. Also a candidate: shared argType for boolean 'online', 'live', etc. that multiple tiles share.

title: [Storybook] Remove Next12HoursViewStory wrapper - refactor view to accept optional args
description: Next12HoursView.stories.tsx wraps the actual component in a Next12HoursViewStory function to flatten the discriminated union args for Storybook controls. If Next12HoursView is refactored to accept a flat props shape (or its union relaxed), the wrapper can be removed and meta can point directly at the real component. This would also make the composeStories pattern in the test file more faithful to the real component.

title: [Storybook] Migrate EventsTileView.stories.test.tsx to composeStories pattern
description: EventsTileView.stories.test.tsx currently uses a different pattern: it imports story exports directly and calls render(<EventsTileView {...story.args} />) rather than using composeStories() like all other tile story tests. Migrate it to use composeStories() + play() execution for consistency with ClimateTileView, Network, Tesla etc. story tests.

title: Title bar + notification center: thin top bar ('Home' left, bell right) with popover
design: This is the full chrome around the notifications system; expect tweaking to get right (Calum: 'might take some tweaking'). Bar lives inside #scaler/.board in apps/web/src/components/Board.tsx so it scales with the panel. Consume a shared notifications source (the useNotifications hook/store introduced by CC-dck) - the bar is the view, the store is the source of truth, so any future notification (alerts, scene changes, device errors) can publish to it. Popover anchored under the bell, dark-theme, dismiss on outside tap. Mind the board's fixed grid: adding a bar means the 12x6 bento needs to fit beneath it (adjust the board flex/grid container, not individual tiles). Keep it minimal/calm to match the wall-panel aesthetic.
acceptance_criteria: A thin, unobtrusive title bar sits at the top of the fixed 1366x1024 board: 'Home' (or Control Center) label on the left, a notification bell on the right; tapping the bell opens a small popover/modal anchored from the bell listing active notifications; the bell shows an unread/active count or dot; the connection-lost notification (CC-dck) renders through this center instead of its own overlay once this lands; bar height + spacing respect the consistent-spacing scale and dark tokens (incl. scrollbar styling inside the popover if it scrolls); board tile grid reflows to sit below the bar without breaking the bento layout or any tile; story + vitest tests for open/close + count; gates green.
notes: Calum: P2 for the full bar (P1 is the connection-lost notification, CC-dck). Bar should host CC-dck's notification once built.

title: Self-healing dev resources: watchdog wrapper restarts web/api on sustained health failure
description:
Tilt readiness_probe only sets display status; it does NOT restart a local_resource serve_cmd. When Vite (web) or the API comes up but isn't actually serving (hung/port race/dep not ready), the probe goes red and the process stays alive, so Tilt never restarts it - Calum has to click Restart in the UI manually. On an unattended wall-panel that's the real defect.

Fix: wrap each serve_cmd in a watchdog that polls its own health URL after a startup grace period; if unhealthy for a sustained window, kill the dev server and exit non-zero so Tilt's normal crash-restart kicks in. Pair with backoff so a genuinely-broken build doesn't thrash.
acceptance_criteria: web and api serve_cmds run under a watchdog script; sustained health failure (after grace) kills the process and exits non-zero; Tilt auto-restarts without manual UI click; existing readiness_probe display preserved; startup grace prevents restart loops on cold start

title: Controls cell: label + On/Off on one bottom line; extract standalone dumb ControlTap component
description:
Two parts (per Calum + screenshot):
1) LAYOUT: in each control cell, keep icon top-left + status dot top-right. Bottom row becomes a SINGLE line: the label (Lamps/Lights/Fan) left-aligned and the On/Off status right-aligned on the same baseline (flex, justify-between, align-baseline) - instead of the current label-with-status-stacked-below. Match the design aesthetic (mono uppercase status, accent green when on).
2) COMPONENTIZE: the cell is currently the internal ETap sub-component inside ControlsTileView. Extract it into a proper STANDALONE, dumb, reusable presentational component (ControlTap) - props: icon, label, on, sub?, pending?, onToggle - zero data/trpc deps - with its own unit tests (renders on/off/pending states, fires onToggle). ControlsTileView consumes it. Keep the no-revert/optimistic logic in the container untouched.
Apply AFTER the polish workflow releases ControlsTileView (CC-lqz).
notes: ALSO (Calum): remove the '…' pending indicator entirely. The control cell status text is ONLY 'On' or 'Off' - never '…'. The optimistic flip already shows the new state immediately, so drop the pending-ellipsis branch in the cell (pending may still drive subtle opacity if desired, but the TEXT is strictly On/Off).

title: DogCamTile: broken-image glyph showing on the feed (until live stream exists)
description: Calum screenshot: tapping/viewing the Dog Cam shows the browser's broken-image icon next to 'Living Room Cam / LIVE'. Cause: an with no valid src (the real stream is deferred - CC-2x4). Remove the broken (or only render it when a real stream URL exists); fall back to the design's camera-glyph placeholder in the .feed shell so there is no broken-image icon. No fake stream. Apply on the post-split DogCamTileView (CC-vbx). Verify with screenshot.

title: ControlsTile: bottom row nearly touches card edge - equalize padding all around
description: Confirmed visually (Calum screenshot): the 2x2 control cells' bottom row sits almost flush against the card's bottom edge, while the top/left/right have ~22px. Bottom spacing must match the side padding (22) so the card has consistent spacing on all sides, per the design. Likely cause: the inner grid has flex:1 + gridTemplateRows '1fr 1fr' and overflows/compresses against the Tile's bottom padding, OR the tile content is taller than its board cell. Fix on the post-split ControlsTileView: ensure the 2x2 grid sits inside consistent 22px padding (bottom == sides) with the 13px inter-cell gap, no overflow. Verify with a 1366x1024 screenshot. Apply AFTER CC-y12 (presentational split) lands.
notes: Design spec confirmed (Evee Dashboard.html ctrl tile): padding 20 (ours is 22); inner grid flex:1, gridTemplateColumns '1fr 1fr', gridTemplateRows '1fr 1fr', gap 13. Our Board grid already matches the design exactly (repeat(12,1fr)/repeat(6,1fr), gap 18, board padding 26), so heights are correct. ROOT CAUSE: the flex:1 grid child has implicit min-height:auto and overflows the tile's bottom padding. FIX: add minHeight:0 to the 2x2 grid container and set tile padding to 20 (match design). Result = symmetric 20px all around.

No trpc/useQuery/useMutation/hooks for data inside the view. Keep TeslaTile as a thin container that calls trpc, derives the props, and renders TeslaTileView. Write unit tests for TeslaTileView covering: loading/skeleton state, error/empty state, populated state, and every callback firing with correct args. Part of [CC-o36].

title: Presentational/container split for all tiles
description: Refactor every tile into a dumb presentational component (props = data + state + callbacks, ZERO data-layer/trpc dependencies, pure presentation, renders all states: loading/skeleton, error, populated) plus a thin container/HOC that fetches data via trpc and maps it to the view's props. Goal: the presentational layer is unit-testable in isolation - assert every visual state and that callbacks fire - with no mocking of trpc needed. Pattern per tile X: XTileView (pure) + XTile (container).

title: ControlsTile: optimistic instant-flip on toggle (incl. unregistered devices)
description: CC-nra removed the optimistic cache flip, so toggles only updated on the next poll (~2s). Restored onMutate optimistic update in ControlsTile: flips the toggled group on + pending:true immediately (which also bumps adaptive refetch to 2s for fast confirm), onError reverts to snapshot, onSettled invalidates. Gives instant feedback even for unregistered devices that have no server-side pending overlay. Test added asserting the setData updater flips on+pending.
close_reason: Implemented optimistic onMutate flip + revert/invalidate; tested

description:

title: ClockGreeting: AM/PM letters look squished (inherited negative tracking)
description:
In ClockGreeting.tsx:54-65 the time wrapper is .mono with letterSpacing:-.05em. The AM/PM span (line 64) only sets fontSize:26 + marginLeft:8 and INHERITS the -.05em, so at 26px the two caps are pulled together and read as squished. marginLeft separates AM/PM from the time but nothing fixes spacing between the A and M. Fix: set letterSpacing:'0.02em' (or 'normal') on the AM/PM span. NOTE the design (evee-tiles.jsx EClockGreeting) has the same inherited-tracking issue, so this is a genuine fix not a port error.

## Acceptance Criteria

AM/PM renders with normal/positive tracking, legibly spaced, distinct from the time digits. No regression to the 96px time tracking.

## Acceptance Criteria

AM/PM renders with normal/positive tracking, legibly spaced, distinct from the time digits. No regression to the 96px time tracking.
acceptance_criteria: AM/PM renders with normal/positive tracking, legibly spaced, distinct from the time digits. No regression to the 96px time tracking.

title: Weather Now: swap Sunset/Sunrise metric cell based on time of day
description:
In the Weather Now card, the bottom metric cell currently always shows 'Sunset' with the sunset time. Once it's past sunset for the day, that value is stale/less useful. Calum wants the cell to flip to the *next* relevant event: show 'Sunset' + sunset time while before sunset, then after sunset passes show 'Sunrise' + the next sunrise time. It should keep swapping between the two across the day so it always shows the upcoming event.

Example: sunset 7:58 PM. Before 7:58 PM show 'Sunset 7:58 PM'. At/after 7:58 PM show 'Sunrise 4:14 AM' (next morning's sunrise). After that sunrise passes, swap back to showing the day's sunset.

## Design

Current state:

* apps/api/src/services/weather-service.ts only requests 'sunset' from Open-Meteo daily (line \~131) and formats it via formatSunset() (line \~113). No sunrise is fetched.
* apps/api/src/trpc/routers/weather.ts schema exposes 'sunset: string' (line 13).
* apps/web/src/components/tiles/WeatherNow.tsx renders <MetricCell label='Sunset' value={w.sunset} /> (line \~180), default sunset '--:--' (line 13).

Work needed:

1. API: add 'sunrise' to the Open-Meteo daily query, parse + format it (reuse/rename formatSunset), and include sunrise in the WeatherData type + tRPC output schema. Likely want today's sunrise/sunset AND tomorrow's sunrise so the 'next event' can be computed when past today's sunset.
2. Web: compute which event is next based on current time vs sunset/sunrise, and render label+value accordingly (Sunset before sunset, Sunrise after). Decide whether the swap logic lives client-side (needs the raw times, recompute on a timer/render) or server-side (API returns the already-resolved next event).
3. Update WeatherNow.test.tsx and weather.test.ts for both states.

Open question: pure client-side computation is simplest for the live swap, but requires the API to return raw sunrise + sunset (and tomorrow's sunrise). Flag for decision before implementing.

title: Tesla odometer shows placeholder (24,113), not real value
description:
The Tesla tile odometer shows a fixed 24,113 instead of the car's real mileage.

Actual root cause (the web tile + API wiring are correct - verified TeslaTile.tsx:270-275 consumes live tRPC data and tesla-service.ts:47 resolves sensor.evee_odometer):

* tesla-service.ts:97-100 comments 'Odometer is frequently disabled in the integration - fall back gracefully' and returns TESLA_PLACEHOLDER.odo = '24,113' whenever the odometer entity is missing/unavailable.
* So the displayed 24,113 is the design placeholder, surfacing because sensor.evee_odometer is not returning a real value from Home Assistant (entity disabled in the Tesla integration, or asleep).

Note: user recalled the number as 47821; the actual hardcoded placeholder is 24,113.

Fix options:

* Enable the odometer sensor entity in the Tesla (tesla_custom / Tesla Fleet) HA integration so sensor.evee_odometer reports a value, then the live value flows through automatically.
* If the entity genuinely can't be enabled, source odometer another way, or hide the stat rather than showing a misleading static number.
close_reason: Code described in issue (discarded query) was already fixed in ffc593d. Real cause of live 24,113: sensor.evee_odometer was disabled-by-default in the Tesla HA integration, so the API correctly fell back. Enabled the entity via HA websocket entity_registry API + woke the car (button.evee_wake) -> odometer now reports 38,743 live through tesla.get. Also fixed a surfaced bug: odometer rendered '0' (not placeholder) when entity exists but state is unknown/unavailable; now degrades to placeholder + regression test added.

Investigate TYE architecture: does app--tye need a public API route?
description: The TYE API is declared as internalService (cluster-only, no public tunnel route). The frontend (app--tye.worldwidewebb.co) is public. Question: how does the frontend call the API in prod? Options: (a) frontend + API are co-located/SSR so no cross-origin call needed, (b) frontend calls API via an internal nginx proxy (same-origin trick), (c) API needs a public api--tye route (not currently planned). Calum noted the internal-only setup was intentional - verify it works end-to-end before considering any change. Do NOT add a public API route without Calum's explicit decision.

title: Investigate 1Password Connect (self-hosted) to kill op rate-limiting
description:
The 1Password service-account token rate-limits HARD on uncached reads - repeated op reads (CLI, agents, deploys) hit the cloud API quota and lock out, sometimes for a while. Investigate running 1Password Connect, a self-hosted sync server (deployable in our OrbStack k8s cluster), which syncs the vault locally and serves secrets with no per-read cloud rate limit.

Two consumers to evaluate:
1. In-cluster: External Secrets Operator already runs (1Password SDK provider). ESO supports a 'onepasswordConnect' provider - point it at an in-cluster Connect server instead of the cloud SDK to remove the per-deploy op churn (see memory bosun-agent-op-rate-limit / the old rate-limit saga).
2. Local dev + agents: 'op connect' / OP_CONNECT_HOST + OP_CONNECT_TOKEN lets the CLI read from the local Connect server, no cloud round-trip. This is what hit us today during CF Access debugging.

Compare vs alternatives Calum floated: a different secrets manager entirely. Weigh: Connect keeps 1P as source of truth + removes rate limits, but adds a stateful in-cluster service + a Connect credentials file/token to bootstrap (chicken-and-egg secret). Also note RAM budget - homelab/cluster is RAM-constrained.

NOT part of www-jtp0 migration. Standalone infra/ops investigation, do later.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Document how 1P Connect works + what it costs (RAM, the bootstrap credentials-file/token, sync model)
- [ ] Decide: ESO via Connect vs current SDK provider - does it remove the per-deploy rate-limit churn?
- [ ] Decide: local CLI/agent reads via op connect - does it remove the interactive rate-limit we hit today?
- [ ] Compare against switching secrets managers entirely (effort vs benefit), recommend one path
- [ ] If Connect chosen: file follow-up impl tickets (deploy Connect in infra/, wire ESO + local op, bootstrap token in 1P)

title: Investigate getting captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co (hostname+HTTPS) to render in the Apple captive sheet
description: Follow-up to CC-q002.26. The captive portal now works on real devices via a raw-IP HTTP landing (portal_use_hostname=false), because the Apple Captive Network Assistant renders + runs JS over http://192.168.0.147/ but will NOT render the same page at https://captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co/ (or http hostname). Extensive elimination (CC-q002.26 session) proved it is purely the hostname-vs-IP URL form: the CNA reached our .147 nginx via the hostname and got 200 + valid LE cert + uncompressed HTML, but rendered nothing (not even a plain or
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Determine WHY the Apple CNA renders the raw-IP HTTP captive page but not the same page served from the real domain (captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co), both reach the same nginx at .147 with identical content; ruled out: DNS-reaches-server, cert validity, gzip, page content
- [ ] Test the leading hypotheses: App Transport Security / ATS cipher+cert requirements over HTTPS, HSTS forcing an upgrade, a redirect chain on the hostname, and the public *.worldwidewebb.co Cloudflare record confusing the CNA resolver
- [ ] If a fix exists (e.g. ATS-compliant TLS, removing the public record so the name is local-only, killing any redirect), switch the UniFi portal back to portal_use_hostname=true + HTTPS and verify the captive sheet renders on a real iPhone AND MacBook
- [ ] If no fix is found, document the conclusion (Apple deliberately will not render real-domain captive pages) so this is not re-litigated

title: bosun-agent verify fails on-box (no curl/jq in image; climate cmdProbe) - deploy logs spurious error
description: After a webhook deploy, the agent's own 'bosun up' verify reports ~2/6 probes passing even though the deploy succeeded: the bun-alpine agent image has no curl/jq for cmdProbes (e.g. 'live HA data' curl|jq), and the climate.now endpoint 404s until data ingests. cmdUp then throws on verify and onDeploy logs '[bosun serve] deploy error', which is misleading - the stack deploy itself succeeded (api rolled correctly). Fix: add curl+jq to the agent image, and/or make the webhook-path verify non-fatal/log-only so a deployed-but-not-yet-warm probe doesn't look like a failed deploy.
acceptance_criteria:
- DONE: a successful webhook deploy does not log a deploy error. The serve path calls cmdUp(images, {advisoryVerify:true}); summarizeVerify() returns failed=false in advisory mode even when probes are red, so runVerify never process.exit()s on the serve path. A genuine deploy failure (secrets/render/stack deploy throw) still logs '[bosun serve] deploy error'.
- verify also HAS the tools it needs now (curl/jq/pg_isready baked in, CC-z65), so warm probes pass; cold/not-yet-warm probes are advisory.
- Interactive 'bosun up'/'bosun verify' still fail loudly (advisory=false). Covered by 3 new unit tests in verify.test.ts.

No key, no commit to git (too large for GitHub: 100MB/file limit + LFS bandwidth quotas).

title: ClimateTile: mode thresholds - heat when target >= 76
description: Calum spec: the displayed mode derived from the setpoint should treat heat as target >= 76 (current modeFromTarget uses >=74). Update the cool/auto/heat threshold mapping accordingly (heat >= 76; cool <= 70; auto between) and the chip presets if needed. Update ClimateTile + tests. Coordinate with the climate presentational-split ticket CC-70w.

title: EventsTile: highlight the nearest upcoming event in accent green
description: Calum: in the design render the first/nearest upcoming event's day-count was green; in ours it's white. Design code (evee-tiles.jsx:305) uses days<=3 ? acc : ink - so green is data-dependent, and our nearest event (16 days) falls outside that, rendering white. Intent: make the NEAREST (first/soonest) upcoming event's number render in accent green to draw the eye, regardless of exact day count (keep/adjust the days<=3 rule as needed). Update EventsTile + its test. Visual fidelity follow-up.

title: [deferred] Wire real UniFi data into NetworkTile (integration not yet set up)
description:
DEFERRED - the UniFi integration is not configured yet (Calum). When ready, replace NetworkTile's placeholder state with REAL live data from UniFi.
Scope when picked up:

* Set up the UniFi controller integration (apps/api/src/integrations/unifi/\* - currently env UNIFI_CONTROLLER_URL default only; credentials/site not wired).
* Real measured download/upload totals (NOT extrapolated from a single bps snapshot - see the bogus txBytes24h math in integrations/unifi/index.ts:111-113; use the real UniFi hourly stats endpoint; the UnifiHourlyBucket interface at index.ts:33-40 is defined but unused).
* Real ping/latency, real hourly traffic buckets for the chart, and live SSID from UniFi (not env.WIFI_SSID).
* Service throws on outage so the tile falls back to the shimmer/placeholder from CC-sxs (never fake data).
  Depends on the design + placeholder work in the NetworkTile design ticket.

## Acceptance Criteria

NetworkTile shows real UniFi data: measured GB totals (not bps-extrapolated), real ping, real hourly buckets, live SSID. On UniFi outage the tile shows the shimmer/placeholder (no fake data). UniFi integration credentials/site configured via env.
acceptance_criteria: NetworkTile shows real UniFi data: measured GB totals (not bps-extrapolated), real ping, real hourly buckets, live SSID. On UniFi outage the tile shows the shimmer/placeholder (no fake data). UniFi integration credentials/site configured via env.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
RESEARCH + PLAN (2026-05-31) - chosen approach for the traffic chart:

CHART: 24 bars = last 2 HOURS at 5-min granularity (1 bucket = 5 min). Chosen over 24h/hourly and 12h/30min because stock UCG/UDM default 5-min retention is ~2h, so 24x5min needs NO controller retention change.

DATA SOURCE - the official Integration API v1 (X-API-KEY, /proxy/network/integrations/v1/...) CANNOT return history, only a live bps snapshot. This is why the current getWanStats() fakes 24h totals (bps * 86400 * 0.001 at unifi/index.ts:110-113). Must use the LEGACY controller API instead:
  POST /proxy/network/api/s/{site}/stat/report/5minutes.site
  body: { attrs: ["wan-rx_bytes","wan-tx_bytes","time"], start: now-2h(ms), end: now(ms) }
  - wan-rx_bytes => download bar, wan-tx_bytes => upload bar (per-bucket BYTE totals, not bps - no diffing needed)
  - time = bucket epoch ms; align to wall-clock, zero-fill missing/quiet buckets, clamp to 24 most recent.

AUTH - legacy API does NOT accept X-API-KEY. Needs cookie login: POST /api/auth/login (UniFi OS 4+, username+password) -> carry TOKEN cookie + X-Csrf-Token on the report POST. So going live needs UniFi USERNAME+PASSWORD in 1Password Homelab (not just the scaffolded UNIFI_API_KEY). Keep self-signed-cert handling (tls rejectUnauthorized:false).

FRESHNESS - frontend already polls every 60s (NetworkTile.tsx refetchInterval 60_000); each poll re-runs getNetworkStatus -> re-pulls the 5min report. Buckets advance every 5 min (UniFi resolution). Frontend (NetworkTileView) needs NO change - it renders whatever bucket count traffic[] contains.

PING - DEMO ping:11 is a placeholder. Real measurePingMs() times a HEAD to the controller URL = LAN latency to the router, NOT WAN/internet ping. DECISION NEEDED: use real WAN latency (wanLatencyMs in the unused UnifiHealth interface, unifi/index.ts:26-31) so the tile's {ping}ms is honest.

SOURCES: Art-of-WiFi/UniFi-API-client Client.php; unificontrol readthedocs; ubntwiki stat/report; HostiFi statistics-retention article.

comments.author: Calum
comments.text:
LIVE TEST CORRECTION (2026-05-31) - verified directly against the controller (https://192.168.0.1, UCG Fiber):

AUTH: the X-API-KEY (Homelab/UniFi/local_api_key) authenticates BOTH the official integration API AND the legacy /proxy/network/api/... surface. NO username/password / cookie login needed (the earlier research note was WRONG for this controller). The username+credential fields in the Homelab UniFi item can stay empty.

TRAFFIC (confirmed working): POST https://192.168.0.1/proxy/network/api/s/default/stat/report/5minutes.site, header X-API-KEY, body {attrs:["wan-rx_bytes","wan-tx_bytes","time"], start: now-2h, end: now} -> returns exactly 24 buckets, 5.0 min each, real per-bucket bytes (e.g. 78.4MB down / 11.6MB up). Map wan-rx_bytes->down, wan-tx_bytes->up. site path segment is "default".

PING (confirmed): GET https://192.168.0.1/proxy/network/api/s/default/stat/health, header X-API-KEY -> data[subsystem=wan].uptime_stats.WAN.latency_average = real WAN internet latency in ms (measured 3ms; gw pings ping.ui.com/1.1.1.1/etc). Use THIS for the tile ping, NOT the current measurePingMs() HEAD-to-controller (that was LAN latency).

STATUS/SSID: stat/health wan subsystem gives status ("ok"), wan_ip, gw_name ("UCG Fiber"), isp_name. SSID can come from the WLAN config endpoint (still to confirm) rather than env.WIFI_SSID.

NET: this ticket is fully unblocked with creds already in 1Password. Implementation = swap getWanStats() bps-extrapolation for the 5minutes.site report (24 buckets), add a getWanHealth() for latency_average, populate traffic[] + real ping, throw on failure so tile falls back to shimmer.

value: www-cuuw access-gate design (decided in brainstorm 2026-06-10, not yet built): lock down *.worldwidewebb.co with DEFAULT-DENY at the edge via a Cloudflare Access WILDCARD app '*.worldwidewebb.co' -> action Block (the invariant: any new subdomain / accidental public route is born locked). Explicit per-host allows above it: dashboard -> kiosk SERVICE TOKEN; storybook+drizzle -> Allow email=[redacted-personal-email]; hooks -> service token (GitHub CI caller, NOT human SSO or deploy breaks). Manage Access declaratively in BOSUN (new reconcileAccess mirroring reconcile/routes.ts: list/create/prune tag-scoped; add access: field to service spec in deploy.config.ts; token client-secrets in 1Password Homelab via fromOp, client-id non-secret). Plus an in-app password page as the INNER layer behind Access. Rejected Tailscale-only+split-DNS: needs LE cert mgmt for the custom name + a tailnet resolver + (ts.net variant) an iOS kiosk rebuild since server.url is baked in capacitor.config.ts; and Tailscale gives 'absent' not enforced 'denied'. CF Access free tier (<=50 users) covers all of this. Token enforcement is at CF's EDGE so the api does NOT need to validate it to function; kiosk just sends CF-Access-Client-Id/Secret headers (then CF_Authorization cookie carries subsequent reqs). Optional hardening: validate Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion at origin.

Credentials/secrets convention (control-center): ALWAYS store any new credential, token, API key, or secret in 1Password via the op CLI in the Homelab vault. Never hardcode secrets, never write them to .env committed files, never invent placeholder creds. New secret ships with an interactive save script (scripts/save-<thing>.sh) per the using-1password skill. The local op is a PATH shim caching reads 24h - invalidate on write.

Auto-deploy (CC-a8p): push to main -> CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) builds changed images + pushes ghcr.io/0x63616c/control-center-{web,api,storybook,bosun}:main -> CI 'deploy' job POSTs https://hooks.worldwidewebb.co/deploy/control-center with BOSUN_WEBHOOK_TOKEN (GH Actions secret) -> the bosun-agent swarm service (packages/bosun/src/serve.ts, image control-center-bosun) runs 'bosun up' on the box, which docker-stack-deploys the rendered stack. The agent has the docker socket, the op SA token, and a GHCR login (entrypoint, GitHub Personal Access Token/token) so it can pull. CF hooks. needs BOTH a tunnel ingress rule (bosun routes sync) AND a proxied CNAME to <tunnelid>.cfargotunnel.com. CRITICAL: 'bosun up' redeploys the WHOLE stack from deploy.config.ts, so the render must stay faithful or it breaks prod (it did once: postgres/cloudflared).

Beads + git pull race: a freshly created bd issue can VANISH if the post-merge 'bd dolt pull' runs before the new row was pushed (origin state overwrites local unpushed rows). Happened to CC-5mek on 2026-06-09: created, claimed, then git pull --rebase ran the beads-pull hook and the row was gone. Recovery: recreate with 'bd create --id <same-id>' (explicit-id flag exists exactly for this). Prevention: after creating tickets you care about, run 'bd dolt push' (or push the repo) before any git pull.

Beads issues created locally can VANISH before they're pushed: a concurrent session's post-merge 'bd dolt pull' / dolt reset wiped CC-dhhr minutes after creation (2026-06-09), and bd show failed transiently from the worktree during the reset window ('database CC not found'). If a ticket disappears mid-flight, just recreate it (ids can't be reused) and continue; verify with bd show before relying on an id in a commit scope.

biome 'check .' is a SILENT NO-OP inside .claude worktrees: biome.json files.includes has '!**/.claude' which matches the worktree's absolute path (.claude/worktrees/...), so every file is ignored and biome exits 1 with 'No files were processed'. In a worktree, run biome on EXPLICIT changed-file paths (bunx biome check $(git diff --name-only HEAD -- '*.ts' '*.tsx')); CI covers the full check post-merge from a normal checkout.

biome worktree footgun: 'bunx biome check .' inside a .claude/worktrees/* checkout checks 0 files and exits 1 - biome.json files.includes excludes **/.claude, and the worktree path matches it. Run gates from a worktree with explicit paths instead: 'bunx biome check apps packages scripts deploy.config.ts' (exit 0 = clean).

Board is a fixed 1350x1020 wall panel (iPad Pro on [redacted-home] [redacted-street] wall; iPad's own res is 1366x1024, panel sits slightly inside it). Never add fluid/responsive layout; tiles are fixed-size within this viewport. Resolution is a SINGLE source of truth: BOARD_W/BOARD_H in apps/web/src/lib/grid-constants.ts. .board (tokens.css) fills #scaler at 100%, Storybook wall-panel viewport imports BOARD_W/BOARD_H, so changing those two constants is the only edit to change resolution.

bosun deploy footgun: the bosun-agent BAKES deploy.config.ts into its image, so removing a service from config takes TWO webhook deploys to fully reconcile. The first 'bosun up' after a config+agent change is executed by the OLD agent (old baked config) - it re-asserts the now-removed services and rolls bosun-agent to the new image. Only the SECOND 'bosun up' (run by the new agent, new baked config) renders without the removed service and 'docker stack deploy --prune' actually removes it. Seen in CC-u34: control-center_ofelia + old stack docker-image-prune lingered after the first deploy; a second webhook POST to https://hooks.worldwidewebb.co/deploy/control-center (Bearer op://Homelab/Bosun Webhook Token/credential) pruned them. To force: re-fire the webhook once the new agent image is live.

bosun self-deploy: ANY change to renderStackYml / secretDockerName / volume naming takes effect ONE DEPLOY LATE, not on the deploy that ships it. The webhook deploy runs cmdUp from the bosun-agent's OWN baked image code, not the freshly-pushed repo. So deploy N (run by the old agent) rolls the agent to the new image but still renders with the OLD render logic; only deploy N+1 (run by the new agent) emits the new output. Same root cause as the CC-czg image-digest lag. Consequence for migrations like CC-8pt (cc_->control-center_ rename): you must trigger a SECOND deploy after the agent rolls to apply a render change, and any on-box data pre-staging (pgdata cold copy) must happen right before that second deploy, because the first deploy resurrects services on the old names/volume.

bosun deploy footgun (CC-8pt): cmdUp runs reconcileSecrets (creates new + PRUNES old secrets) BEFORE deployStack. removeSecret = 'docker secret rm' with no --force, and docker refuses to remove an in-use secret. So renaming a secret's docker name (e.g. changing the cc_ prefix, or a value-hash rotation) makes the prune of the still-in-use old secret fail and aborts the whole deploy. Combined with the auto-deploy webhook (push to main -> bosun up), a mass secret rename WILL break prod. Fix before any namespace migration: prune AFTER deploy (or force/retry). Same class of hazard as the cc_pgdata volume rename (empty-volume). Both tracked in CC-8pt.

Captive portal domain: captive-portal.worldwidewebb.co (NOT captive.) - Calum's explicit naming. LAN-only via split-horizon DNS: NO Cloudflare tunnel route (bosun must not add one), UniFi gateway local DNS record -> Mini LAN IP, DNS-01 Let's Encrypt cert terminated in portal nginx. UniFi controller changes (guest WLAN, hotspot/external-portal settings, local DNS records) should be done PROGRAMMATICALLY by the agent via op://Homelab/UniFi/local_api_key (X-API-KEY header; authenticates the private controller API incl. set/setting - read-WRITE), controller https://192.168.0.1. Calum confirmed 2026-06-09.

Captive-portal epic is coming in a SEPARATE session and interacts with the www-cuuw access gate (see access-gate-design-www-cuuw). These are layered: CF Access gate (www-cuuw) is the OUTER layer (edge, Cloudflare-enforced), captive portal is a completely different feature (LAN-only splash/auth for guest WiFi). Do not conflate them.

CF Access service tokens (headless callers: iPad kiosk app--cc, CI hooks) MUST use policy decision 'non_identity' (Service Auth), NOT 'allow'. An 'allow' policy is identity-based: a valid service token is recognized (meta JWT service_token_status:true) but NOT authorized (auth_status:NONE), so CF 302-redirects to the IdP login and mints NO CF_Authorization cookie. Symptom: iPad kiosk bounced to email-OTP -> 'invalid login session', while Safari interactive login worked. Diagnose without iOS via 3 curls to app--cc: (1) no headers -> 302; (2) with CF-Access-Client-Id/Secret -> should be 200 + Set-Cookie CF_Authorization; (3) cookie-only -> 200. Decode the 302 Location meta JWT (base64) to read service_token_status/auth_status. Keep at least one 'allow' policy on the app if you need the JWT cookie reused. Codified in infra/cloudflare/src/access.ts serviceTokenPolicy(). Fixed in www-n7xj.

Climate desired-state invariant (CC-dnpj): device_state.desiredState for climate must ONLY carry commandable fields (mode/target/targetLow/targetHigh/fanMode) - never the reported-only ambient/action. Any NEW code path that writes climate desired must pass it through sanitizeClimateDesired() (device-state-mapping.ts). A desired carrying ambient freezes the panel room temp via the desired-over-reported merge overlay.

world-wide-webb (formerly control-center) git remote is origin git@github.com:0x63616c/world-wide-webb.git - repo renamed 2026-06-15. GHCR images stay control-center-* (product-specific per naming convention). Shared packages across products use @www/* scope; product packages use product name (@control-center/*, @text-your-ex/*, etc).

control-center has a reusable Missions-style ship workflow at .claude/workflows/ship.mjs (invoke via Workflow {name:'ship', args:{issue|goal, contract, milestones, resume, ui, maxFixRounds, push}}). Beads IS the shared mission state: scope writes a validation contract into the epic --design, each feature is a child issue with --acceptance + milestone-N label + deps for serial order; build->validate->fix runs per milestone; resumable via args.resume=<epicId>. Model tiers (Calum's rule: haiku is a good validator but bad coder): opus=scope, sonnet=build/fix/harden (ALL code), haiku=validate judges + bd/gate bookkeeping (never writes code). Conservative git: commits per feature, no push unless args.push. Generalized from wf-finish-dashboard.mjs which stays as the original one-shot.

control-center is now a PUBLIC GitHub repo (0x63616c/control-center), migrated 2026-06-05 (CC-3zi/CC-d3j). It was re-created fresh from rewritten clean history (the old private repo was renamed control-center-private and DELETED) to purge the home address from git history + merged-PR refs. Key consequences for future work: (1) GHCR auth in CI uses the GHCR_PAT secret (a user PAT with write:packages from 1Password 'GitHub Personal Access Token'), NOT GITHUB_TOKEN - because the control-center-* packages' Actions-access was linked to the old repo and GITHUB_TOKEN 403s; packages remain PRIVATE. (2) beads Dolt is LOCAL-ONLY now - the dolt remote 'origin' was removed and refs/dolt/data deleted from GitHub so the issue DB isn't published; do NOT re-add a public dolt remote. (3) Home location is never in source: HOME_LAT/HOME_LON/HOME_PLACE_NAME/HOME_RADIUS_MILES env, real values from 1Password item 'Home Location', public LA City Hall placeholder defaults in apps/api/src/env.ts + apps/web/src/config/home.ts. (4) scripts/check-no-home-address.sh (lefthook) BLOCKS commits with the location name via a base64-encoded pattern; full backup bundle at ~/cc-history-rewrite-backup-*. (5) Public repo = free unlimited Actions minutes; CI builds native arm64 on ubuntu-24.04-arm.

control-center workflow: do NOT use PRs/MRs. Commit on a branch (or directly), then fast-forward merge into main and push. CI runs on every branch but the bosun deploy webhook only fires on main pushes (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Direct-to-main push is the intended ship path.

The 'Fan' toggle in the Controls tile is NOT a fan.* device - it's the AC/climate entity's fan_mode attribute on climate.home (controls-service.ts findFanClimate/handleControl 'fan' case). Quirks: (1) ON sends set_fan_mode:'on' (force AC fan to run continuously); (2) OFF sends set_fan_mode:'auto' - there is NO 'off' fan_mode on this AC, so 'off' really means 'auto' (fan runs only when heating/cooling). (3) Entity is chosen by findFanClimate = first climate entity advertising a fan_modes list; today only climate.home qualifies (the Tesla climate.evee_climate has fan_modes:null so it's filtered out) - but it's the same unsorted .find() fragility as the old alphabetical climate bug, just currently masked. 'evee parity' in the comments = matching a prior evee-tiles/ha-service getFanState/turnFanOn behavior. See [[ha-evee-is-tesla-not-home-climate]].

Design docs / planning docs convention (control-center, Calum's preference): write design docs and plans INTO beads issues and epics, not standalone markdown files. Use epic --design for the overall design/validation contract, issue --acceptance for per-feature acceptance criteria, and bd comments/decisions for rationale. Calum wants Beads as the single source of truth for all task/project management like this.

bd dolt push 'Uploading...: signal: killed' / 'must be run in a work tree' root cause (fixed 2026-06-12): the GLOBAL git core.hooksPath (dotcalum dispatcher->lefthook) leaks into dolt's INTERNAL git push for refs/dolt/data, which runs in a BARE/worktree-less repo. lefthook's startup probe 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' errors exit 128 in a bare repo -> dolt reports 'addTableFiles, updateManifestAddFiles ... exit status 128'; hangs on dolt git+ssh round-trips then SIGKILL = 'signal: killed'. FIX in dotcalum/git: (1) _dispatch bare-repo guard exits 0 when 'git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree' != true; (2) reconcile-hooks.sh heal_global_dir restores clobbered _dispatch symlinks (a tool's install resolving to global core.hooksPath overwrites them, machine-wide; reconcile only guarded the POINTER not the CONTENTS); (3) lefthook shell shim pins local core.hooksPath=.git/hooks so 'lefthook install' never targets the global dir. Hermetic tests: dotcalum/git/hooks/test-bare-repo-guard.sh + dotcalum/git/test-heal-global-dir.sh. If push ever breaks again: 'bash dotcalum/git/reconcile-hooks.sh --heal' then verify 'ls -la dotcalum/git/hooks' are _dispatch symlinks.

GHCR auth for the control-center deploy (CC-5ag) does NOT need a new credential or package-visibility flip. The existing 1Password item op://Homelab/GitHub Personal Access Token (field: token) is scoped repo+write:packages; write:packages includes read:packages, so the homelab box pulls ghcr.io/0x63616c/control-center-* by 'docker login ghcr.io' with this token. Also: 'Service Account Auth Token: Homelab' in op is likely the 1Password service-account token for the on-box bosun-agent (ac_code_auto). deploy.config.ts/bootstrap should reference these existing items, not mint new ones.

In Home Assistant, any entity named 'evee' (e.g. climate.evee_climate) is Calum's TESLA (the car is named Evee), NOT the home/house climate. The Tesla HA integration uses quirky entity naming under the car's name. The real house thermostat is climate.home. Anything that should control the HOUSE (climate setpoint, HVAC) must target climate.home and must NOT alphabetically auto-pick the first climate entity, because 'climate.evee_climate' sorts before 'climate.home' and is the car. climate.evee_climate is heat_cool-only and usually off, so sending it a bare set_temperature 500s.

homelab (Mac Mini M2, 8GB RAM, macOS 15.6) topology: Home Assistant runs ON the Mini as a launchd-managed QEMU VM (qemu-system-aarch64, 4GB alloc, /Users/calum/homeassistant-os/haos.qcow2) - it is BOTH the smart-home controller AND this dashboard's data source, so HA_URL points at this local VM. Tailscale runs as a host-level system extension (not a container). App services run as Docker containers under OrbStack. 8GB RAM is the bottleneck (CPU is mostly idle): the 4GB HA VM + ~2GB OrbStack VM keep the box under memory-compressor pressure. As of 2026-06-02 the box was wiped clean of a prior Kamal-deployed 'evee-*' stack (app + prometheus/grafana/loki/cadvisor monitoring) to redeploy lean via docker-compose + OrbStack built-in monitoring.

ID format DEFAULT (always, unless Calum explicitly asks otherwise): Stripe-style prefixed IDs - prefix_<id> (short semantic prefix + underscore + id), e.g. cus_8af2e52a, tile_x1o, evt_abc123. Use this for EVERY identifier you design/generate (DB primary keys, API resource ids, event ids, anything), not only when explicitly asked. Rationale: traceability - the prefix tells you what an id refers to at a glance. Only deviate on explicit request for a different format.

iOS reproducibility policy: commit Package.resolved lockfiles for buildable native app source, including Xcode workspace SwiftPM lockfiles and nested Capacitor CapApp-SPM/Package.resolved files. Do not ignore them broadly. Ignore build artifacts like .build/ and DerivedData, but lockfile diffs are useful signal.

ZERO fake/placeholder data rule: tiles show shimmer Skeleton on unavailable data, never invent a value. FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER (uppercase identifiers) are banned everywhere. DEMO_/demo_ allowed only in network-service.ts and weather-service.ts (and their tests). Pre-commit hook scripts/check-fake-data.sh enforces this and exits non-zero to block the commit

In control-center, oc means opencode. ALWAYS load the customize-opencode skill before changing opencode configuration, agents, plugins, skills, provider settings, or anything under opencode.jsonc/.opencode/.

Pan-lab perf experiment (epic CC-5p4) verdict for the pannable-canvas board: on the iPad Calum tested all 5 arms by hand and NATIVE-SCROLL felt best (iOS momentum/rubber-band). The desktop sweep's data-winner was transform-WINDOWING (100fps/85%, only arm that ignores heavy-tile ratio + world size because it never mounts off-screen tiles); native-scroll was mid (57fps/55%) and canvas was last (30fps) but that's a HEADLESS software-WebGL artifact, not its real-GPU potential. Conclusion: the real board should marry native-scroll FEEL + windowing's DOM-thinning (+ zoom). Calum wants pinch zoom-in/out with a default zoom level: feasible via native overflow-scroll for pan + CSS transform:scale on the world inside a matching sizer div (so scroll area tracks zoom), pinch hand-rolled with touch-action:pan-x pan-y. Recommendation doc: docs/perf-lab-recommendation.md. pan-lab throwaway at ../pan-lab.

control-center has a beads-tracked Workflow at .claude/workflows/perf-lab.mjs (run via Workflow {name:'perf-lab', args:{dir,epic,arms,matrix,port}}). It builds + benchmarks a THROWAWAY pan-lab prototype (default dir ../pan-lab) to settle the rendering strategy for turning the board into a pannable 2D canvas. ONE Vite+React app, a route per rendering arm (transform, transform-cv, transform-window, native-scroll, canvas), HYBRID tiles (synthetic text/svg + real MapLibre + real video), a full-matrix autopan sweep -> results.json + the knee per arm, and a launcher+results UI Calum drives on the iPad (homelab:<port>). Mission state = bd epic CC-5p4 (children .1 scaffold, .2-.6 arms, .7 sweep, .8 launcher, .9 finalize; milestone labels + deps for order). Model tiers: sonnet builds all code, haiku finalizes; no opus scope (plan is baked). Full design in plan idempotent-snacking-clock.md.
Calum preference: prefer git operations for shipping work. Keep changes committed/pushed through normal git flow rather than leaving local-only state.
Calum preference: use subagents by default for milestone/work execution, especially parallel milestone work. Main thread can coordinate and handle narrow non-overlapping tasks.

Before any fix or change, step back and evaluate whether it's the right approach: right structure, right layer, right code, or whether a higher-level change beats the local band-aid. A quick fix that papers over a symptom (instead of root cause or a cleaner primitive one level up) is the wrong fix even when it works. Surface the better/structural option even if it's more work. Relates to the LAYOUT first-principles rule (no magic-number band-aids).

Screenshotting the 1366x1024 wall-panel board: at DPR 2 the agent-browser window/display must be >= 1370x1040 to yield a TRUE 1366x1024 content viewport. Browser chrome steals ~24px, so a too-small window silently gives 1366x1000 instead. Always assert the captured viewport is actually 1366x1024 before trusting a render screenshot. Relates to board-is-fixed-1366x1024.

Shared UI primitives live in apps/web/src/components/ui/ (TileHeader, Tile, Skeleton, Stat, Pill, Chip, StatusDot); always prefer them over inline styles, and never introduce FALLBACK/PLACEHOLDER constants - unavailable data renders a Skeleton.
shared UI primitives live at apps/web/src/components/ui/ - barrel export at index.ts. All tiles MUST use TileHeader, StatCell, Pill, Skeleton, TileWrapper from there; never re-inline them

Shared UI primitives (TileHeader, Stat, Pill, Skeleton) live under apps/web/src/components/ui/ - always prefer them over re-inlining; design tokens in apps/web/src/styles/tokens.css; shimmer keyframes in globals.css; zero fake/hardcoded data - tiles must render Skeleton on unavailable data

Storybook-first development (control-center): ALWAYS create a component and its view in Storybook FIRST, then build to satisfy the story. The .stories.tsx is the design surface and first consumer - write it before/alongside the component, not after. Benefits: free dark-mode + state + edge-case coverage, component lands already wired into Storybook, and addon-vitest runs the stories as component tests under 'bun run test'. Every new tile/component ships with its story in the same change. See [[shared-ui-primitives-location]] and [[no-fake-data-rule]].
test runner is bun run test (vitest). NEVER bare bun test - Bun's native runner is incompatible with vi.mock and reports false failures. Always: bun run test

Tile stories must NOT add their own fixed-size wrapper decorators: the global BoardDecorator (.storybook/preview.tsx) already frames any registered tile at its exact board footprint via tilePixelSize(cols,rows). A story-local hardcoded width/height div (e.g. QuickPlayTileView's old 280x160) nests inside it and renders the tile at the wrong size.

UCG-Fiber + @pulumiverse/unifi 0.2.0 (bridged filipowm) quirks for guest networks (CC-j934.3.2): (1) a 'guest' network purpose is COERCED to 'corporate' by the controller -> declare purpose:corporate, guest behavior goes on the WLAN (isGuest + l2Isolation). (2) WLAN needs apGroupIds -> look up default via unifi.getApGroupOutput({},{provider}) (else api.err.ApGroupMissing). (3) firewall rule_index must be 5-digit (~22000); 2000 -> FirewallRuleIndexOutOfRange (IPS rules sit at 20000/20001). (4) controller defaults network multicastDns:true -> declare it or the provider tries to remove it and the bridged networkconf Update 404s ('not found'). (5) computed fields aren't in stored inputs, so apply with 'pulumi up --refresh' or you get a phantom diff that wedges. The unifi stack is its OWN Pulumi project (control-center-unifi), NOT in CI yet (gitops gap CC-j934.17); applied via local pulumi up for now.

UniFi guest WiFi naming for captive portal: the UniFi network name is 'guest', the SSID is 'www-guest', and the VLAN ID is 20. Use network name 'guest' in UniFi/controller contexts; use SSID 'www-guest' only for WLAN/guest-facing references.

Task tracking convention (control-center): use beads (bd) to track work you need to do, NOT TodoWrite/markdown TODOs. Group related work under an epic; put machine-checkable acceptance criteria on each issue (bd create --acceptance). This makes goals expressible as 'all beads under epic X are closed' - a clean, queryable definition of done (bd list --epic / bd ready). Epic = the goal, child issues = the work, acceptance criteria = per-issue done test.

vitest 12GB/worker memory claim is WRONG (stale from CC-ddo9 era). Measured actual peak RSS: ~1.5GB per worker (jsdom + React + v8 coverage). With 2 workers, total is ~3GB, well within CI's 16GB runner. Removed --max-old-space-size=12288 from vitest.config.ts, root test script, coverage.sh, CI workflow, and apps/web + apps/captive-portal vitest configs. Relax maxWorkers from 1 to 2 for ~2x test speedup.

Any Workflow/multi-agent run whose agents COMMIT must pin its REPO/cwd to an ISOLATED git worktree, never the shared main checkout. This machine runs concurrent claude sessions that commit to main; if a workflow's agents also cd into the main checkout and commit, the histories interleave and a partial/broken state can get pushed to origin/main. Incident (deploy-epic CC-5ag, 2026-06-03): REPO was hardcoded to the main checkout -> partial bosun (missing reconcile/health/cli) landed on origin/main while a perf-lab session committed board work there. Recovery: complete work was intact on the worktree branch; PR'd it back to repair main. Fix: pin REPO to worktree + hard rules (never checkout/commit/push/rebase main). See .claude/workflows/deploy-epic.mjs.

Working preference (control-center): COMMIT OFTEN and proactively. After each discrete/logical piece of work, make a small focused conventional commit (feat/fix/chore/etc.) without being asked. Calum wants main kept clean most of the time - no large piles of uncommitted changes. CORRECTION (was wrong before): this repo DOES have a git remote (origin git@github.com:0x63616c/control-center.git), so ALWAYS git push after committing at session end - the commit is NOT the final persistence step. See [[control-center-git-remote]]. Don't batch many unrelated changes into one big commit; keep them small and frequent.

When testing agent/opencode/skill changes from a git worktree, launch the tool/session under tmux from that worktree so it actually loads the worktree files. Keep probes quick: use low timeouts around 5s because these tests should fail or succeed fast. Always clean up by killing the tmux session you created when done; do not leave test sessions running.

Running gates INSIDE a .claude/worktrees/ worktree: (1) 'bunx biome check .' checks 0 FILES and exits 1 - biome.json files.includes has '!**/.claude' and the worktree path contains a .claude segment. Use the lefthook/CI form instead: git ls-files -z -- '*.ts' '*.tsx' ... | xargs -0 bunx biome check --no-errors-on-unmatched, or pass explicit file paths. (2) bunx knip can false-flag 'unlisted binaries' (vite, storybook) / 'unresolved imports' until you run 'bun install --frozen-lockfile' in the worktree (its node_modules may be stale/partial; resolution otherwise walks up to the main checkout). Both are worktree-env artifacts, not code findings.

Worktrees under .claude/worktrees/ break 'bunx biome check .' - biome ignores hidden dirs, scans 0 files and exits 1 ('No files were processed'). From a worktree run 'bunx biome check apps packages scripts' instead. Same class of trap: the main checkout's node_modules can be stale for the lefthook pre-push knip gate - run bun install in the checkout that executes the hook.
